  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Size curves are often season-specific (seasonal adjustment factors, basis periods); season_code exists but no FK to season master. Adding season_id FK normalizes season reference and removes redundant']
  size_scale_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.size_scale. Business justification: Size curves define percentage distributions across size ranges and must reference the actual size scale (XS-XXL, numeric, etc.) to be operationally meaningful. Allocation planning requires mapping cur']
  sourcing_purchase_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order_line. Business justification: Size curves determine the size-run quantities on sourcing PO lines. Buyers apply the size curve to set ordered_quantity per size_code on each PO line. This link enables size curve accuracy reporting b']
  basis_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the historical sell-through data period used to derive this size curve\'s distribution percentages. Together with basis_period_start_date, defines the historical window for statistical calibration of size ratios.']
  basis_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the historical sell-through data period used to derive this size curve\'s distribution percentages. Defines the lookback window for statistical calibration. Typically covers 1-3 prior seasons of comparable selling history.']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'Statistical confidence level of the size curve based on the volume and quality of historical sell-through data used in derivation. High confidence curves are used directly in buy plans; low confidence curves require planner override review. Drives curve applicability flags in Anaplan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this size curve record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) per platform conventions.']
  curve_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the size curve profile, used in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Anaplan for cross-system reference. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  curve_derivation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to derive the size distribution percentages for this curve. Historical STR uses actual sell-through data; planner_override reflects manual planner judgment; vendor_recommended uses supplier-provided size ratios; statistical_model uses algorithmic forecasting; blended combines multiple methods. Affects confidence level assignment.']
  curve_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the size curve profile (e.g., \'Women\'s Fitted Tops Standard\', \'Men\'s Denim 28-42 Bell Curve\'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Used in Oracle Retail and Anaplan planning interfaces.']
  curve_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the size curve profile indicating the fit and silhouette category that drives the distribution shape. Standard curves follow bell-curve distributions; fitted curves skew toward smaller sizes; oversized curves skew toward larger sizes. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|fitted|oversized|petite|plus|tall|slim|relaxed — promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this size curve profile is no longer active for new planning runs. Null indicates an open-ended curve with no planned expiry. Used to manage curve versioning and seasonal transitions in Oracle Retail and Anaplan.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this size curve profile becomes effective for use in buy planning, allocation, and replenishment. Curves are versioned by effective date to support seasonal transitions and curve updates without disrupting in-flight planning cycles.']
  is_default_for_class boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this size curve is the default curve applied to new styles within the associated merchandise class when no style-specific curve exists. True = default fallback curve for the class. Used in Oracle Retail automated allocation and buy plan generation.']
  is_replenishment_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this size curve is approved for use in automated replenishment size pack calculations in Manhattan Associates WMS. True = curve can drive replenishment size breaks. False = curve is restricted to initial buy plan and allocation use only.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this size curve was last reviewed and validated by a merchandise planner. Curves not reviewed within a defined period (e.g., 2 seasons) are flagged for re-validation to ensure continued accuracy. Supports governance of curve quality.']
  markdown_risk_index decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk score (0.00-100.00) indicating the likelihood of size-related markdown exposure if this curve is applied to a buy plan. Derived from historical size fragmentation patterns and STR variance across sizes. Higher scores indicate greater markdown risk from size imbalance. Critical for GMROI optimization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for merchandise planner annotations regarding this size curve, including rationale for manual overrides, known anomalies in the historical basis data, or special instructions for application. Supports planning governance and knowledge transfer.']
  numeric_size_distribution varchar [note: 'Serialized representation of the size distribution percentages for numeric size runs (waist sizes 28-42 or equivalent), stored as a delimited string of size:percentage pairs (e.g., \'28:5.0|30:12.0|32:22.0|34:25.0|36:18.0|38:12.0|40:4.0|42:2.0\'). Null for alpha or shoe size scale types. Used when the number of numeric sizes exceeds the available discrete percentage columns.']
  pct_l decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size L in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 25.00 = 25%). Null for numeric or shoe size scale types. Used in buy plan size ratio calculations and replenishment size pack optimization.']
  pct_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size M in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 28.00 = 28%). Typically the modal size in standard bell-curve distributions. Null for numeric or shoe size scale types.']
  pct_s decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size S in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 18.00 = 18%). Null for numeric or shoe size scale types. Used in buy plan size ratio calculations and allocation size breaks.']
  pct_xl decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size XL in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 14.00 = 14%). Null for numeric or shoe size scale types. Used in buy plan size ratio calculations and initial distribution planning.']
  pct_xs decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size XS in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 8.50 = 8.5%). Null for numeric or shoe size scale types. All size distribution percentages must sum to 100.00 for a valid curve. Serves as the principal MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE for this MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  pct_xxl decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size XXL in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 6.50 = 6.5%). Null for numeric or shoe size scale types. Monitoring XXL distribution is critical for plus-size assortment planning and markdown minimization.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region for which this size curve applies (e.g., \'NA\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\', \'LATAM\'). Size distributions vary significantly by region due to demographic differences. Used to apply region-specific size ratios in allocation and buy planning.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the merchandise planner who last reviewed and validated this size curve. Provides accountability for curve quality and supports audit trail requirements for merchandise planning governance.']
  sample_unit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of units sold in the historical basis period used to derive this size curve. Higher sample counts yield higher statistical confidence. Used to validate confidence_level assignment — curves based on fewer than a threshold number of units are flagged as low confidence.']
  seasonal_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Multiplicative adjustment factor applied to the base size distribution percentages to account for seasonal demand shifts (e.g., 1.0500 = 5% upward adjustment). Captures known seasonal size preference changes such as outerwear seasons skewing larger. Applied in Anaplan seasonal buy plan calculations.']
  shoe_size_distribution varchar [note: 'Serialized representation of the size distribution percentages for shoe size runs (US sizes 5-14 or equivalent), stored as a delimited string of size:percentage pairs (e.g., \'7:8.0|7.5:10.0|8:15.0|8.5:18.0|9:17.0|9.5:14.0|10:12.0|10.5:6.0\'). Null for alpha or numeric apparel size scale types.']
  size_count varchar [note: 'Total number of discrete sizes in the size run covered by this curve (e.g., 6 for XS-XXL alpha, 8 for 28-42 even numeric). Drives the number of size break columns populated in allocation and replenishment size pack calculations.']
  size_curve_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the size curve profile. Active curves are used in live buy plans and allocation runs. Draft curves are under development. Archived curves are retained for historical analysis but excluded from new planning cycles. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  size_range_max varchar [note: 'The largest size in the size run covered by this curve (e.g., \'XXL\', \'42\', \'14\'). Combined with size_range_min to define the full size run span. Used to validate that buy plan size breaks fall within the defined range.']
  size_range_min varchar [note: 'The smallest size in the size run covered by this curve (e.g., \'XS\', \'28\', \'5\'). Combined with size_range_max to define the full size run span. Used to validate that buy plan size breaks fall within the defined range.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this size curve record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. Oracle Retail is the primary system of record; Anaplan is used for planning-layer curves; Centric PLM may provide initial vendor-recommended curves.']
  str_basis_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum Sell-Through Rate (STR) achieved in the historical basis period for the data to be considered statistically valid for curve derivation. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 75.00 means 75% STR). Curves derived from low-STR periods may be flagged as low-confidence.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this size curve record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update to any attribute of the size curve profile. Used for incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer and for audit trail purposes.']

  Note: 'Size curve profile master defining the expected unit sales distribution across size runs (alpha: XS/S/M/L/XL/XXL, numeric: 28-42, shoe: 5-14) for a style, merchandise class, or product category by channel, region, and store cluster. Captures curve type (standard, fitted, oversized), historical sell-through basis period, confidence level, and seasonal adjustment factors. Used to optimize buy plan size ratios, allocation size breaks, replenishment size packs, and initial distribution. Critical for minimizing size-related markdowns and stockouts in apparel where size fragmentation drives 15-20% of excess inventory. Managed in Oracle Retail and Anaplan.'
}

Table merchandising.season {
  season_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the merchandise season record. Primary key for the season master reference table in the merchandising domain.']
  channel_scope varchar [note: 'The distribution channel scope for which this season is planned. \'All\' covers all channels; \'DTC\' covers direct-to-consumer retail and e-commerce; \'Wholesale\' covers wholesale and distribution partners. Drives channel-specific OTB allocation and assortment planning in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  clearance_end_date date [note: 'The date by which all clearance selling for this season must be completed. After this date, remaining inventory is subject to return-to-vendor (RTV), off-price channel transfer, or write-off. Defines the final boundary of the season\'s sell-through window.']
  collection_theme varchar [note: 'The creative or commercial theme name assigned to this season\'s collection (e.g., \'Urban Explorer\', \'Coastal Luxe\'). Defined during collection planning in Centric PLM and used to align product design, marketing campaigns, and visual merchandising narratives.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this season record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary planning amounts for this season are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency operations in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO and Anaplan.']
  end_date date [note: 'The official last trading date of the merchandise season — the date on which the season\'s assortment is retired from active selling. Defines the close of the OTB budget window and triggers end-of-season markdown cadence and clearance planning.']
  first_delivery_date date [note: 'The earliest scheduled date for merchandise receipts into the distribution center or stores for this season. Defines the opening of the delivery window and is a key milestone in the Time and Action (T&A) calendar for sourcing and procurement coordination.']
  fiscal_half varchar [note: 'The fiscal half-year period (H1 or H2) to which this season is aligned. Spring/Summer and Resort seasons typically fall in H1; Fall/Winter, Pre-Fall, and Holiday seasons in H2. Used for semi-annual merchandise financial planning (MFP) and OTB budget allocation in Anaplan.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this season belongs for financial planning and reporting purposes (e.g., 2025). Aligns season-level OTB budgets and merchandise financial plans (MFP) to the corporate fiscal calendar in Anaplan and SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.']
  gender_target varchar [note: 'The primary gender target classification for this season\'s collection, used to segment assortment planning, allocation strategies, and marketing campaigns. Aligns with product hierarchy classifications in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Centric PLM.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this season is currently active and available for use in planning, buying, and allocation transactions. Set to True when the season status is \'active\'; False for planning, closed, or archived seasons. Used to filter active seasons in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Anaplan.']
  is_nos boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this season includes a Never Out of Stock (NOS) or carry-over program, where core styles are replenished continuously regardless of seasonal boundaries. NOS seasons require separate OTB budget allocation and replenishment planning in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  last_delivery_date date [note: 'The latest permissible date for merchandise receipts for this season. Defines the close of the delivery window. Receipts after this date may be subject to late delivery penalties or cancellation per vendor agreements. Key milestone in the T&A calendar.']
  markdown_cadence varchar [note: 'The planned frequency of markdown (MD) events during the clearance phase of this season. Drives the markdown calendar in Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management and informs maintained markup (MMU) forecasting in Anaplan.']
  markdown_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the first planned markdown (MD) event for this season begins. Initiates the end-of-season clearance cadence. Drives markdown (MD) planning in Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management and Anaplan merchandise financial planning.']
  market_week_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the industry market week associated with this season. Together with market_week_start_date, defines the window during which wholesale buyers finalize orders and assortment commitments.']
  market_week_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the industry market week (trade show / buying week) associated with this season, during which buyers review collections and place wholesale orders. Key date for collection planning and wholesale distribution calendars.']
  mfp_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the approved merchandise financial plan (MFP) associated with this season in Anaplan (e.g., \'v1.0-Initial\', \'v2.1-Revised\'). Tracks plan revisions through the planning cycle and ensures alignment between OTB budgets and financial targets.']
  num_planned_skus varchar [note: 'The total number of distinct SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) planned for this season across all styles, colors, and sizes. Used to manage assortment breadth, warehouse capacity planning in Manhattan Associates WMS, and OTB unit buy planning.']
  num_planned_styles varchar [note: 'The total number of distinct styles (SKU parent-level products) planned for this season\'s assortment. Set during collection planning in Centric PLM and used to manage line architecture, OTB depth-versus-breadth trade-offs, and assortment productivity analysis.']
  order_deadline_date date [note: 'The final date by which all purchase orders (POs) for this season must be submitted to suppliers to ensure on-time delivery within the season\'s delivery window. Critical T&A calendar milestone managed in SAP S/4HANA MM and Centric PLM.']
  otb_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total approved Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget in the company\'s reporting currency for this season. Represents the maximum planned merchandise spend authorized for buying activities. Set during merchandise financial planning (MFP) in Anaplan and tracked against actual PO commitments in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  planned_gross_margin_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The planned gross margin percentage for this season as set in the merchandise financial plan (MFP). Calculated as (planned sales minus planned cost of goods sold) divided by planned sales. Drives GMROI targets and IMU/MMU planning in Anaplan.']
  planned_imu_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The planned initial markup (IMU) percentage for this season, representing the difference between the cost of goods and the initial retail price (MSRP) as a percentage of retail. Set during merchandise financial planning and used to guide pricing strategy in Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management.']
  planned_sales_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total planned net sales revenue in the company\'s reporting currency for this season as set during merchandise financial planning (MFP). Serves as the top-line revenue target against which actual sell-through rate (STR) and average unit retail (AUR) performance are measured.']
  planned_str_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The planned sell-through rate (STR) percentage target for this season, representing the proportion of received inventory expected to be sold at full price before markdown. Used to benchmark actual in-season performance and trigger markdown cadence decisions.']
  planning_start_date date [note: 'The date on which internal merchandise planning activities formally begin for this season, including OTB budget setting, assortment planning, and buy plan development in Anaplan and Oracle Retail Merchandising System. Precedes the trading start date by several months.']
  pre_production_start_date date [note: 'The date on which pre-production (PP) activities commence for this season, including fabric and trim approvals, fit samples, and pre-production sample sign-off. Key milestone in the T&A calendar tracked in Centric PLM and Infor PLM.']
  region_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic region or market scope for which this season is defined (e.g., \'Global\', \'North America\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\'). Supports multi-regional merchandise planning where season start/end dates and delivery windows may vary by market. Used in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Anaplan regional planning.']
  season_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the season, capturing key strategic intent, collection positioning, target consumer profile, or planning notes. Used by merchandising and planning teams for context in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Anaplan.']
  season_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable full name of the merchandise season (e.g., \'Spring/Summer 2025\', \'Fall/Winter 2024\', \'Holiday 2024\', \'Resort 2025\'). Used in reporting, planning dashboards, and buyer-facing documents.']
  season_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the merchandise season. \'planning\' indicates the season is in pre-production planning; \'active\' indicates the season is currently trading; \'closed\' indicates the season has ended trading; \'archived\' indicates the season is retained for historical reference only. Controls visibility in planning tools and reporting.']
  season_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trading season by industry-standard merchandise calendar type. Drives collection planning, OTB budget cycles, and markdown cadence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Spring/Summer|Fall/Winter|Resort|Pre-Fall|Holiday|Cruise — promote to reference product if additional season types are introduced]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this season record was sourced (e.g., ORM = Oracle Retail Merchandising System, ANAPLAN = Anaplan, CENTRIC = Centric PLM). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  source_system_season_code varchar [note: 'The native season identifier as recorded in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Oracle Retail Merchandising System season number, Anaplan season key). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and source systems.']
  start_date date [note: 'The official first trading date of the merchandise season — the date on which the season\'s assortment becomes available for sale in retail stores and e-commerce channels. Serves as the temporal anchor for all season-level planning entities including assortment, OTB, allocation, and MFP.']
  tna_calendar_code varchar [note: 'Reference code linking this season to its associated Time and Action (T&A) calendar template in Centric PLM or Infor PLM. The T&A calendar defines all critical path milestones from design brief through to final delivery for the season.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this season record was last modified in the data platform. Used to detect changes, support incremental data loads, and maintain audit trail compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  weeks_of_supply_target decimal(5,1) [note: 'The target number of weeks of supply (WOS) to be maintained during the peak trading period of this season. Used to calibrate inventory depth in buy plans and allocation strategies, ensuring sufficient stock without excess inventory build-up.']

  Note: 'Merchandise season master reference defining trading seasons (Spring/Summer, Fall/Winter, Resort, Pre-Fall, Holiday, Cruise) with start and end dates, delivery windows, market week dates, T&A calendar milestones, financial planning periods, and markdown cadence calendar. Every planning entity in the merchandising domain (assortment, collection, OTB, buy plan, allocation, MFP) references a season as its temporal anchor. Serves as the canonical time-boundary definition for merchandise planning cycles. Managed in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.'
}

Table merchandising.price_change_event {
  price_change_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each price change event record in the merchandising system. Primary key for the price_change_event data product.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.channel. Business justification: Price change events are channel-specific in apparel retail — a markdown applied in DTC stores may differ from wholesale pricing adjustments. price_change_event has channel as a STRING attribute. Addin']
  cluster_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_cluster. Business justification: Price change events in apparel are frequently executed at store cluster level (e.g., markdown all outlet cluster stores simultaneously). Cluster-level markdown execution is a standard apparel pricing ']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.collection. Business justification: End-of-season collection clearance markdowns are a standard apparel practice — price change events are executed at collection level. This FK enables collection-level markdown tracking, collection clea']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Markdown decisions are tracked against cost center budgets for accountability. Finance requires this link to allocate markdown expenses to responsible cost centers and monitor markdown budget consumpt']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Targeted markdown events: price change events in fashion are often segment-targeted (e.g., loyalty Gold members receive early markdown access 48 hours before general public). This FK enables the prici']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.event. Business justification: Markdowns and clearance price changes are coordinated with marketing events (end-of-season sales, brand events). Linking price_change_event to the triggering marketing event enables markdown cost attr']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Price changes post to specific GL accounts (markdown expense, inventory reserve, promotional allowance). Finance requires this link to automate GL posting of markdown events and ensure proper expense ']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Price change events (markdowns, clearance) generate GL postings that must be recorded in a specific accounting ledger. Finance requires ledger assignment on price change events to post markdown entrie']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Price changes (especially markdowns) are often timed to marketing campaigns. This link tracks which campaign drove the pricing decision, enabling analysis of campaign-driven markdown effectiveness and']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Price change events are applied at merchandise hierarchy levels (department, class, subclass) in retail pricing systems. price_change_event has class_code, department_code, and subclass_code as STRING']
  price_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.price_master. Business justification: Price change events modify prices; price_master is the authoritative source for pricing data. Adding price_master_id FK links transactional price changes to master pricing records and removes redundan']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Markdowns impact profit center margin performance. Financial reporting requires linking markdown events to profit centers to measure margin erosion by channel/brand and assess merchandising effectiven']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional campaign or event that triggered this price change, if applicable. Links promotional price changes to the originating marketing or merchandising promotion.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store, channel, or distribution location where this price change event is effective. Supports location-specific pricing strategies across DTC, wholesale, and e-commerce channels.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Price change events (markdowns, promotions, clearance) are tied to merchandise seasons in apparel retail. price_change_event has season_code as a STRING attribute — a denormalized reference to the sea']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) to which this price change event applies. A SKU represents the most granular sellable unit (style-color-size combination).']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent style (product) to which this price change event applies. A price change may be applied at the style level and cascade to all associated SKUs.']
  vendor_cost_quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.vendor_cost_quote. Business justification: Markdown decisions consider original vendor costs from quotes to assess margin impact and determine vendor allowance eligibility. Critical for markdown planning, vendor negotiation for markdown suppor']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the price change event was formally approved by the authorized merchandising manager or buyer. Required for governance compliance and audit trail in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code from Oracle Retail Merchandising System explaining the business rationale for the price change (e.g., SEASONAL_MD, COMPETITIVE_RESPONSE, CLEARANCE, PROMO, CORRECTION). Supports markdown (MD) analysis and GMROI reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product]']
  change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description providing additional context for the price change event beyond the standardized reason code. Entered by the merchandising planner or buyer.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price change event by its business purpose. Permanent Markdown (MD) reduces the retail price indefinitely; Temporary Markdown is time-bound; Markup (MU) increases price; Clearance applies end-of-season liquidation pricing; Promotional applies campaign-driven pricing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: permanent_markdown|temporary_markdown|markup|clearance|promotional — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price change event record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the price change amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency retail operations across international markets.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the new price becomes active and visible to customers across applicable channels. Used for scheduling future price changes in advance.']
  end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the price change expires and the price reverts to the prior or next scheduled price. Null for permanent price changes. Critical for temporary markdowns and promotional pricing.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the price change event within the Oracle Retail Merchandising System workflow. Drives whether the price change is visible and applied at POS and e-commerce channels.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise real-world date and time when the price change event was executed or became effective in the system of record. This is the principal business event time, distinct from audit timestamps.']
  imu_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Initial Markup (IMU) percentage for the affected SKU or style at the time of this price change event. Represents the original margin built into the retail price relative to cost. Used to assess markdown (MD) impact on maintained markup (MMU).']
  is_clearance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price change event designates the SKU or style as a clearance item. Once flagged as clearance, the item is typically excluded from future regular-price replenishment and tracked separately for end-of-season liquidation reporting.']
  is_competitive_response boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price change event was triggered by a competitive pricing response to a market competitor\'s price action. Supports competitive pricing analytics and brand positioning strategy.']
  is_pos_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price change event has been successfully transmitted and applied at the Point of Sale (POS) system via SAP Customer Activity Repository. Supports reconciliation between merchandising system and store operations.']
  markdown_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the ordinal position of this markdown (MD) event within the seasonal markdown cadence for the affected SKU or style (e.g., 1st markdown, 2nd markdown, 3rd markdown). Supports sell-through rate (STR) analysis and markdown optimization.']
  mmu_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Maintained Markup (MMU) percentage for the affected SKU or style after applying this price change event. Reflects the actual margin achieved post-markdown or post-markup. Key metric for GMROI optimization.']
  new_retail_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The updated retail selling price applied to the SKU or style as a result of this price change event. Represents the Average Unit Retail (AUR) post-change and is used in sell-through rate (STR) and GMROI analysis.']
  original_retail_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The retail selling price of the SKU or style immediately prior to this price change event. Serves as the baseline for calculating markdown (MD) depth, sell-through rate (STR) impact, and maintained markup (MMU).']
  price_change_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The absolute monetary value of the price change (new_retail_price minus original_retail_price). A negative value indicates a markdown (MD); a positive value indicates a markup (MU). Used in merchandise financial planning and OTB impact analysis.']
  price_change_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the merchandise hierarchy level at which this price change event was initiated. A style-level change cascades to all associated SKUs; a SKU-level change applies only to the specific variant.']
  price_change_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the price change event as assigned by Oracle Retail Merchandising System or SAP S/4HANA SD module. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit traceability.']
  price_change_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'The percentage change in retail price relative to the original retail price. Calculated as (price_change_amount / original_retail_price) * 100. Used for markdown (MD) depth reporting and sell-through rate (STR) analysis in Anaplan.']
  pricing_zone_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the geographic or channel-based pricing zone to which this price change event applies. Pricing zones group locations with identical pricing strategies in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this price change event. Supports data lineage and cross-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  str_at_change decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Sell-Through Rate (STR) for the affected SKU or style at the time of this price change event, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.65 = 65%). Provides context for the markdown decision and is used in post-event STR lift analysis.']
  units_on_hand varchar [note: 'The inventory quantity on hand for the affected SKU at the time of this price change event. Used to calculate the total markdown (MD) dollar exposure and weeks of supply (WOS) impact in merchandise financial planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price change event record was last modified. Tracks amendments, corrections, or status updates to the price change event.']
  wos_at_change decimal(8,2) [note: 'The Weeks of Supply (WOS) for the affected SKU at the time of this price change event, calculated as units on hand divided by average weekly sales rate. Used to assess inventory urgency and justify markdown (MD) depth decisions.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of any retail price modification event including permanent markdowns, temporary markdowns, markups, clearance pricing, and promotional price changes applied to a style or SKU'
}

  price_book_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for price_book']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.brand. Business justification: Price books in multi-brand apparel retailers are brand-specific — luxury brands maintain separate price books from value brands. This FK enables brand-level pricing strategy management, brand price bo']

Business justification: Price books in apparel retail are channel-specific — a DTC price book differs from a wholesale price book. price_book has channel as a STRING attribute. Adding channel_id FK normalizes this relationsh']
  cluster_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_cluster. Business justification: Price books in apparel are assigned to store clusters — outlet clusters receive outlet price books, flagship clusters receive full-price books. Cluster-level price book assignment is a standard appare']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Price book segmentation: price_book.customer_segment is a plain text denormalization. Normalizing to a proper FK follows the established model pattern (assortment_plan, collection_plan, price_master, ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Price books define pricing tiers and markup percentages that drive revenue GL postings. Finance uses price book GL account assignment for revenue recognition by price tier and channel, enabling automa']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.loyalty_program. Business justification: Loyalty tier pricing: price books are directly tied to loyalty programs in fashion retail (e.g., Gold member price book, Platinum exclusive price book). This FK enables the pricing engine to automatic']
  parent_version_price_book_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous version of this price book, enabling version history tracking and audit trails.']
  primary_base_price_book_id varchar [note: 'Reference to parent or base price book when this price book is derived or inherits pricing rules from another.']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.region. Business justification: Price books are region-specific in apparel retail — pricing strategies differ by geographic region (e.g., North America vs. EMEA). price_book has region_code as a STRING attribute. Adding region_id FK']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Price books are seasonal pricing references. The price_book currently has season_code STRING attribute, which should be normalized to a FK to the season master table. This establishes the temporal con']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the merchandising manager or executive who approved this price book for use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price book was approved for operational use.']

  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the price book becomes active and pricing rules become enforceable for merchandising operations.']
  initial_markup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Initial markup percentage representing the difference between cost and original retail price for items in this price book.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the default price book for the organization or channel when no other price book applies.']

  markdown_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default markdown percentage applied across items in this price book for promotional or clearance pricing.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who last modified this price book record.']

  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, instructions, or business rationale for this price book.']
  price_book_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the price book used in merchandising systems and reporting.']
  price_book_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the price book purpose, scope, and application within merchandising operations.']
  price_book_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the price book for identification and reporting purposes.']
  price_book_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price book indicating its operational state and availability for use.']
  price_book_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price book indicating its primary merchandising purpose and pricing strategy application.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Merchandising pricing strategy applied in this price book (MSRP: Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price).']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking used to resolve conflicts when multiple price books apply to the same item. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether pricing changes in this price book require management approval before activation.']
  rounding_rule varchar [note: 'Rule applied for rounding calculated prices in this price book to achieve psychological pricing or operational simplicity.']

  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number tracking revisions and updates to this price book over time.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who created this price book record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for price_book. Referenced by price_book_id.'
}

Normalizing to a proper FK follows the model pattern and enables merchandising planners to filter assortme']

Finance tracks revenue, gross margin, and GMROI by division via profit center assignment — a standard apparel finance report']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this division record was first created in the system.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the division, used for reporting and system integration.']
  division_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the division\'s scope, product categories, and business purpose.']
  division_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the division (e.g., Men\'s Apparel, Women\'s Footwear, Accessories).']
  division_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the division.']
  division_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the division by product category type.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this division was discontinued or became inactive. Null for active divisions.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this division became active in the merchandise hierarchy.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this division record was last updated.']
  lead_time_weeks varchar [note: 'Average lead time in weeks from order placement to merchandise receipt for this division.']

  merchandise_hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of this division within the merchandise hierarchy (e.g., 1=Division, 2=Department, 3=Class).']
  otb_budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current open-to-buy budget amount allocated to this division for merchandise purchasing.']
  otb_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the OTB budget amount.']
  planner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary merchandise planner responsible for assortment and allocation planning for this division.']
  price_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier classification for products within this division.']
  private_label_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of private label merchandise within this division\'s assortment.']
  product_lifecycle_weeks varchar [note: 'Average product lifecycle duration in weeks for merchandise in this division.']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'Method used for inventory replenishment for products in this division.']
  seasonal_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this division carries seasonal merchandise (True) or year-round merchandise (False).']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value used to control the display order of divisions in reports and user interfaces.']
  sustainability_rating varchar [note: 'Sustainability rating for the division based on environmental and ethical sourcing practices.']
  target_gmroi decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target GMROI for this division, calculated as gross margin dollars / average inventory cost.']
  target_gross_margin_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target gross margin percentage for this division, used in merchandise financial planning.']
  target_initial_markup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target initial markup percentage for products in this division, calculated as (retail - cost) / retail * 100.']
  target_maintained_markup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target maintained markup percentage after markdowns for this division.']
  target_sell_through_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target sell-through rate percentage for this division, calculated as units sold / units received * 100.']
  vendor_concentration_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of division purchases concentrated with top vendors, used for risk assessment.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for division. Referenced by division_id.'
}

Table merchandising.channel {
  channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for channel']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Channel-segment targeting: channel.target_customer_segment is a plain text denormalization. A proper FK enables merchandising systems to automatically apply segment-appropriate assortments, promotions']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Sales channels (DTC, wholesale, e-commerce) map to specific GL accounts for channel-level revenue recognition. Finance uses channel-GL mapping for channel P&L reporting and automated revenue account d']
  parent_channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent channel if this channel is a sub-channel or variant within a hierarchical channel structure. Null for top-level channels.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Sales channels map to profit centers for channel-level P&L reporting. Finance tracks gross margin, AUR, and sell-through by channel via profit center assignment — essential for omnichannel profitabili']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the geographic region where this channel primarily operates.']
  allocation_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking (1=highest) used in merchandise allocation strategies when distributing inventory across channels.']
  assortment_strategy varchar [note: 'Merchandising assortment strategy defining the breadth and depth of product selection offered through this channel.']
  average_transaction_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Historical average transaction value in the channel\'s currency, used for financial planning and Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) analysis.']
  channel_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the channel by operational medium: physical (brick-and-mortar), digital (online), or omnichannel (integrated).']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the channel, used for reporting and system integration (e.g., WEB, RETAIL, WHSL).']
  channel_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the channel, including its purpose, target customer segment, and operational characteristics.']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the sales and distribution channel (e.g., E-Commerce, Retail Stores, Wholesale Partners).']
  channel_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the channel in the merchandising system lifecycle.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the channel based on ownership and operational model (direct-owned, indirect-partner, hybrid, franchise, marketplace, outlet).']
  commission_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Commission rate (as a decimal, e.g., 0.0500 for 5%) paid to channel partners or sales associates for transactions in this channel.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for business inquiries and operational communication related to this channel.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for business inquiries and operational communication related to this channel.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country of operation for this channel.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this channel record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial transactions in this channel.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this channel ceased operations or was retired. Null for currently active channels.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this channel became operational and available for transactions.']
  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to fulfill customer orders in this channel: ship from store, ship from distribution center, in-store pickup, drop-ship, or mixed.']
  inventory_visibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time inventory levels are visible to customers in this channel (True) or not (False).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel record is currently active (True) or logically deleted/archived (False) in the system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this channel record was last updated in the system.']
  loyalty_program_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether purchases made through this channel are eligible for loyalty program points and rewards (True) or not (False).']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the business leader or manager responsible for this channel\'s performance and strategy.']
  markdown_policy varchar [note: 'Markdown policy governing price reductions in this channel to optimize sell-through rate (STR) and inventory turnover.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or special instructions related to this channel\'s operations or configuration.']
  omnichannel_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel is integrated into the omnichannel customer experience (True) or operates independently (False).']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for this channel (e.g., 9:00 AM - 9:00 PM Mon-Sat, 24/7 for digital channels).']
  payment_methods_accepted varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of payment methods accepted in this channel (e.g., credit card, debit card, digital wallet, cash, gift card).']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary pricing approach used in this channel: Manufacturer\'s Suggested Retail Price (MSRP), promotional, clearance, dynamic, tiered, or wholesale pricing.']
  return_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the return and exchange policy applicable to purchases made through this channel.']
  seasonal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel operates seasonally (True) or year-round (False).']
  target_initial_markup_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Initial Markup percentage for merchandise sold through this channel, used in Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget planning.']
  target_maintained_markup_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Maintained Markup percentage after markdowns, used to measure pricing effectiveness and margin preservation.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary tax jurisdiction applicable to transactions in this channel for sales tax calculation.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Primary website URL for digital channels or channel-specific landing pages.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for channel. Referenced by channel_id.'
}

Table merchandising.region {
  region_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for region']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Finance uses region-to-company-code mapping for legal entity co']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Merchandising regions map to cost centers for regional overhead allocation and budget tracking. Finance uses region-to-cost-center mapping for regional P&L reporting and budget variance analysis acros']
  distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary distribution center serving this region for inventory allocation and fulfillment.']
  parent_region_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent region in a hierarchical region structure, enabling multi-level geographic or organizational rollups.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Merchandising regions map to profit centers for regional P&L reporting. Finance tracks revenue, gross margin, and OTB performance by region via profit center assignment — standard in apparel companies']
  allocation_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for inventory allocation decisions when supply is constrained (lower number = higher priority).']
  aur_target_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target average unit retail price for merchandise sold in this region, used for assortment planning and pricing strategy.']
  climate_zone varchar [note: 'Climate classification of the region used for seasonal assortment planning and collection timing.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the primary country associated with this region.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this region record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code representing the primary currency used for financial planning and reporting in this region.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this region was discontinued or became inactive. Null indicates the region is currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this region became active and available for business operations and planning.']
  gmroi_target decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target gross margin return on investment ratio for this region, measuring profitability of inventory investment.']
  imu_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target initial markup percentage for merchandise in this region, representing the difference between cost and initial retail price.']
  is_ecommerce_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether e-commerce sales channels are active and operational in this region.']
  is_wholesale_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether wholesale distribution partnerships are active in this region.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO language code representing the primary language used in this region for business communications.']
  markdown_policy varchar [note: 'Markdown policy governing price reduction strategies and timing for this region to optimize sell-through rate (STR) and gross margin return on investment (GMROI).']
  market_size_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the region based on market size and maturity for strategic planning and resource allocation.']
  otb_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Open-to-buy budget allocated to this region for merchandise purchasing, representing planned inventory investment.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary pricing strategy applied in this region based on market positioning and competitive landscape.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the region, used for external identification and system integration.']
  region_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the region including its scope, coverage, and business purpose.']
  region_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the region hierarchy (e.g., 1=Global, 2=Continental, 3=Country, 4=State/Province, 5=District).']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the region (e.g., North America, EMEA, Asia Pacific).']
  region_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the region indicating whether it is actively used in business operations.']
  region_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the region based on its business purpose (geographic, sales, merchandising, distribution, operational, administrative).']
  regulatory_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting region-specific regulatory requirements, trade restrictions, or compliance considerations.']
  sales_target_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual sales target amount set for this region, used for performance measurement and planning.']
  season_calendar_type varchar [note: 'Seasonal calendar pattern used for merchandising planning in this region (e.g., northern hemisphere follows spring/summer/fall/winter).']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary tax jurisdiction applicable to this region for sales tax and VAT calculations.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Standard time zone identifier for the region (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London) used for scheduling and reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this region record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for region. Referenced by region_id.'
}

Table store.retail_store {
  retail_store_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each physical retail store location operated by Apparel Fashion. Primary key for the retail_store master record, referenced by POS, inventory, workforce, and omnichannel systems as the SSOT store dimension key.']
  cluster_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_cluster. Business justification: Retail stores are grouped into clusters for merchandising, pricing, and operational strategy. The visual_merchandising_plan product references store_cluster_code, indicating stores belong to clusters.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Retail stores are cost centers in apparel operations. Essential for store-level P&L reporting, budget variance analysis, and operational expense allocation. Every store manager reviews cost center per']
  district_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.district. Business justification: Retail stores belong to districts for regional management hierarchy. retail_store currently has district_name (STRING) which is redundant once district_id FK is added. The district table contains dist']
  primary_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.brand. Business justification: Many apparel retail stores are mono-brand (Nike store, Gucci boutique, Levi\'s outlet). primary_brand_id on retail_store drives brand compliance reporting, brand-mandated store standards, co-op marketi']
  primary_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.distribution_center. Business justification: Each store is assigned a primary DC for replenishment routing, inventory allocation planning, and freight cost modeling. Fundamental supply chain network design relationship used in allocation and tra']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Stores are profit centers for segment reporting and profitability analysis. Required for management reporting, regional performance comparison, and strategic decision-making on store portfolio. Standa']
  replenishment_routing_guide_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.routing_guide. Business justification: In apparel fashion, each retail store follows a specific routing guide governing inbound replenishment shipments from DC — specifying carrier, service level, and delivery windows. Store-level routing ']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the retail store location. Used for logistics, lease documentation, customer store-finder, and regulatory filings. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the retail store (suite, floor, unit, mall name, or building identifier). Supplements address_line1 for precise location identification in complex retail environments such as shopping malls or department stores.']
  annual_base_rent decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual base rent amount payable under the store lease agreement in the store\'s local currency. Used for occupancy cost analysis, GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) calculation, and store profitability modelling. Excludes turnover/percentage rent and service charges.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality in which the retail store is located. Used for geographic reporting, tax jurisdiction determination, and regional performance analytics.']
  climate_zone varchar [note: 'Köppen-based climate zone classification for the store\'s geographic location. Drives seasonal assortment planning, markdown timing, and inventory allocation decisions for weather-sensitive apparel categories (outerwear, swimwear, knitwear).']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the store permanently ceased trading operations. Nullable for active stores. Used for store network rationalization reporting, lease surrender tracking, and historical performance analysis. Populated only when operational_status is permanently_closed.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the retail store operates (e.g., USA, GBR, FRA, DEU). Drives currency, tax, regulatory compliance, and international reporting hierarchies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the retail store master record was first created in the system of record (SAP S/4HANA Real Estate / SAP Customer Activity Repository). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the primary trading currency of the store (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Used for POS transaction recording, financial consolidation, and MSRP/AUR reporting in local currency.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary operational email address for the retail store location. Used for internal communications, vendor coordination, and omnichannel order notifications. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  fitting_room_count varchar [note: 'Total number of fitting rooms (changing rooms/dressing rooms) available in the store. Used for store capacity planning, customer experience benchmarking, and visual merchandising layout design. Relevant for apparel conversion rate analytics.']
  gross_selling_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total gross interior square footage of the retail store including all selling, back-of-house, stockroom, and ancillary areas. Used for lease cost-per-square-foot analysis, capital expenditure planning, and store portfolio benchmarking.']
  headcount_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum approved headcount (number of employees) for the store as defined in the workforce plan. Used for labour scheduling, Workday HCM position management, and compliance with local employment regulations. Drives OTB labour cost budgeting.']
  is_bopis_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store is configured and operationally enabled to fulfil Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) orders. Drives omnichannel order routing logic in Salesforce Commerce Cloud and SAP Customer Activity Repository. True = BOPIS active; False = BOPIS not available at this location.']
  is_rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store is equipped with Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) infrastructure for inventory tracking, cycle counting, and loss prevention. RFID-enabled stores support real-time inventory accuracy and ATS (Available to Sell) visibility. True = RFID infrastructure active.']
  is_ship_from_store_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store is configured to fulfil e-commerce orders by shipping directly to customers from store inventory. Enables distributed fulfilment network capabilities. Drives order routing in Salesforce Commerce Cloud and Manhattan Associates WMS. True = ship-from-store active.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the retail store location in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for store-finder applications, trade area mapping, proximity-based marketing, and omnichannel routing (BOPIS, ship-from-store).']
  lease_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the current lease agreement for the store premises. Used for lease renewal pipeline management, store closure planning, and IFRS 16 lease liability amortization schedule. Nullable for month-to-month or perpetual arrangements.']
  lease_start_date date [note: 'Commencement date of the current lease agreement for the store premises as recorded in SAP S/4HANA Real Estate module. Used for lease term tracking, IFRS 16 right-of-use asset calculation, and lease renewal planning.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the retail store location in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used alongside latitude for geospatial analytics, trade area delineation, and omnichannel fulfillment routing.']
  net_selling_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Net productive selling floor area in square feet, excluding stockroom, fitting rooms, cash wrap, and back-of-house. Primary denominator for sales-per-square-foot (SPF) KPI, visual merchandising density planning, and fixture capacity modelling.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date on which the store first opened for trading to customers. Used for store age calculations, comparable store (comp-store) sales analysis, and new store ramp-up performance benchmarking.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the store location within Apparel Fashion\'s retail network. Pre-open indicates the store is in fit-out or pre-launch phase; trading indicates active selling operations; temporarily_closed covers seasonal or refurbishment closures; permanently_closed indicates the store has ceased operations. Drives inclusion/exclusion in POS reporting, inventory allocation, and workforce scheduling.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary customer-facing telephone number for the retail store location. Used for customer service, click-and-collect (BOPIS) coordination, and store directory listings. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  pos_system_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the POS terminal configuration deployed in the store. Fixed terminals are traditional counter-based registers; mobile POS (mPOS) enables floor-based selling; self-checkout enables customer-operated transactions; mixed indicates a combination. Drives IT infrastructure planning and customer experience design.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the retail store location. Used for trade area analysis, catchment modelling, demographic profiling, and logistics routing.']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Name of the geographic region within Apparel Fashion\'s retail organizational hierarchy (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC). Top tier of the region/district/territory hierarchy used for regional performance reporting and resource allocation.']
  register_count varchar [note: 'Total number of active Point of Sale (POS) terminals (registers) installed in the store. Used for transaction throughput capacity planning, queue management, and SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR) terminal configuration management.']
  remodel_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent store remodel or significant capital refurbishment. Used to assess store condition age, plan future capital expenditure cycles, and correlate store investment with post-remodel sales uplift.']
  rent_structure_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rent payment structure under the store lease. Fixed rent is a flat periodic payment; turnover rent is a percentage of net sales; fixed_plus_turnover combines a base rent with a sales-linked top-up; concession_percentage applies to shop-in-shop and concession formats. Drives financial planning and OTB (Open-to-Buy) modelling.']
  standard_trading_hours varchar [note: 'Standard weekly operating hours for the store expressed as a structured string (e.g., \'Mon-Sat 09:00-21:00; Sun 11:00-18:00\'). Used for customer-facing store finder, workforce scheduling in Workday HCM, and daily sales reconciliation cut-off timing in SAP Customer Activity Repository.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region in which the retail store is located. Used for tax jurisdiction mapping, regional hierarchy assignment, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  stockroom_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total square footage of the store\'s back-of-house stockroom area. Used for inventory capacity planning, WMS (Warehouse Management System) configuration, and the calculation of net-to-gross selling area ratio. Informs replenishment frequency and NOS (Never Out of Stock) capability.']
  store_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the store, used across SAP S/4HANA, SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR), Oracle Retail, and EDI integrations. Conforms to NRF ARTS store numbering convention. Unique across all store formats and geographies.']
  store_format varchar [note: 'Operational format classification of the store that determines merchandising strategy, assortment depth, pricing authority, and capital investment profile. Flagship stores carry full-price full-assortment; outlet stores carry clearance/off-price; pop-up stores are temporary; shop-in-shop and concession operate within a host retailer\'s space.']
  store_name varchar [note: 'Official trading name of the retail store location as displayed on signage, customer receipts, and marketing materials. Used for customer-facing communications and brand management reporting.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Tax authority jurisdiction code assigned to the store location for sales tax, VAT, or GST calculation and remittance. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO tax configuration. Used for POS transaction tax determination and regulatory tax reporting.']
  territory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the territory or cluster within the district hierarchy. Lowest tier of the organizational hierarchy above individual store level, used for territory manager assignments and localized assortment planning.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the store\'s local operating time zone (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Critical for accurate POS transaction timestamping, daily sales reconciliation, and omnichannel order cut-off time management in SAP Customer Activity Repository.']
  trading_area_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the retail trading environment in which the store operates. Determines foot traffic patterns, trading hours, lease structure, and competitive benchmarking peer group. Used in store performance analytics and site selection models. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: high_street|shopping_mall|outlet_center|airport|resort|standalone|department_store — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical retail store location operated by Apparel Fashion. Captures store identity, format (flagship, outlet, pop-up, shop-in-shop, concession), trading area classification, gross and net selling square footage, register inventory (terminal count, types, configurations), lease terms and rent structure, regional/district/territory hierarchy, climate zone, and operational status (pre-open, trading, temporarily closed, permanently closed). SSOT for store-level dimensional attributes referenced by POS, inventory, workforce, and omnichannel systems. Conforms to NRF ARTS RetailStore entity. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Real Estate and SAP Customer Activity Repository.'
}

Table store.pos_transaction {
  pos_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pos_transaction']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: B2B wholesale transactions processed at retail locations generate AR invoices for trade customers. Common in apparel brands with hybrid retail/wholesale models where stores serve business accounts req']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: POS transactions track campaign attribution via promo codes and campaign-specific offers. Essential for measuring in-store campaign ROAS, conversion rates, and incremental sales lift—core retail marke']
  cashier_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales associate or cashier who processed the transaction. Used for associate-level performance tracking, commission calculation, and loss prevention audits.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program under which points were earned or redeemed in this transaction. Supports multi-program loyalty analytics and Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) measurement.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the identified customer (loyalty member or registered account) associated with this transaction. Nullable for anonymous/walk-in transactions. Enables Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) and loyalty analytics.']
  promo_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.promo_code. Business justification: POS transactions in apparel retail capture promo code redemptions at checkout. A direct FK to promo_code enables real-time promo performance reporting, fraud detection, and redemption limit enforcemen']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary promotional offer applied to this transaction. Supports promotion effectiveness analysis, markdown (MD) attribution, and Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) reporting.']
  register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific POS register (terminal) at which the transaction was processed within the store. Supports register-level reconciliation and shrinkage analysis.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store where this POS transaction was processed. Links to the store master in the Store Operations domain.']
  return_original_transaction_pos_transaction_id varchar [note: 'For RETURN or EXCHANGE transaction types, references the original sale transaction being returned against. Enables return merchandise authorization (RMA) traceability and shrinkage analysis. Null for non-return transactions.']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.rma. Business justification: In-store returns with defects or warranty claims generate RMA records for vendor credit processing (RTV authorization). Business process: store return disposition triggers formal RMA when item require']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated omnichannel sales order in SAP S/4HANA SD or Salesforce Commerce Cloud for BOPIS, ship-from-store, or endless aisle transactions. Null for standard in-store transactions. Enables cross-channel order traceability.']
  business_date date [note: 'The fiscal/retail business date to which this transaction is attributed for daily sales reconciliation and End-of-Month (EOM) reporting. May differ from the wall-clock date for transactions processed after midnight during extended trading hours.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the omnichannel fulfillment mode under which the transaction was processed. IN_STORE: standard in-store purchase; BOPIS: Buy Online Pick Up In Store; SHIP_FROM_STORE: store fulfills an online order; ENDLESS_AISLE: in-store kiosk ordering; CURBSIDE: curbside pickup. Supports Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transaction record was first ingested into the Silver layer of the Databricks Lakehouse from SAP Customer Activity Repository. Audit trail field for data lineage and ETL reconciliation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the transaction was denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency retail operations and international store reporting.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary value of all discounts and markdowns (MD) applied to the transaction, including promotional discounts, employee discounts, coupon redemptions, and loyalty-funded discounts. Used in Maintained Markup (MMU) and Sell-Through Rate (STR) analysis.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total pre-discount, pre-tax merchandise value of all items in the transaction at their selling price. Represents the sum of line-item extended prices before any adjustments. Used in Average Unit Retail (AUR) and Average Selling Price (ASP) calculations.']
  item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual unit items (units) included in the transaction across all line items. Used for basket size analytics, Average Order Value (AOV) benchmarking, and store traffic conversion metrics.']
  line_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct line items (SKU entries) in the transaction. Differs from item_count when multiple units of the same SKU are purchased. Used for transaction complexity analysis.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty reward points earned by the customer on this transaction based on the applicable loyalty program earn rate. Used for loyalty liability accrual and Customer Relationship Management (CRM) analytics.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty reward points redeemed by the customer as a form of payment or discount on this transaction. Used for loyalty liability reduction and redemption rate analytics.']
  manager_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a manager authorization override was required to complete this transaction (e.g., for price overrides, high-value returns, or discount exceptions). Critical for loss prevention and exception-based reporting.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final amount tendered by the customer: gross_amount minus discount_amount plus tax_amount. Represents the total cash obligation of the transaction. Primary revenue figure for daily sales reconciliation and KPI reporting.']
  price_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a manual price override was applied to one or more items in this transaction, bypassing the system-calculated selling price. Used in loss prevention audits and markdown (MD) exception reporting.']
  receipt_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the transaction receipt was delivered to the customer: PRINT (paper receipt), EMAIL (digital email receipt), SMS (text message receipt), NONE (no receipt requested). Supports sustainability reporting and digital engagement analytics.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Customer-facing receipt identifier printed on the physical or digital receipt. May differ from transaction_number in some POS configurations. Used for customer service, return authorization, and receipt lookup.']
