  planogram_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the planogram PDF or digital asset stored in the content management system. Provides store teams direct access to the fixture layout and product placement diagrams.']
  product_placement_zone_notes varchar [note: 'Narrative instructions describing product placement by zone, wall, table, and window. Captures hero product positioning, color blocking direction, and adjacency rules for the floor set.']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the visual merchandising plan was published and made available to store teams via the distribution channel (e.g., Oracle Retail portal, intranet).']
  signage_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes detailing specific signage placement instructions, size requirements, and messaging guidelines for this visual merchandising plan.']
  signage_specification_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the signage package specification (e.g., price tickets, lifestyle imagery, campaign headers, promotional banners) prescribed for this plan. Links to the signage asset library.']
  size_run_display_standard varchar [note: 'Standard governing how size runs are displayed on the sales floor (e.g., full size run on fixture, key sizes only on mannequin). Aligns with Oracle Retail size curve and assortment planning.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this visual merchandising plan was sourced. ORM = Oracle Retail Merchandising System; CENTRIC_PLM = Centric PLM; MANUAL = manually entered.']
  sustainability_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the visual merchandising plan materials (fixtures, signage, packaging) comply with the company\'s sustainability standards, including GOTS, BCI, and OEKO-TEX requirements for display materials.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this visual merchandising plan record was last modified. Tracks revisions to planogram versions, compliance scores, or corrective action updates.']
  window_display_count varchar [note: 'Number of storefront window displays included in this visual merchandising plan. Used to track window execution compliance separately from interior floor sets.']
  zone_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct merchandising zones (e.g., front-of-store, mid-floor, back wall, window) covered by this visual merchandising plan.']

  Note: 'Captures visual merchandising directives, planogram assignments, and compliance audit results for a store or store cluster. Includes fixture layout, product placement by zone/wall/table/window, seasonal collection display guidelines, mannequin styling instructions, signage specifications, compliance deadline, audit scores, non-compliant zones, and corrective action tracking. After merge with vm_compliance_check, this entity is SSOT for both the plan and its execution verification. Sourced from Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Centric PLM collection management.'
}

Table store.markdown_event {
  markdown_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for markdown_event']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.brand. Business justification: Brand-level markdown events are common in apparel (end-of-season brand clearance, brand exit events). Brand-level markdown authorization requires brand owner approval and drives brand equity reporting']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Markdown events are coordinated with marketing campaigns (seasonal clearance, flash sales). Business tracks which campaigns justify markdowns and measures promotional markdown effectiveness vs. campai']
  cogs_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cogs_entry. Business justification: Markdown events in apparel retail require inventory write-down COGS adjustments under the retail inventory method. Finance links markdown events to COGS entries for inventory valuation adjustments, IM']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.collection. Business justification: Collection-level markdown events are standard in apparel (end-of-season collection clearance). Collection-level markdown tracking enables sell-through reporting by collection, OTB reconciliation, and ']
  colorway_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.colorway. Business justification: Colorway-specific markdowns are standard in apparel (mark down only the red colorway of a style with poor sell-through). Colorway-level markdown events drive targeted clearance strategies and color pe']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Markdowns create inventory valuation adjustments requiring GL posting for accurate gross margin and inventory valuation. Critical for financial reporting compliance and margin analysis. Each markdown ']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchandise hierarchy node (department, class, subclass) to which the SKU belongs at the time of the markdown event. Enables hierarchical MD analysis and category-level GMROI reporting.']
  otb_budget_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget record impacted by this markdown event. Supports OTB reconciliation workflows in Anaplan by linking markdown-driven inventory reductions to planned buy budgets.']
  price_change_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent price change event in the merchandising domain from which this store-level markdown event originates. Supports traceability from store execution back to the central pricing decision.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing promotion or campaign associated with this markdown event, if applicable. Links promotional markdowns to campaign performance tracking in Adobe Experience Platform and SAP Customer Activity Repository.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail store at which this markdown event is executed. Links to the store master record for store-level MD tracking and performance analysis.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchandise season (e.g., Spring/Summer, Fall/Winter) associated with this markdown event. Supports seasonal MD analysis, OTB reconciliation, and collection lifecycle tracking.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) subject to the markdown event. Enables SKU-level MD tracking, sell-through rate (STR) analysis, and GMROI reporting.']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: Markdown decisions require landed cost context from sourcing PO for accurate margin analysis, IMU/MMU calculations, and vendor cost reconciliation. Critical for pricing strategy and profitability repo']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.style. Business justification: Markdown events in apparel are initiated at the style level (all SKUs of a style marked down together). Style-level markdown authorization, financial reporting, and sell-through analysis are core appa']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: Markdown events often require vendor negotiation for markdown allowances or return-to-vendor (RTV) authorization. Real business process: buyers negotiate vendor-funded markdowns or cost recovery on sl']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Markdowns resulting from production quality issues, overruns, or late deliveries require work order linkage for factory chargeback calculations, vendor performance scorecards, and cost recovery negoti']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the markdown event was formally approved in Oracle Retail Price Management. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the markdown authorization lifecycle.']
  authorization_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or code from the authorization workflow that approved this markdown event. Typically sourced from Oracle Retail Price Management approval chain. Required for audit compliance and financial controls.']
  channel_applicability varchar [note: 'Specifies the retail channel(s) to which this markdown event applies. Supports omnichannel pricing consistency, BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), and ship-from-store fulfillment scenarios.']
  cost_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Unit cost of goods sold (COGS) for the SKU at the time of the markdown event. Used to calculate gross margin impact of the markdown and support GMROI analysis. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM module.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this markdown event record was first created in the source system (Oracle Retail Price Management). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this markdown event record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency retail operations and financial consolidation.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the markdown price becomes active and is applied at the store POS. Aligns with the Oracle Retail Price Management effective date field. Used for OTB reconciliation and fiscal period reporting.']
  end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the markdown event expires and the price reverts (for temporary/promotional markdowns). Null for permanent markdowns. Used for event duration analysis and POS price management.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the markdown event within the Oracle Retail Price Management workflow. Controls whether the markdown is visible and applied at the POS.']
  initial_markup_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Initial Markup (IMU) percentage at the time the SKU was first priced for the season, expressed as a decimal. Provides the baseline for measuring markdown erosion of margin and maintained markup (MMU) tracking.']
  maintained_markup_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Maintained Markup (MMU) percentage after applying the markdown, reflecting the actual margin retained post-markdown. Calculated as (markdown_price minus cost_price) / markdown_price. Critical for GMROI analysis and financial planning in Anaplan.']
  markdown_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Absolute monetary reduction applied to the original retail price (original_retail_price minus markdown_price). Expressed in the store\'s operating currency. Used for financial impact reporting and GMROI analysis.']
  markdown_event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the markdown event as assigned by Oracle Retail Price Management. Used for cross-system reference, audit trails, and reconciliation with source system records.']
  markdown_level varchar [note: 'Granularity at which the markdown event is defined and applied within the merchandise hierarchy. SKU-level markdowns are the most granular; department-level markdowns apply broadly across a category.']
  markdown_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage reduction from the original retail price to the markdown price, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.3000 = 30%). Key metric for MD depth analysis, OTB reconciliation, and maintained markup (MMU) tracking.']
  markdown_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The new selling price for the SKU after the markdown event is applied. This is the price presented to customers at POS during the markdown event period. Core field for AUR and ASP reporting.']
  markdown_reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason driving the markdown event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clearance|seasonal|competitive|slow_seller|overstock|damage|end_of_line — promote to reference product]']
  markdown_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the markdown event by pricing permanence. Permanent markdowns reduce the ongoing retail price; temporary markdowns revert after the event period; promotional markdowns are tied to a campaign; clearance markdowns are end-of-lifecycle reductions.']
  msrp_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The original Manufacturer\'s Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) for the SKU prior to any markdown. Serves as the baseline for markdown percentage calculation and IMU/MMU analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by the merchandising team regarding the markdown event. May include competitive intelligence, vendor negotiation context, or store-specific execution instructions.']
  on_hand_qty varchar [note: 'Store-level on-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the time the markdown event is created. Captured to support sell-through rate (STR) analysis, weeks of supply (WOS) calculation, and OTB reconciliation.']
  original_retail_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The store\'s current selling price for the SKU immediately before this markdown event is applied. May differ from MSRP due to prior price changes. Used as the direct basis for markdown amount and percentage calculations.']
  pos_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the markdown price has been successfully pushed to and activated at the store Point of Sale (POS) system (SAP Customer Activity Repository). True when the markdown is live at POS; False when pending or failed.']
  price_label_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether physical price label reprinting is required for store fixtures and merchandise as part of this markdown event. Drives visual merchandising and store operations task management.']
  price_zone_code varchar [note: 'Oracle Retail Price Management price zone code identifying the geographic or store-cluster pricing zone to which this markdown event applies. Supports zone-based pricing strategy and regional MD analysis.']
  rfid_repriced_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RFID-tagged units for this SKU have been repriced and relabeled in the store as part of the markdown event execution. Supports RFID-enabled inventory accuracy and shrinkage control.']
  sell_through_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Ratio of units sold during the markdown event to on-hand quantity at event start, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.6500 = 65% STR). Key KPI for markdown effectiveness and inventory liquidation performance. Sourced or calculated from SAP Customer Activity Repository data.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Native primary key or record identifier of this markdown event in the originating source system (Oracle Retail Price Management). Enables exact record traceability and idempotent Silver Layer upserts.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this markdown event record was sourced (e.g., ORM = Oracle Retail Merchandising, SAP_CAR = SAP Customer Activity Repository). Supports data lineage and Silver Layer provenance tracking.']
  units_sold_during_event varchar [note: 'Total number of units of the SKU sold at the markdown price during the event period. Populated at event close or via daily reconciliation from SAP Customer Activity Repository. Supports STR and markdown effectiveness analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this markdown event record was last modified in the source system. Tracks amendments to markdown pricing, status changes, or date adjustments post-creation.']

  Note: 'Records markdown pricing events executed at the store level, including SKU, original MSRP, markdown price, markdown percentage, effective date, end date, markdown reason (clearance, seasonal, competitive), and authorization reference. Supports MD tracking, GMROI analysis, and OTB reconciliation. Sourced from Oracle Retail Price Management.'
}

  cluster_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for store_cluster']

FK to customer.segment suppor']
  parent_store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on store_cluster (parent_store_cluster_id)']
  primary_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.distribution_center. Business justification: In apparel fashion, store clusters are served by a primary DC for replenishment allocation and inventory planning. Cluster-level DC assignment drives replenishment routing, allocation decisions, and l']
  average_store_size_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average retail floor space in square feet across all stores in the cluster.']
  climate_zone varchar [note: 'Primary climate zone classification for the cluster used for seasonal merchandise planning and allocation.']

  cluster_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the store cluster for business users and reporting purposes.']

  cluster_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cluster based on grouping methodology: geographic proximity, demographic profile, performance tier, store format similarity, strategic importance, or seasonal behavior.']
  competitive_intensity varchar [note: 'Level of competitive pressure from other apparel retailers in the cluster\'s market area.']

  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the cluster configuration became effective and stores were assigned to it.']

  is_flagship_cluster boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cluster contains flagship or brand showcase stores requiring premium treatment.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cluster record was last updated or modified.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Business email address of the cluster manager for operational communication.']

  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about the cluster configuration, special considerations, or historical context.']
  omnichannel_capability varchar [note: 'Level of omnichannel fulfillment capabilities enabled for stores in this cluster (BOPIS: Buy Online Pick-up In Store).']
  planning_system_code varchar [note: 'Cluster identifier used in merchandise planning and allocation systems (e.g., SAP Customer Activity Repository).']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Pricing approach applied to the cluster reflecting market positioning and competitive dynamics.']
  replenishment_frequency varchar [note: 'Standard inventory replenishment frequency for stores in this cluster.']
  seasonal_profile varchar [note: 'Seasonal sales pattern classification for the cluster used in demand forecasting and inventory planning (e.g., winter-peak, summer-peak, balanced).']
  store_count varchar [note: 'Total number of stores currently assigned to this cluster.']
  traffic_pattern varchar [note: 'Typical customer foot traffic pattern classification for stores in the cluster.']
  visual_merchandising_standard varchar [note: 'Visual merchandising and store presentation standard applied to the cluster.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for store_cluster. Referenced by store_cluster_id.'
}

Table store.register {
  register_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for register']
  replaced_register_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on register (replaced_register_id)']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail store where this register is located.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the register was activated and made available for processing transactions.']
  barcode_scanner_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this register is equipped with a barcode scanning device.']
  card_reader_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this register is equipped with and configured to accept card payments.']
  cash_drawer_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this register is equipped with and configured to use a physical cash drawer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this register record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for transactions processed at this register.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when the register was deactivated or taken out of service. Null if currently active.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the firmware installed on the register hardware.']
  floor_level varchar [note: 'Floor number where the register is located within multi-level retail stores.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the register was installed and commissioned for use in the store.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Network IP address assigned to the register for connectivity and system integration.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance or service performed on the register.']
  location_zone varchar [note: 'Physical zone or area within the store where the register is positioned (e.g., front entrance, back corner, fitting rooms).']
  mac_address varchar [note: 'Hardware MAC address of the register network interface for device identification.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the point-of-sale hardware.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum transaction value that can be processed at this register without manager override.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model number or designation for the register hardware.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next preventive maintenance or service check.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, special instructions, or operational comments about the register.']
  offline_mode_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this register can continue processing transactions when network connectivity is lost.']
  payment_terminal_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the payment terminal device associated with this register for card processing.']
  receipt_printer_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this register has a functioning receipt printer attached.']
  register_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label assigned to the register for easy identification by store staff.']
  register_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique register number used for operational identification and reporting.']
  register_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the register indicating its availability for transactions.']
  register_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the register based on its operational purpose and configuration.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer for hardware identification and warranty tracking.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the POS software application currently running on the register.']
  supports_employee_discount boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this register can apply employee discount codes and pricing.']
  supports_exchanges boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this register is configured to process product exchanges.']
  supports_gift_cards boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this register can process gift card sales, redemptions, and balance inquiries.']
  supports_layaway boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this register can process layaway transactions and payments.']
  supports_loyalty_program boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this register is configured to process loyalty program enrollments and point redemptions.']
  supports_returns boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this register is configured to process customer returns and refunds.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this register record was last modified in the system.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer warranty coverage for the register hardware expires.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for register. Referenced by register_id.'
}

Business justification: Apparel fashion districts are operationally aligned to a primary DC for replenishment, returns consolidation, and logistics SLA reporting. District-to-DC assignment is used in replenishment planning, ']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent region that this district belongs to in the organizational hierarchy.']
  annual_sales_target decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned annual sales revenue target for the district, used for performance measurement and incentive calculations.']
  annual_traffic_target varchar [note: 'Planned annual customer traffic target across all stores in the district, used for conversion and productivity analysis.']
  bopis_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Buy Online Pickup In Store capability is enabled for stores in this district.']
  comp_store_sales_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Year-over-year comparable store sales growth target percentage for the district.']

  omnichannel_fulfillment_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stores in this district are enabled for omnichannel fulfillment capabilities including Buy Online Pickup In Store (BOPIS) and ship-from-store.']
  sap_car_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stores in this district are integrated with SAP Customer Activity Repository for traffic and customer analytics.']

  store_count varchar [note: 'Total number of active stores currently assigned to this district.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the district headquarters location, used for scheduling and reporting alignment.']

  visual_merchandising_standard varchar [note: 'Visual merchandising standard tier applied to stores within this district, determining display guidelines and fixture requirements.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for district. Referenced by district_id.'
}

Table store.planogram {
  planogram_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for planogram']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.brand. Business justification: Planograms in apparel retail are frequently brand-specific (Nike wall, Levi\'s fixture bay). Brand-level planogram assignment drives brand compliance audits, co-op advertising requirements, and brand-m']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary product category this planogram is designed for.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.collection. Business justification: Planograms in apparel are built around seasonal collections (Spring 2025 collection floor reset). Collection-level planogram assignment drives seasonal floor resets, OTB alignment, and collection laun']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Apparel planograms are designed for specific customer segments (target_demographic plain-text column exists). FK to customer.segment enables space productivity analysis and assortment optimization by ']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Planograms in apparel are built for specific merchandise hierarchy nodes (Women\'s Denim, Men\'s Footwear). Hierarchy-based planogram organization is a standard VM process — space planning and fixture a']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Planograms in apparel are season-specific (spring floor set vs. fall floor set). Seasonal planogram management is a core VM process — planograms are created, approved, and retired by season. season_co']
  superseded_planogram_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on planogram (superseded_planogram_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of the planogram in the design and review process.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this planogram was officially approved for store implementation.']
  average_unit_retail_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average unit retail price in USD of products included in this planogram, used for sales forecasting.']
  climate_zone varchar [note: 'Climate zone classification for which this planogram is optimized, affecting seasonal product mix.']
  color_story varchar [note: 'Dominant color palette or color blocking strategy used in this planogram to create visual impact.']
  compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether strict compliance to this planogram is mandatory or if stores have flexibility to adapt.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for country-specific planogram variations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this planogram record was first created in the system.']
  digital_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this planogram includes digital display elements, QR codes, or interactive technology.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this planogram is scheduled to be retired and replaced with a new layout. Null for open-ended planograms.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this planogram becomes active and should be implemented in stores.']
  fixture_count varchar [note: 'Number of fixtures or display units required to implement this planogram.']
  fixture_type varchar [note: 'Primary type of retail fixture or display unit this planogram is designed for.']
  floor_space_sqm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total floor space in square meters required to implement this planogram.']
  height_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum vertical height in centimeters of the planogram display from floor to top shelf.']
  implementation_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated time in hours required for store staff to implement this planogram from start to completion.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this planogram record was last updated or modified.']
  merchandising_zone varchar [note: 'Strategic zone within the store where this planogram is intended to be placed for optimal customer engagement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional implementation instructions, special considerations, or merchandising tips for store teams.']
  omnichannel_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this planogram supports omnichannel features such as buy online pickup in store (BOPIS) or ship from store integration.']
  planogram_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the planogram used across systems and communications.']
  planogram_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the planogram including merchandising strategy, target customer segment, and visual presentation goals.']
  planogram_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the planogram describing its purpose or target category.']
  planogram_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the planogram based on merchandising strategy and product lifecycle stage.']
  price_point_strategy varchar [note: 'Pricing tier strategy for products featured in this planogram.']
  projected_sales_per_sqm_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Forecasted sales revenue per square meter in USD expected from this planogram based on historical performance.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code where this planogram is intended to be deployed (e.g., NAM, EUR, APAC).']
  store_format varchar [note: 'Type of retail store format this planogram is designed for, reflecting store size and merchandising approach.']
  style_theme varchar [note: 'Fashion style theme or aesthetic direction of the planogram (e.g., athleisure, minimalist, bold prints, heritage).']
  sustainability_rating varchar [note: 'Sustainability rating of the planogram based on percentage of eco-friendly and sustainable products featured.']
  total_sku_count varchar [note: 'Total number of unique SKUs included in this planogram layout.']
  total_unit_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of individual product units that can be displayed when the planogram is fully stocked.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this planogram, incremented with each revision or update.']
  wall_space_linear_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total linear wall space in meters required for wall-mounted fixtures in this planogram.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for planogram. Referenced by planogram_id.'
}

Table logistics.shipment {
  shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment record. Primary key for the logistics shipment entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier or logistics service provider responsible for moving the shipment.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.collection. Business justification: Seasonal collection-level OTIF reporting and shipment planning are standard apparel logistics KPIs. Merchants and logistics teams track on-time delivery by collection to ensure floor-set readiness. Wi']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.contract. Business justification: Every shipment in apparel logistics is executed under a specific carrier or 3PL contract that governs rates, service levels, and terms. Linking shipment directly to contract enables contract utilizati']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Freight costs are allocated to cost centers for departmental budgeting and P&L tracking. Monthly cost center reporting requires shipment-level freight allocation to calculate actual vs budget variance']
  destination_facility_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility, warehouse, store, or customer location to which the shipment is destined.']
  hs_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.hs_code. Business justification: Shipments are classified by HS code for customs and duty purposes. N:1 relationship. Normalizes hs_code string to FK for referential integrity and to enable HS code master data management.']
  letter_of_credit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.letter_of_credit. Business justification: Letters of credit are drawn upon presentation of shipping documents for a specific shipment. Apparel importers link LCs to shipments to verify latest shipment date compliance, track document presentat']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.lot. Business justification: Shipments carry goods from specific production lots. Direct lot-to-shipment traceability is critical for quality recalls — if a lot fails post-shipment QC, logistics must immediately identify the ship']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.material. Business justification: Inbound raw material shipments from fabric/trim suppliers to factories must be traceable to the specific material record for production planning, restricted substance compliance, and sustainability ce']
  origin_factory_supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: Inbound shipments from supplier factories require direct factory tracking for origin verification, GSP/duty calculations, factory OTIF performance metrics, and compliance audits. Multi-factory vendors']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.factory. Business justification: Outbound shipments from factories require origin factory tracking for customs country-of-origin certification, GSP eligibility validation, factory OTIF performance measurement, and freight cost alloca']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Shipments serve specific channels/brands tracked via profit centers. Channel profitability analysis requires linking shipment costs to profit centers to calculate true landed cost by channel for margi']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.rfq. Business justification: Sample shipments for RFQ evaluation require traceability to originating RFQ for quality validation, vendor performance tracking, and sample cost reconciliation in apparel sourcing operations.']
  routing_guide_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.routing_guide. Business justification: Shipments should follow approved routing guide rules for carrier and lane selection. N:1 relationship. Enables routing compliance analysis and SLA tracking against routing guide targets.']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.schedule. Business justification: Production schedules drive ex-factory dates and shipment booking windows. Linking shipment to schedule enables OTIF monitoring — logistics planners compare actual shipment dates against planned ex-fac']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Season-level OTIF and delivery performance reporting: apparel shipments must be attributed to a season for on-time delivery KPI tracking by collection. Merchandising and logistics teams jointly review']
  shipment_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility, warehouse, or manufacturing location from which the shipment originates.']
  shipping_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: DTC last-mile delivery and carrier label generation require the customer\'s validated shipping address directly on the shipment record. Apparel brands use this for address verification, carrier routing']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: Core operational link - inbound shipments of finished goods originate from sourcing POs; essential for ASN matching, receiving reconciliation, OTIF measurement, and landed cost allocation in apparel s']
  third_party_logistics_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the 3PL partner managing logistics operations for this shipment, if applicable.']
  tna_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_tna_calendar. Business justification: In apparel fashion, each shipment is executed against a TNA calendar\'s planned ex-factory and in-DC dates. Linking shipment to sourcing_tna_calendar enables direct OTIF measurement against the seasona']
  wholesale_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.wholesale_account. Business justification: Wholesale OTIF performance reporting and account-level logistics SLA tracking are standard KPIs in apparel wholesale operations. A direct wholesale_account_id on shipment enables account-level on-time']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Inbound finished goods shipments from factories must reference originating work orders for landed cost allocation, duty calculation per PO/WO, and end-to-end production-to-delivery traceability requir']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the shipment arrived at the destination facility or port.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the shipment departed from the origin facility or port.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Legal document number issued by the carrier acknowledging receipt of cargo for shipment.']
  booking_reference varchar [note: 'Carrier or freight forwarder booking reference number for the shipment reservation.']
  carton_count varchar [note: 'Total number of cartons or packages included in the shipment.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the shipping container (for ocean FCL shipments), following ISO 6346 standard.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was first created in the system.']
  cubic_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume of the shipment measured in cubic meters, used for freight cost calculation.']
  current_milestone varchar [note: 'Most recent shipment milestone or checkpoint reached in the logistics journey (e.g., departed origin, in customs, out for delivery).']
  customs_entry_number varchar [note: 'Customs declaration or entry number assigned by customs authorities for international shipments.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the shipment destination location.']
  duty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total customs duty amount paid or payable for the shipment in the company\'s reporting currency.']
  estimated_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned or estimated timestamp when the shipment is expected to arrive at the destination facility or port.']
  estimated_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned or estimated timestamp when the shipment is expected to depart from the origin facility or port.']
  freight_cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated freight transportation cost for the shipment in the company\'s reporting currency.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment including packaging, measured in kilograms.']
  gsp_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment qualifies for GSP tariff preferences, reducing or eliminating customs duties.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International commercial term defining the responsibilities of buyers and sellers for the delivery of goods (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  insurance_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Declared value of the shipment for insurance purposes in the company\'s reporting currency.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was most recently modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions related to the shipment handling, routing, or delivery requirements.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the shipment origin location.']
  otif_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment was delivered on time and in full per the agreed service level agreement.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets included in the shipment.']
  priority_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment has been flagged as high priority requiring expedited handling.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the shipment, used across systems and with external partners.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipment in the logistics workflow.']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment based on commercial terms and destination type. FOB (Free on Board), LDP (Landed Duty Paid), DTC (Direct-to-Consumer), wholesale, inter-facility transfer, or RTV (Return to Vendor).']
  sla_delivery_date date [note: 'Target delivery date per the service level agreement with the customer or internal business unit.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for real-time shipment visibility and last-mile delivery tracking.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for the shipment. FCL (Full Container Load), LCL (Less than Container Load). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ocean_fcl|ocean_lcl|air|ground|courier|rail|intermodal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Core master record for every inbound and outbound shipment movement in the apparel supply chain. Captures shipment type (FOB, LDP, DTC, wholesale, inter-facility transfer), origin and destination facilities, carrier assignment, transport mode (ocean FCL/LCL, air, ground, courier, rail), booking reference, ETD/ETA and actual departure/arrival timestamps, carton count, gross weight, cubic volume, freight cost estimate, insurance value, current milestone status, and priority flag. Serves as the SSOT for shipment identity across the logistics domain, linking to purchase orders, sales orders, 3PL partners, customs entries, and freight bookings. Supports both pre-retail (factory-to-DC) and post-retail (DC-to-store, DC-to-consumer) movements.'
}

Table logistics.shipment_milestone {
  shipment_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment milestone event record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Shipment milestones are recorded by carriers at various transit points. N:1 relationship. Normalizes carrier_scac_code string to FK for carrier performance tracking by milestone.']
  customs_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.customs_entry. Business justification: Shipment milestones include customs clearance events (customs_cleared_flag, duty_amount, hs_code fields exist on shipment_milestone). Linking directly to the customs_entry record enables traceability ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Milestone events (customs clearance, port arrival, delivery) trigger financial accruals posted to GL accounts — duty accruals, demurrage charges, and freight cost recognition. Apparel importers post m']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent shipment for which this milestone event was recorded.']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: Milestone tracking (ex-factory, in-transit, customs clearance, in-DC) is performed at sourcing PO level for TNA compliance monitoring, OTIF measurement, and delay escalation in apparel supply chain.']
  sourcing_tna_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_tna_milestone. Business justification: In apparel supply chain, logistics milestones (ex-factory, port departure, DC arrival) directly confirm or trigger sourcing TNA milestones. This link is core to critical path tracking and delay report']
  supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: Ex-factory and goods-ready milestones in apparel logistics must reference the originating supplier factory for OTIF factory performance reporting and CBP country-of-origin audit trails. A supply chain']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Shipment milestones (ex-factory, in-transit, delivered) are tracked against work orders in apparel TNA management. Production and logistics teams jointly monitor ex-factory milestone compliance per wo']
  actual_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the shipment reached this milestone status, recorded in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  city varchar [note: 'City name where the milestone location is situated.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the shipping container or trailer associated with this milestone, used for ocean and intermodal freight tracking.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the milestone location is situated (e.g., USA, CHN, VNM).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this milestone record was first created in the system, recorded in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts recorded at this milestone (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  customs_cleared_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether customs clearance was completed at this milestone, applicable for international shipments.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'System or channel from which this milestone data was captured (e.g., WMS, EDI, CARRIER_API, GPS_TRACKER, MANUAL_ENTRY, CUSTOMS_SYSTEM).']
  duty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total customs duty amount assessed at this milestone for international shipments, used for Landed Duty Paid (LDP) calculations.']
  edi_transaction_set varchar [note: 'Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) transaction set code that provided this milestone data (e.g., 214 for Transportation Carrier Shipment Status Message).']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the type of exception or issue encountered at this milestone (e.g., WEATHER_DELAY, CUSTOMS_HOLD, DAMAGED_GOODS, ADDRESS_CORRECTION).']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Detailed text description of the exception or issue encountered, providing context for root cause analysis and resolution.']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this milestone represents an exception or deviation from the planned shipment flow (e.g., delay, damage, customs hold).']
  gsp_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether the shipment qualifies for Generalized System of Preferences (GSP) duty-free treatment.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized System (HS) code used for customs classification of the goods in this shipment, required for international trade compliance.']
  humidity_reading decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage recorded at this milestone, applicable for shipments requiring controlled environmental conditions.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the milestone location in decimal degrees, used for GPS tracking and route optimization.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific facility, port, or address where the milestone event occurred (e.g., UN/LOCODE for ports, internal facility codes for warehouses and stores).']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the location where the milestone occurred (e.g., Port of Los Angeles, Memphis Distribution Center, Store #1234).']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the location where the milestone occurred (e.g., PORT, WAREHOUSE, DISTRIBUTION_CENTER, STORE, CUSTOMER_ADDRESS, TRANSSHIPMENT_HUB, CUSTOMS_FACILITY). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PORT|WAREHOUSE|DISTRIBUTION_CENTER|STORE|CUSTOMER_ADDRESS|TRANSSHIPMENT_HUB|CUSTOMS_FACILITY|CARRIER_FACILITY — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the milestone location in decimal degrees, used for GPS tracking and route optimization.']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the milestone status reached by the shipment (e.g., BOOKED, DEPARTED_ORIGIN, ARRIVED_TRANSSHIPMENT, CUSTOMS_CLEARED, ARRIVED_DC, DELIVERED). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BOOKED|DEPARTED_ORIGIN|IN_TRANSIT|ARRIVED_TRANSSHIPMENT|CUSTOMS_CLEARED|ARRIVED_DC|OUT_FOR_DELIVERY|DELIVERED|EXCEPTION|CANCELLED — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the milestone event (e.g., Departed Origin Port, Customs Cleared, Delivered to Store).']
  milestone_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this milestone within the shipment lifecycle, used to track progression through the supply chain.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text notes or comments providing additional context about this milestone event, used for operational communication and issue resolution.']
  otif_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this milestone meets On Time In Full (OTIF) delivery criteria for the shipment.']
  planned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The originally planned or scheduled date and time for this milestone, used to calculate variance and On Time In Full (OTIF) performance.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the milestone location, used for geographic analysis and last-mile delivery tracking.']
  recorded_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user or automated process that recorded this milestone event.']
  responsible_party_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or code for the specific carrier, Third-Party Logistics (3PL) provider, customs broker, or other party responsible for this milestone.']
  responsible_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the carrier, Third-Party Logistics (3PL) provider, customs broker, freight forwarder, or other party responsible for this milestone.']
  responsible_party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the party responsible for executing or recording this milestone (e.g., CARRIER, 3PL, CUSTOMS_BROKER, FREIGHT_FORWARDER, WAREHOUSE_OPERATOR). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CARRIER|3PL|CUSTOMS_BROKER|FREIGHT_FORWARDER|WAREHOUSE_OPERATOR|INTERNAL|CUSTOMER — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this milestone was achieved within the Service Level Agreement (SLA) time window.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code where the milestone location is situated.']
  temperature_reading decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature reading in Celsius recorded at this milestone, applicable for temperature-controlled shipments of apparel with special storage requirements.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking or waybill number for this shipment leg, used for parcel and courier shipments.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the shipment leg associated with this milestone (e.g., OCEAN, AIR, TRUCK, RAIL, INTERMODAL, PARCEL). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OCEAN|AIR|TRUCK|RAIL|INTERMODAL|PARCEL|COURIER — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  variance_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The time difference in hours between planned and actual milestone timestamp, used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance tracking. Positive values indicate delay, negative values indicate early arrival.']
  vessel_voyage_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the vessel, flight, or voyage number associated with this milestone, applicable for ocean and air freight.']

  Note: 'Transactional event log capturing every status milestone reached by a shipment throughout its lifecycle — booked, departed origin port, arrived transshipment hub, customs cleared, arrived destination DC, delivered to store or customer. Records milestone code, actual timestamp, location (port, facility, GPS coordinates), responsible party (carrier, 3PL, customs broker), exception flag, and variance from planned milestone date. Enables OTIF tracking and SLA compliance monitoring for apparel shipments.'
}

Table logistics.freight_booking {
  freight_booking_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the freight booking record. Primary key for the logistics freight booking entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or freight forwarder responsible for transporting the shipment. Links to the carrier master data.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.collection. Business justification: Freight bookings in apparel are made by collection/season to secure carrier capacity for seasonal deliveries. collection_season_code is a denormalized plain attribute; replacing it with a proper FK to']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.contract. Business justification: Freight bookings are made under specific carrier contracts or rate agreements. The freight_booking has a rate_agreement_reference string field that is a natural candidate for normalization to a FK to ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Freight bookings are charged to cost centers for logistics budget tracking and variance reporting. Apparel supply chain finance teams monitor freight spend vs. cost center budget; this link enables fr']
  delivery_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.delivery_window. Business justification: Freight bookings are made to meet specific delivery windows. The freight booking references the delivery window to ensure space is allocated within the correct ship window — a standard apparel logisti']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Freight booking costs require GL account assignment for freight expense accrual at booking stage. Apparel brands accrue freight charges before carrier invoice receipt; the GL account drives automated ']
  hs_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.hs_code. Business justification: Freight bookings declare HS codes for cargo classification and customs pre-clearance. N:1 relationship. Normalizes hs_code string to FK.']
  letter_of_credit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.letter_of_credit. Business justification: Freight bookings under LC payment terms must reference the LC to verify latest shipment date compliance. Apparel sourcing teams check that the booking date does not exceed the LC\'s latest shipment dat']
  origin_factory_supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: Freight bookings for factory shipments need factory origin for routing guide selection, port-of-loading determination, and factory-specific consolidation planning. Vendor_id insufficient when vendor o']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.factory. Business justification: Freight bookings for export shipments originate from factories. Factory reference enables factory-level freight cost analysis, capacity planning coordination between production and logistics, and rout']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Freight costs are allocated to profit centers for brand/channel P&L reporting. Apparel companies with multi-brand structures allocate inbound freight by profit center to compute accurate landed cost a']
  routing_guide_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.routing_guide. Business justification: Freight bookings should follow approved routing guide rules for carrier selection and lane assignment. N:1 relationship (many bookings follow one routing guide). Enables compliance tracking and rate v']
  sample_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sample_request. Business justification: Apparel sample shipments (proto, fit, PP samples) are booked via freight bookings (courier/air). Linking freight_booking to sample_request enables sample tracking, freight cost allocation to the sampl']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.schedule. Business justification: Freight bookings are made based on the production schedule\'s planned ex-factory date. Freight forwarders reference the schedule to confirm space allocation timing. This direct link enables automated b']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Seasonal freight capacity planning: freight_booking.collection_season_code is a denormalized plain attribute. Proper FK to merchandising.season enables season-level freight booking analysis, capacity ']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment that this booking covers. Links to the shipment master record for tracking goods movement.']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: Freight bookings for inbound shipments are created based on sourcing PO ship windows, ex-factory dates, and delivery requirements to ensure on-time arrival for apparel seasonal launches.']
  third_party_logistics_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the 3PL provider managing the freight booking and logistics coordination. Links to the 3PL partner master data.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customs broker responsible for clearing the shipment through customs at the destination. Links to the customs broker master data.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Freight bookings in apparel are made against specific work orders to coordinate ex-factory pickup timing and confirm quantities. Logistics planners and freight forwarders reference the work order to a']
  booking_date date [note: 'Date when the freight booking was formally placed with the carrier or freight forwarder. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  booking_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments related to the freight booking. Captures operational details, exceptions, or coordination information.']
  booking_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique booking reference number assigned by the carrier or freight forwarder. Used for tracking and communication with logistics partners.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the freight booking. Tracks progression from draft through confirmation, execution, and completion or cancellation.']
  confirmed_space_allocation decimal(15,3) [note: 'Confirmed cargo space allocated by the carrier for this booking, measured in cubic meters (CBM) or container equivalent units (TEU). Supports capacity planning.']
  container_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of containers booked for this freight booking. Applicable for FCL (Full Container Load) shipments.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Type of shipping container used for the booking. Includes standard dry containers, high-cube, refrigerated (reefer), and specialized containers. Applicable for ocean freight FCL bookings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this freight booking record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  cut_off_date date [note: 'Final date and time by which cargo must be delivered to the carrier or terminal to meet the scheduled departure. Critical for operational planning.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the destination country where the shipment will be delivered. Three-letter uppercase code.']
  estimated_arrival_date date [note: 'Estimated date when the carrier is scheduled to arrive at the port of discharge or destination terminal.']
  estimated_departure_date date [note: 'Estimated date when the carrier is scheduled to depart from the port of loading or origin terminal.']
  freight_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total freight charge amount for this booking as per the rate agreement. Excludes surcharges, duties, and taxes.']
  freight_charge_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the freight charge amount. Indicates the currency in which the freight charge is denominated.']
  gsp_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the shipment is eligible for GSP duty-free treatment. True if eligible, False otherwise. Supports duty optimization.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and documentation. True if hazardous, False otherwise.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the responsibilities, costs, and risks between buyer and seller for this shipment. Examples: FOB (Free on Board), CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  insurance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether cargo insurance is required for this booking. True if insurance is required, False otherwise.']
  insurance_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Declared value of the cargo for insurance purposes. Used to calculate insurance premiums and coverage limits.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this freight booking record was last updated. Audit trail for record modifications.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country of origin where the shipment originates. Three-letter uppercase code.']
  port_of_discharge_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE for the port or terminal where goods are unloaded from the carrier. Five-character alphanumeric code.']
  port_of_loading_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE (United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations) for the port or terminal where goods are loaded onto the carrier. Five-character alphanumeric code.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of freight service booked. FCL (Full Container Load), LCL (Less than Container Load), air charter, express courier, ocean freight, road freight, rail freight, or courier service. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FCL|LCL|AIR_CHARTER|AIR_EXPRESS|OCEAN_FREIGHT|ROAD_FREIGHT|RAIL_FREIGHT|COURIER — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  space_allocation_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the confirmed space allocation. CBM (Cubic Meters), TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit), FEU (Forty-foot Equivalent Unit), KG (Kilograms), or LBS (Pounds).']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special handling requirements for the shipment, such as temperature control, fragile handling, hazardous materials, or priority processing.']
  vessel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vessel (for ocean freight) or flight number (for air freight) assigned to transport the shipment. Null for road or rail freight.']
  voyage_number varchar [note: 'Voyage number for ocean freight or flight number for air freight. Identifies the specific journey leg for the shipment.']

  Note: 'Records the formal booking of freight capacity with a carrier or freight forwarder for apparel shipments. Captures booking reference number, carrier/forwarder identity, service type (FCL, LCL, air charter, express courier), vessel or flight details, port of loading, port of discharge, booking date, cut-off date, confirmed space allocation, rate agreement reference, and booking status. Supports capacity planning and carrier performance management for seasonal collection launches.'
}

Table logistics.customs_entry {
  customs_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customs entry declaration record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Customs entry records the carrier that transported the goods. Currently stored as carrier_code string; normalize to FK for referential integrity and to enable carrier performance analysis by customs c']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Import duties are allocated to requesting departments for cost accountability. Cost center managers need visibility into duty costs for their purchase orders to manage budgets and explain variances.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Duty payments post to specific GL accounts for financial reporting. Month-end close requires customs duty accrual and payment posting to correct GL accounts for balance sheet and P&L accuracy.']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_factory. Business justification: Customs entries require country-of-origin declarations referencing the production factory. For brands with owned factories, production_factory is distinct from supplier_factory (already linked). Direc']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Customs entries drive duty costs allocated to profit centers for brand/channel P&L and landed cost reporting. Apparel importers assign customs entries to profit centers so duty expenses flow into the ']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Customs entries are filed for specific shipments arriving at port. N:1 relationship (multiple customs entries can relate to one consolidated shipment). Normalizes bill_of_lading_number which can be re']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: Customs entries must reference sourcing PO for duty calculation, landed cost allocation, compliance documentation, and reconciling declared values with PO terms in apparel import operations.']
  sourcing_purchase_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order_line. Business justification: CBP customs entries in apparel are filed at line level (each entry line = one PO line with its own HS code, value, COO). Line-level FK enables duty drawback claims, trade compliance audits, and entry ']
  third_party_logistics_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.third_party_logistics. Business justification: Customs entries are filed by customs brokers, which are a type of 3PL service provider. N:1 relationship. Normalizes customs_broker_name and customs_broker_license_number to FK. The third_party_logist']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: Customs entries in apparel fashion must be attributable to the vendor for duty chargeback billing, vendor compliance reporting, and importer-of-record reconciliation. Finance teams run vendor-level du']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Customs entries cover goods produced under specific work orders. Direct link enables duty drawback claims and compliance audits tracing from production order to customs clearance — required by CBP and']
  arrival_date date [note: 'Date when the vessel or aircraft arrived at the U.S. port. Establishes the timeline for entry filing and duty payment deadlines.']
  bond_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Face value of the customs bond in U.S. dollars. For single transaction bonds, typically 2-3 times the estimated duties. For continuous bonds, minimum $50,000 or 10% of duties paid in prior year, whichever is greater.']
  bond_type varchar [note: 'Type of customs bond securing the entry. Single: covers one transaction; Continuous: covers all transactions for one year (minimum $50,000); None: no bond required (e.g., informal entries under $2,500). Bonds guarantee payment of duties and compliance with regulations.']
  certificate_of_origin_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the certificate of origin document supporting the claimed country of origin and trade preference eligibility. Required for many FTA and GSP claims. Format varies by issuing authority.']
  clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the customs entry. Filed: submitted to CBP; Accepted: entry accepted for processing; Hold: flagged for review; Exam: selected for physical or documentary examination; Released: goods authorized for delivery; Liquidated: final duty determination made; Cancelled: entry voided. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: filed|accepted|hold|exam|released|liquidated|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  commercial_invoice_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the commercial invoice from the foreign seller. Primary document supporting the declared value and description of goods. Required for all formal entries.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code identifying where the goods were manufactured, produced, or grown. Determines applicable duty rates and eligibility for trade preference programs. Critical for FTC country-of-origin labeling compliance for apparel.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this customs entry record was first created in the system. Represents initial data capture, not the customs filing date.']
  declared_value_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total declared customs value of the imported goods in U.S. dollars. Represents the transaction value (price paid or payable) adjusted for international freight, insurance, and other costs as required by customs valuation rules. Basis for duty calculation.']
  duty_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total customs duty assessed on the entry in U.S. dollars. Calculated by applying the applicable tariff rate to the dutiable value. May be reduced or eliminated under trade preference programs.']
  entry_filing_date date [note: 'Date when the customs entry was officially filed with the customs authority. Critical for calculating duty payment deadlines and compliance with entry filing timelines.']
  entry_number varchar [note: 'Official customs entry number assigned by customs authority. Format: XXX-NNNNNNN-N (port code, sequential number, check digit). Required for all formal entries and used as the primary reference for customs clearance tracking and audit.']
  entry_summary_date date [note: 'Date when the entry summary (CBP Form 7501) was filed with CBP. Must be filed within 10 working days of entry release. Contains final classification, valuation, and duty calculation.']
  entry_type varchar [note: 'Classification of customs entry based on value threshold and processing requirements. Formal entries required for shipments over $2,500 USD; informal for lower values; in-bond for goods in transit; warehouse for deferred duty payment; FTZ (Free Trade Zone) for goods entering foreign trade zones.']
  exam_type varchar [note: 'Type of physical or documentary examination conducted by CBP or Partner Government Agency. Intensive: full container exam; Tailored: targeted inspection; Compliance: verification of entry accuracy; PGA: inspection by FDA, USDA, or other agency; None: no exam required.']
  ftz_admission_number varchar [note: 'Admission number assigned when goods are admitted into a Foreign Trade Zone. Allows duty deferral or elimination. Null if goods did not enter an FTZ.']
  harbor_maintenance_fee_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee assessed at 0.125% of cargo value for commercial cargo shipped through identified U.S. ports. Used to fund harbor maintenance and dredging. Not applicable to air shipments.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'CBP-assigned code indicating the reason for entry hold or exam. Examples: 01 (intensive exam), 02 (tailored exam), 03 (compliance measurement), PGA (Partner Government Agency hold). Null if no hold.']
  importer_of_record_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the entity responsible for ensuring imported goods comply with all laws and regulations and for paying duties, taxes, and fees. The IOR is legally liable for the accuracy of the customs declaration.']
  importer_of_record_number varchar [note: 'IRS-assigned Employer Identification Number (EIN) or CBP-assigned importer number used to identify the importer of record. Format: XX-NNNNNNN.']
  isf_filing_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the ISF (10+2) filing submitted to CBP at least 24 hours before ocean cargo is loaded at foreign port. Required for all ocean shipments to the U.S. Links security data to the entry.']
  line_item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of line items (distinct HS code classifications) on this customs entry. Each line represents a different product classification with its own duty rate and origin.']
  liquidation_date date [note: 'Date when CBP made the final determination of duties, taxes, and fees owed. Liquidation typically occurs within 314 days of entry but may be extended. After liquidation, the entry is considered final unless protested.']
  merchandise_processing_fee_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'CBP-assessed fee for processing formal entries. Calculated as 0.3464% of entered value with minimum $27.75 and maximum $538.40 per entry (rates subject to periodic adjustment). Informal entries have fixed MPF rates.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this customs entry record was last updated. Tracks changes to entry status, liquidation, or other attributes throughout the entry lifecycle.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which estimated duties, taxes, and fees must be paid to CBP. Typically 10 working days after entry release for non-periodic monthly statement filers. Late payment incurs interest charges.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to pay customs duties and fees. ACH (Automated Clearing House): electronic funds transfer; Check: paper check; Statement: periodic monthly statement for approved importers; Bond: continuous customs bond for deferred payment; Other: alternative payment arrangements.']
  port_of_entry_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit Schedule D port code identifying the U.S. port where goods are entered for customs clearance. Examples: 2704 (Los Angeles/Long Beach), 4601 (New York/Newark), 5301 (Miami).']
  port_of_unlading_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit Schedule D port code identifying where goods were physically unloaded from the arriving vessel or aircraft. May differ from port of entry for in-bond shipments.']
  protest_filed_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal protest has been filed challenging CBP\'s liquidation decision. Protests must be filed within 180 days of liquidation. True if protest filed; False if no protest.']
  reconciliation_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entry is filed under CBP\'s reconciliation program, which allows importers to file entries with estimated data and reconcile with final data within 15 months. True if reconciliation entry; False if standard entry.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when CBP authorized release of the goods from customs custody, allowing delivery to the importer. Critical for calculating dwell time and measuring OTIF (On Time In Full) performance against carrier and 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) SLAs (Service Level Agreements).']
  surety_code varchar [note: 'Three-digit code identifying the surety company that issued the customs bond. Surety must be on the Treasury Department\'s list of approved sureties.']
  total_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of goods covered by this entry, summed across all line items. Unit of measure varies by commodity (pieces, dozens, kilograms, square meters, etc.).']
  total_weight_kg decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment in kilograms, including packaging. Used for freight calculation and certain duty assessments. Required for all entries.']
  trade_program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the trade preference program under which duty reduction or elimination is claimed. GSP (Generalized System of Preferences), USMCA (United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement), FTA (Free Trade Agreement), AGOA (African Growth and Opportunity Act), CBI (Caribbean Basin Initiative), ATPA (Andean Trade Preference Act), NONE (no preference claimed). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GSP|USMCA|FTA|AGOA|CBI|ATPA|NONE — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation used to bring goods into the U.S. Ocean: vessel; Air: aircraft; Truck: highway; Rail: railroad; Courier: express consignment. Affects processing requirements and fee calculations.']
  voyage_flight_number varchar [note: 'Vessel voyage number or aircraft flight number on which the goods arrived. Links the entry to the specific conveyance manifest filed by the carrier.']

  Note: 'Manages customs clearance declarations for apparel and footwear imports and exports. Stores entry type (formal, informal, in-bond), entry number, filing date, port of entry, importer of record, customs broker, declared value, duty amount, applicable trade program (GSP, FTA), HS code references, country of origin, and clearance status. Tracks holds, exams, and release dates. Manages associated trade compliance documents including certificates of origin, commercial invoices, and ISF (10+2) filings as structured attributes. Critical for FTC labeling compliance and trade compliance reporting across all sourcing countries.'
}

Table logistics.hs_code {
  hs_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the HS code record. Primary key.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: HS codes determine duty type and are mapped to GL accounts for duty expense classification — e.g., Section 301 surcharges post to a different GL than standard MFN duties. Apparel customs teams maintai']
  antidumping_duty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this HS code is subject to antidumping duties for imports from specific countries due to unfair pricing practices.']
  chapter varchar [note: 'The two-digit chapter number of the HS code representing the broad product category (e.g., Chapter 61 for knitted apparel, Chapter 62 for woven apparel, Chapter 64 for footwear).']
