  countervailing_duty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this HS code is subject to countervailing duties for imports from specific countries due to foreign government subsidies.']
  cpsc_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products under this HS code are subject to CPSC safety standards and testing requirements (e.g., flammability standards for children\'s sleepwear, lead content limits).']
  cpsc_regulation_reference varchar [note: 'Specific CPSC regulation citation applicable to this HS code (e.g., 16 CFR 1610 for flammability, 16 CFR 1501 for small parts). Null if not CPSC regulated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this HS code record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  duty_rate_unit varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the duty rate is ad valorem (percentage of value), specific (per unit/weight), or compound (combination of both).']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this HS code classification and associated duty rates become effective. Critical for time-based duty calculations and compliance.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this HS code classification and duty rates remain valid. Null for currently active codes. Used to manage HS code changes during WCO amendment cycles.']
  environmental_certification_required varchar [note: 'Environmental or sustainability certifications required or recommended for products under this HS code (e.g., OEKO-TEX Standard 100, GOTS, BCI). Null if no specific requirements.']
  ftc_labeling_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products under this HS code require FTC-mandated fiber content labeling, country of origin marking, or care labeling per Textile and Wool Acts.']
  ftc_labeling_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific FTC labeling requirements for this HS code including fiber content disclosure, country of origin, manufacturer identification, and care instructions. Null if not required.']
  general_duty_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The standard Most Favored Nation (MFN) duty rate expressed as a percentage or specific rate applicable to imports from countries without preferential trade agreements. Used for Landed Duty Paid (LDP) calculations.']
  gsp_beneficiary_countries varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3-letter country codes eligible for GSP treatment for this HS code. Null if GSP not applicable.']
  gsp_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this HS code is eligible for duty-free treatment under the US Generalized System of Preferences program when imported from designated beneficiary developing countries.']
  heading varchar [note: 'The four-digit heading number providing a more specific product classification within the chapter (e.g., 6109 for T-shirts, singlets, and other vests).']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'The full Harmonized System tariff classification code (6 to 10 digits) used for international trade classification of apparel, footwear, and accessories. This code determines duty rates, trade statistics, and regulatory requirements.']
  hs_code_description varchar [note: 'Full official description of the product classification including material composition, construction method, and intended use as defined by the Harmonized System nomenclature.']
  hs_code_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this HS code. Active codes are used for current classifications. Deprecated codes have been replaced by new codes in HS updates. Superseded codes reference the replacement code. Pending codes are scheduled for future activation.']
  hs_version varchar [note: 'The version year of the Harmonized System nomenclature (e.g., HS2017, HS2022) under which this code is classified. WCO updates the HS every 5 years.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this HS code record was last modified. Updated whenever duty rates, descriptions, or regulatory flags change. Critical for maintaining accurate duty calculations.']
  national_extension varchar [note: 'Additional digits beyond the 6-digit subheading added by individual countries for more granular classification (e.g., US uses 10-digit HTS codes). May be null for base 6-digit codes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, guidance, or special instructions for proper classification under this HS code including exclusions, inclusions, and edge cases. Used by customs brokers and compliance teams.']
  origin_criteria varchar [note: 'Rules of origin criteria that must be met for products under this HS code to qualify for preferential duty treatment (e.g., tariff shift rules, regional value content thresholds, substantial transformation tests).']
  preferential_duty_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Reduced duty rate available under preferential trade agreements such as USMCA, GSP, or bilateral free trade agreements. Null if no preferential rate applies.']
  restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this HS code is subject to import or export restrictions, quotas, licensing requirements, or prohibitions by customs authorities.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Type of restriction or special requirement applicable to this HS code. Null if restricted_flag is false.']
  section_301_duty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this HS code is subject to additional Section 301 tariffs imposed on imports from specific countries (e.g., China tariffs).']
  section_301_duty_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Additional duty rate percentage imposed under Section 301 trade actions. Null if not subject to Section 301 duties.']
  short_description varchar [note: 'Abbreviated description of the HS code for operational use in warehouse management, shipping documents, and internal systems.']
  subheading varchar [note: 'The six-digit subheading providing internationally standardized product classification. This is the minimum level required for international trade.']
  superseded_by_hs_code varchar [note: 'The new HS code that replaces this code when status is superseded. Used to maintain continuity during HS version transitions. Null for active codes.']
  textile_category varchar [note: 'Textile category classification used for quota administration and trade monitoring (historically used for Multi-Fiber Arrangement, still relevant for certain trade agreements).']
  trade_agreement varchar [note: 'Name of the trade agreement or preferential program under which the preferential duty rate applies (e.g., USMCA, GSP, CAFTA-DR, EU-FTA). Null if general duty rate applies.']
  unit_of_quantity varchar [note: 'The unit of measure required for statistical reporting and quota tracking for this HS code (e.g., dozens for shirts, pairs for footwear, kilograms for fabric).']

  Note: 'Reference master for Harmonized System (HS) tariff classification codes applicable to apparel, footwear, and accessories. Stores HS code, chapter, heading, subheading, full description, applicable duty rate, preferential duty rates by trade agreement, restricted/prohibited flag, CPSC safety classification, FTC fiber content labeling requirement flag, and effective date range. Enables accurate duty calculation, GSP utilization, and customs entry filing across all import/export lanes.'
}

Table logistics.duty_calculation {
  duty_calculation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the duty calculation record. Primary key for the duty calculation transaction.']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bom. Business justification: Landed cost analysis and customs audit require tracing each duty calculation back to the BOM that defined material composition, country of origin, and HS codes. Apparel costing teams routinely reconci']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Duty costs are allocated to cost centers for budgeting and variance analysis. Cost center managers track landed cost components against budget to manage total product acquisition costs by department.']
  customs_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.customs_entry. Business justification: Duty calculations are performed for specific customs entries. N:1 relationship (multiple duty calculations can exist per customs entry for different line items or recalculations). Normalizes customs_e']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Landed cost components (duty, freight, insurance) post to specific GL accounts. Month-end close requires duty calculations to post to correct GL accounts for COGS and inventory valuation accuracy.']
  hs_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.hs_code. Business justification: Duty calculations use HS codes to determine duty rates. N:1 relationship to hs_code master table. Normalizes hs_code string and duty_rate_percentage which can be retrieved from hs_code.general_duty_ra']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Duty costs are allocated to profit centers for landed cost and product-line P&L reporting. Apparel brands allocate import duties by brand/channel profit center to compute accurate landed cost per unit']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inbound shipment associated with this duty calculation. Links to logistics shipment tracking in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: SKU-level landed cost reporting and margin analysis require linking each duty calculation to the specific SKU imported. Apparel finance teams calculate IMU and LDP cost at SKU level; without this FK, ']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: Duty calculations are performed per sourcing PO to determine landed costs, allocate duty to specific SKU receipts, and reconcile with PO-level FOB values in apparel costing.']
  sourcing_purchase_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order_line. Business justification: Apparel duty calculations are performed at line level — each PO line has its own HS code, COO, and quantity. Line-level FK enables CBP entry line reconciliation, FTA/GSP claims per SKU, and landed cos']
  supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: CBP duty assessment in apparel requires factory-level country-of-origin determination — GSP eligibility, Section 301 rates, and antidumping duties are factory-specific. Duty calculations must referenc']
  third_party_logistics_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the 3PL partner managing the logistics and customs clearance for this shipment.']
  vendor_cost_quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.vendor_cost_quote. Business justification: Apparel landed cost management requires reconciling actual duty calculations against the vendor cost quote\'s duty_cost field. This FK enables LDP variance reporting and quote accuracy audits — a stand']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed customs broker who prepared and submitted the customs entry and duty calculation.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Landed duty paid (LDP) cost calculations in apparel are tracked per work order for margin analysis and costing. Finance and sourcing teams need duty_calculation linked to work_order to report total la']
  anti_dumping_duty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional duty imposed to offset unfair pricing practices when goods are sold below fair market value. Applied on top of standard duties.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the duty calculation was approved for submission. Part of audit trail for compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  base_duty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard import duty calculated by applying the duty rate percentage to the CIF value, before any trade preferences or additional duties.']
  calculation_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the duty calculation, used for reference in customs documentation and financial reporting. Format: DC-XXXXXXXXXX.']
  calculation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the duty calculation. Tracks progression from initial calculation through customs approval and payment reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|calculated|submitted|approved|rejected|paid|reconciled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the duty calculation was performed. Critical for audit trail and compliance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  cif_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total customs value including FOB value, freight, and insurance. The dutiable value upon which import duties and taxes are calculated.']
  countervailing_duty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional duty imposed to offset foreign government subsidies that unfairly benefit exporters. Applied on top of standard duties.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the goods were manufactured or produced. Determines eligibility for trade preferences and duty rates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this duty calculation record was first created in the database. Part of audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts in this calculation (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All amounts are expressed in this currency.']
  destination_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating the country where goods are being imported. Determines applicable customs regulations and duty rates.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert foreign currency amounts to the reporting currency. Used when FOB value is in a different currency than destination.']
  excise_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Special excise taxes on specific product categories (e.g., luxury goods, alcohol, tobacco). Applied based on product classification.']
  fob_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value of goods at the point of shipment, excluding freight and insurance. Base value for duty calculation before transportation costs.']
  freight_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transportation cost from origin to destination port. Added to FOB value to calculate CIF value for duty assessment.']
  fta_claimed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether preferential duty treatment under a Free Trade Agreement was claimed. FTAs provide reduced or zero duty rates for qualifying goods.']
  fta_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific Free Trade Agreement under which preferential treatment is claimed (e.g., USMCA, CAFTA-DR, Korea FTA).']
  fta_reduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Duty reduction amount granted under FTA preferential treatment. Reduces the base duty amount when FTA eligibility is confirmed.']
  gsp_claimed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GSP preferential duty treatment was claimed for this shipment. GSP provides duty-free treatment for eligible products from developing countries.']
  gsp_reduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Duty reduction amount granted under GSP preferential treatment. Reduces the base duty amount when GSP eligibility is confirmed.']
  hmf_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'U.S. fee assessed on cargo shipped through U.S. ports to fund harbor maintenance. Calculated as a percentage of cargo value.']
  insurance_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Insurance premium for goods in transit. Added to FOB and freight to calculate CIF value for customs valuation.']
  ldp_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total landed cost including CIF value, all duties, taxes, and fees. Represents the complete cost to bring goods into the destination country.']
  ldp_unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Per-unit landed cost calculated by dividing LDP total amount by quantity. Used for COGS calculation and margin analysis in SAP FI/CO.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this duty calculation record was last updated. Tracks data lineage and change history. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  mpf_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'U.S. Customs fee for processing imported merchandise. Calculated as a percentage of shipment value with minimum and maximum caps.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special circumstances, assumptions, or clarifications related to the duty calculation. Used for audit trail.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Actual date when duty and tax payment was made to customs authority. Used for reconciliation and cash flow tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which duty and tax payment must be made to customs authority to avoid penalties. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official payment confirmation or transaction reference number from customs authority or payment processor.']
  port_of_entry varchar [note: 'Official customs port or border crossing where goods entered the destination country. Impacts processing times and local regulations.']
  quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total quantity of units in the shipment for which duty is calculated. Used to derive per-unit landed costs.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of reconciliation between calculated duty amounts and actual amounts paid or invoiced by customs. Tracks variance resolution.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the duty calculation was submitted to customs authorities. Critical for compliance and SLA tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  total_duty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all duty components including base duty, anti-dumping, countervailing duties, less any GSP or FTA reductions. Excludes taxes and fees.']
  total_fees_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all customs processing fees including MPF, HMF, and other regulatory fees.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all tax components including VAT, excise taxes, and other applicable import taxes.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity field. Common values: EA (each), DZ (dozen), CS (case), KG (kilogram), LB (pound), M (meter), YD (yard). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|DZ|CS|KG|LB|M|YD — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between calculated duty amount and actual amount assessed by customs. Positive indicates underpayment, negative indicates overpayment.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any variance between calculated and actual duty amounts. Used for process improvement and dispute resolution.']
  vat_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Import VAT or sales tax assessed on the total landed value including duties. Applicable in jurisdictions with VAT systems.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of landed duty paid (LDP) calculations performed for apparel purchase orders and shipments. Captures FOB value, freight cost, insurance, CIF value, applicable HS code, duty rate applied, GSP or FTA preference claimed, anti-dumping/countervailing duty amounts, MPF, HMF, total duty and tax, calculated LDP unit cost, and calculation timestamp. Feeds COGS and financial planning in Anaplan and SAP FI/CO for accurate margin management.'
}

Table logistics.carrier {
  carrier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the transportation carrier. Primary key for the carrier master record.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Carriers are mapped to freight expense GL accounts for automated AP invoice coding. Apparel AP teams configure carrier-to-GL mappings so freight invoices are automatically posted to the correct expens']
  api_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier provides API integration for real-time tracking, rate quotes, and booking automation (True/False).']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the transportation carrier or logistics service provider (e.g., Maersk Line, FedEx Corporation, United Parcel Service).']
  carrier_status varchar [note: 'Current operational and business relationship status of the carrier. Preferred carriers receive priority routing; approved carriers meet minimum standards; probation indicates performance issues under review; suspended carriers are temporarily blocked; inactive carriers are no longer used.']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the carrier based on transportation mode: ocean shipping line, air freight carrier, ground less-than-truckload (LTL), ground full-truckload (FTL), rail operator, last-mile courier, third-party logistics provider (3PL), or freight forwarder. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ocean|air|ground_ltl|ground_ftl|rail|courier|3pl|freight_forwarder — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current carrier contract or service agreement became effective.']
  contract_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current carrier contract or service agreement expires. Null for open-ended or evergreen agreements.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the master service agreement or transportation contract governing the business relationship with this carrier.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for invoicing and payment transactions with this carrier (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  damage_claim_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of shipments handled by this carrier that resulted in damage claims. Lower rates indicate better handling quality.']
  dot_number varchar [note: 'US Department of Transportation identification number assigned to commercial motor carriers. Required for regulatory compliance and safety tracking.']
  edi_capability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier supports EDI for automated booking, shipment status updates, and invoicing (True/False).']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Description of the geographic regions, countries, or trade lanes served by this carrier (e.g., North America, Trans-Pacific, Europe-Asia, Last-Mile USA).']
  headquarters_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the carriers headquarters street address.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City where the carriers headquarters is located.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the carriers headquarters location (e.g., USA, CHN, DEU).']
  headquarters_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the carriers headquarters address.']
  headquarters_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region where the carriers headquarters is located.']
  iata_code varchar [note: 'Two or three-character airline designator code assigned by IATA for air freight carriers.']
  insurance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number of the carriers cargo insurance or liability insurance policy on file. Required for risk management and compliance.']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum liability coverage amount provided by the carriers insurance policy, typically in USD. Used to assess risk exposure for high-value shipments.']
  insurance_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the carriers current insurance certificate. Monitored to ensure continuous coverage.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier is currently active and available for new shipment bookings (True) or inactive (False).']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal performance review or scorecard evaluation of the carrier.']
  mc_number varchar [note: 'Motor Carrier operating authority number issued by FMCSA for interstate commerce in the United States.']
  on_time_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage score (0.00 to 100.00) representing the carriers on-time delivery performance based on historical shipment data. Used for carrier selection and routing optimization.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier was first onboarded and approved for use in the logistics network.']
  otif_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage score (0.00 to 100.00) representing the carriers performance in delivering shipments both on time and in full (complete quantity, undamaged). Key performance indicator for carrier evaluation.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated with the carrier (e.g., Net 30, Net 45, Net 60, prepaid, upon delivery). Governs accounts payable processing.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary business contact at the carrier for operational communication, booking confirmations, and issue resolution.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact or account manager at the carrier organization.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number of the carrier contact for urgent operational matters and escalations.']
  scac_code varchar [note: 'Unique two-to-four-letter code used to identify transportation companies in North America. Required for EDI transactions and customs documentation.']
  service_mode varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of service modes offered by the carrier (e.g., express, standard, economy, next-day, two-day, international, domestic, white-glove).']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or trade name of the carrier used in operational systems and documentation (e.g., Maersk, FedEx, UPS, DHL).']
  sla_target_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days committed by the carrier for delivery under the service level agreement. Used for shipment planning and performance measurement.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental or sustainability certifications held by the carrier (e.g., SmartWay, ISO 14001, Carbon Neutral Certified). Supports corporate sustainability reporting.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (TIN, EIN, VAT number) of the carrier entity. Used for invoicing, tax reporting, and compliance.']
  tracking_url_template varchar [note: 'URL template for shipment tracking on the carriers website. Placeholder tokens (e.g., {tracking_number}) are replaced with actual shipment identifiers.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier record was last modified or updated.']

  Note: 'Master record for all transportation carriers engaged by the apparel business — ocean shipping lines (Maersk, MSC, CMA CGM), air freight carriers, domestic LTL/FTL truckers, last-mile couriers (FedEx, UPS, DHL), and rail operators. Stores SCAC code, DOT number, carrier name, carrier type, service modes, contract reference, preferred/approved/probation status, insurance certificate, on-time performance score, and active status. SSOT for carrier identity referenced by freight bookings, shipments, routing guides, SLAs, and freight invoices.'
}

Table logistics.routing_guide {
  routing_guide_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the routing guide rule. Primary key.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.contract. Business justification: Routing guides define approved carrier assignments and are executed under specific carrier contracts. The routing_guide has a carrier_contract_number string field that directly maps to the contract ma']
  destination_facility_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination distribution center, warehouse, or retail store receiving the shipment.']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transportation lane this routing guide applies to, linking origin-destination pairs.']
  origin_production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_factory. Business justification: Routing guides in apparel specify factory-specific shipping instructions — carrier, mode, and lane from a specific origin factory to destination DC. Vendor compliance programs require factory-level ro']
  primary_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preferred carrier assigned to this lane as the first choice for transportation.']
  primary_routing_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific origin facility, warehouse, or manufacturing plant from which shipments originate.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Seasonal routing guide activation: routing guides in apparel are published per season to reflect seasonal lane rates, carrier contracts, and delivery windows. routing_guide.season_code is a denormaliz']
  tertiary_routing_last_mile_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier responsible for final delivery to the destination, used for multi-leg shipments.']
  third_party_logistics_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the 3PL partner managing logistics execution for this routing guide, if applicable.']
  compliance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this routing guide has mandatory compliance requirements such as customs clearance, HS code validation, or GSP utilization.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the cost per unit amount.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'Standard transportation cost per unit (carton, pallet, or weight unit) for this routing guide, used for cost control and budgeting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this routing guide record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this routing guide rule is no longer valid, allowing for seasonal or contract-based routing changes.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this routing guide rule becomes active and applicable for shipment planning.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Payment and responsibility terms for freight charges, defining who pays transportation costs and when ownership transfers.']
  gsp_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this routing guide supports shipments eligible for GSP duty-free treatment under trade preference programs.']
  guide_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the routing guide rule, used for external reference and operational lookup.']
  guide_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the routing guide rule for business user identification.']
  hs_code_validation_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether shipments using this routing guide must have validated HS codes for customs clearance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this routing guide record.']
  ldp_calculation_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether landed cost calculations including duties, taxes, and fees must be computed for this routing guide.']
  max_transit_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of calendar days allowed for shipment transit from origin to destination under this routing guide.']
  minimum_shipment_volume decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum shipment volume or weight required to utilize this routing guide, enforcing carrier minimum order quantity (MOQ) requirements.']
  mode_of_transport varchar [note: 'Primary transportation method to be used for shipments following this routing guide.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking to determine routing guide selection order when multiple guides match the same lane criteria (lower number = higher priority).']
  routing_guide_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the routing guide rule, controlling whether it is available for shipment assignment.']
  routing_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional routing instructions, special handling requirements, or operational notes for logistics teams.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Transportation service tier defining speed and priority of delivery for this routing guide.']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of shipment flow direction and purpose within the supply chain network.']
  sla_target_otif_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage for on-time and in-full delivery performance that carriers must meet under this routing guide.']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for minimum shipment volume (cartons, pallets, kilograms, pounds, cubic meters, cubic feet).']

  Note: 'Defines the approved transportation routing rules and carrier assignments for apparel shipments by lane, origin-destination pair, shipment type, and season. Captures lane origin region, destination DC or store, preferred carrier, backup carrier, service level (standard, expedite, direct), mode of transport, maximum transit days, freight terms (FOB, prepaid, collect), effective date range, and compliance requirement flag. Used by sourcing and logistics teams to enforce carrier compliance and cost controls.'
}

Table logistics.distribution_center {
  distribution_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution center. Primary key for the distribution center entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each DC operates as a cost center for expense tracking. DC operating expenses (labor, utilities, rent, equipment) are tracked via cost center for budget variance analysis and cost-per-unit calculation']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Distribution centers incur facility operating costs (rent, utilities, labor) posted to specific GL accounts. Apparel DC finance teams map each DC to its primary GL account for facility cost posting, l']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: DCs may serve specific channels tracked as profit centers. Channel-specific DCs (wholesale vs DTC vs outlet) roll up operating costs to profit center P&L for true channel profitability analysis.']
  third_party_logistics_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.third_party_logistics. Business justification: Distribution centers are operated by 3PL providers. N:1 relationship (many DCs can be operated by one 3PL). Normalizes three_pl_provider_name string to FK for 3PL performance tracking and contract man']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the distribution center facility. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or unit information. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Degree of warehouse automation deployed at the distribution center. Manual: labor-intensive operations. Semi-automated: conveyor systems and sortation. Fully automated: automated storage and retrieval systems (AS/RS). Robotic: autonomous mobile robots and advanced automation.']
  bulk_storage_capacity_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Square footage allocated to bulk pallet storage zone for high-volume inventory.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the distribution center is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  cold_storage_capacity_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Square footage allocated to temperature-controlled storage for specialty materials or products requiring climate control.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the distribution center location (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX, GBR, DEU).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the distribution center record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data governance.']
  customs_bonded_warehouse_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distribution center is a customs bonded warehouse authorized to store imported goods before duties are paid. Critical for international trade and Landed Duty Paid (LDP) calculations.']
  dc_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the distribution center used across operational systems and documentation. Externally-known unique code for facility identification.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the distribution center was decommissioned and ceased operations. Null for active facilities.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email contact for distribution center operations and coordination. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the distribution center or fulfillment facility.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution center by operational model and purpose. Owned DC: company-owned distribution center. 3PL-operated: Third-Party Logistics (3PL) operated facility. Cross-dock: transshipment facility. Returns center: dedicated returns processing. Forward stocking location: regional inventory buffer. Fulfillment center: e-commerce order fulfillment.']
  go_live_date date [note: 'Date when the distribution center became operational and began processing inventory and shipments.']
  hanging_storage_capacity_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Square footage allocated to hanging garment storage on rails for apparel that requires hanging to maintain quality.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the distribution center record was last updated. Audit trail for data governance and change tracking.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the distribution center for routing, distance calculations, and network optimization.']
  lease_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the facility lease expires for leased distribution centers. Null for owned facilities. Used for real estate planning and renewal negotiations.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the distribution center for routing, distance calculations, and network optimization.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the distribution center (e.g., 24/7, Mon-Fri 6AM-6PM, Mon-Sat 8AM-8PM). Used for scheduling and service level planning.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the distribution center in the logistics network lifecycle.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the distribution center facility. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  pick_zone_capacity_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Square footage allocated to active picking zones for order fulfillment operations.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the distribution center address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  rfid_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distribution center is equipped with Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology for automated inventory tracking and visibility.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the distribution center location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  supports_dtc_channel boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution center supports Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) e-commerce order fulfillment.']
  supports_outlet_channel boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution center supports outlet store fulfillment for clearance and off-season merchandise.']
  supports_retail_replenishment_channel boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution center supports replenishment shipments to company-owned retail stores.']
  supports_wholesale_channel boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution center supports wholesale distribution to retail partners and department stores.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental or sustainability certifications held by the facility (e.g., LEED, ISO 14001, Carbon Neutral). Supports corporate sustainability reporting and compliance.']
  throughput_capacity_units_per_day varchar [note: 'Maximum daily processing capacity measured in units that can be received, stored, picked, packed, and shipped. Used for capacity planning and load balancing.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the distribution center location used for scheduling, cutoff times, and operational planning (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London).']
  total_square_footage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total facility area in square feet including warehouse, office, and support spaces. Used for capacity planning and cost allocation.']
  wms_instance_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the Manhattan Associates WMS instance managing this distribution center. Links facility to specific WMS deployment.']

  Note: 'Master record for all distribution centers, fulfillment centers, cross-dock facilities, and returns processing centers in the apparel logistics network. Captures DC code, facility name, full address, facility type (owned DC, 3PL-operated, cross-dock, returns center, forward stocking location), total square footage, storage capacity by zone (bulk, pick, hanging, cold), throughput capacity (units/day), WMS instance (Manhattan Associates), operating hours, supported channels (wholesale, DTC, retail replenishment, outlet), and active status. SSOT for facility identity referenced by inventory, shipment, and allocation processes.'
}

Table logistics.third_party_logistics {
  third_party_logistics_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for third_party_logistics']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: 3PL costs are tracked against cost centers for warehousing and fulfillment budget management. Apparel companies with outsourced DC operations assign each 3PL provider to a cost center for spend monito']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: 3PL providers are mapped to GL accounts for warehousing and fulfillment expense posting. Apparel AP teams configure 3PL-to-GL mappings so that 3PL invoices are automatically coded to the correct expen']
  company_name varchar [note: 'Legal registered name of the third-party logistics provider company.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the master service agreement or contract governing the relationship with this 3PL partner.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this 3PL partner record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for financial transactions with this 3PL partner (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customs_broker_license_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued license number authorizing the 3PL partner to perform customs brokerage services. Applicable only when service_type includes customs brokerage.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet DUNS number uniquely identifying the 3PL partner organization for business credit and verification purposes.']
  edi_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the 3PL partner supports EDI transaction sets for automated data exchange (warehouse shipping orders, shipment status, inventory advice).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the 3PL partnership relationship ended or is scheduled to end. Null for ongoing relationships.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the 3PL partnership relationship became or will become effective and operational.']
  freight_forwarder_code varchar [note: 'Standard Carrier Alpha Code (SCAC) or International Air Transport Association (IATA) code for the 3PL partner when acting as a freight forwarder.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes representing the regions where this 3PL partner operates and provides services (e.g., USA,CAN,MEX for North America coverage).']
  headquarters_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address for the 3PL partner\'s headquarters or primary business office.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City where the 3PL partner\'s headquarters is located.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the 3PL partner\'s headquarters location (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  headquarters_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the 3PL partner\'s headquarters location.']
  headquarters_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the 3PL partner\'s headquarters is located.']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum liability insurance coverage amount in USD that the 3PL partner maintains for cargo loss, damage, or liability claims.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent operational or compliance audit conducted on this 3PL partner\'s facilities and processes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this 3PL partner record was last updated or modified.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next business review or performance evaluation of this 3PL partnership.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional operational notes, special handling instructions, or partnership-specific details about the 3PL provider.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the 3PL partner relationship. Prospective indicates evaluation phase, onboarding indicates setup in progress, active indicates operational, suspended indicates temporary hold, terminated indicates contract ended, and inactive indicates no longer used.']
  partner_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the 3PL partner, used in EDI transactions, WMS integrations, and operational communications.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms in days for invoices from this 3PL partner (e.g., 30, 45, 60 days net).']
  performance_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite performance score (0.00 to 100.00) calculated from key metrics including On-Time In-Full (OTIF) delivery rate, order accuracy, damage rate, and responsiveness. Updated quarterly based on operational KPIs.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary business contact at the 3PL partner for operational communications and escalations.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact or account manager at the 3PL partner organization.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary business contact at the 3PL partner for urgent operational matters.']
  quality_certification varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of quality management certifications held by the 3PL partner (e.g., ISO 9001, WRAP, FLA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ISO_9001|ISO_14001|ISO_45001|WRAP|FLA|CTPAT|AEO|TAPA — promote to reference product]']
  returns_processing_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the 3PL partner has capabilities to process customer returns, including inspection, restocking, and disposition management.']
  rfid_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the 3PL partner\'s facilities are equipped with RFID technology for automated inventory tracking and shipment verification.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Primary category of logistics services provided by the 3PL partner. Warehousing covers storage operations, fulfillment includes pick-pack-ship, freight forwarding manages international shipping, customs brokerage handles clearance and compliance, last-mile delivery covers final delivery to consumer, and integrated provides multiple service types.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Contractual service level tier defining performance commitments, response times, and quality standards for this 3PL partnership.']
  sustainability_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the 3PL partner holds recognized sustainability or environmental certifications (e.g., ISO 14001, SmartWay, LEED).']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (EIN, VAT, GST) for the 3PL partner used for tax reporting and compliance.']
  total_warehouse_sqft decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total warehouse storage capacity in square feet across all facilities operated by this 3PL partner.']
  vendor_managed_inventory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the 3PL partner participates in vendor-managed inventory programs where they monitor and replenish stock levels autonomously.']
  warehouse_count varchar [note: 'Total number of warehouse or distribution center facilities operated by this 3PL partner within their coverage regions.']
  wms_integration_type varchar [note: 'Technical integration method used to connect the 3PL partner\'s WMS with internal systems. API indicates real-time REST/SOAP integration, EDI 940/945 uses standard warehouse transaction sets, flat file uses CSV/XML batch files, SFTP uses secure file transfer, and none indicates manual processes.']

  Note: 'Master record for all third-party logistics (3PL) providers engaged to operate warehousing, fulfillment, transportation, or customs brokerage services for the apparel business. Stores 3PL partner identifier, company name, service type (warehousing, fulfillment, freight forwarding, customs brokerage, last-mile delivery), contract reference, geographic coverage regions, EDI capability flag, WMS integration type (API, EDI 940/945, flat file), SLA tier, composite performance rating, insurance coverage amount, onboarding status, and relationship effective dates. Distinct from the supplier domain which covers manufacturing vendors — this entity specifically governs logistics service providers. Note: PK uses legacy _3pl_partner_id naming convention pending infrastructure-level schema generation fix.'
}

Table logistics.sla_agreement {
  sla_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service level agreement record.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.contract. Business justification: SLA agreements are typically embedded within or referenced by formal logistics contracts. The sla_agreement has a contract_reference_number string field that is a natural candidate for normalization t']
  distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.distribution_center. Business justification: SLA agreements in apparel logistics are frequently scoped to specific distribution centers (e.g., DC-level OTIF targets, inbound receiving SLAs, outbound fulfillment SLAs). The sla_agreement has origi']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: SLA penalty and bonus amounts must be posted to GL accounts as financial line items. Apparel brands track carrier/3PL SLA breach penalties as AP debit memos and bonus credits; the GL account on the SL']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.lane. Business justification: SLA agreements in freight logistics are commonly scoped to specific trade lanes (origin-destination pairs). A lane-level SLA defines performance commitments for a specific routing corridor (e.g., Shan']
  primary_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: SLA agreements with carriers define service level commitments. N:1 relationship. Adds dedicated FK for carrier-type SLAs to eliminate silo. counterparty_id and counterparty_type remain for polymorphic']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_factory. Business justification: SLA agreements in apparel logistics are scoped to specific production factories — e.g., on-time ex-factory SLAs or inbound DC receiving SLAs tied to a factory\'s shipments. Factory-level SLA tracking a']
  third_party_logistics_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.third_party_logistics. Business justification: SLA agreements with 3PL providers define warehouse and fulfillment service level commitments. N:1 relationship. Adds dedicated FK for 3PL-type SLAs. counterparty_id remains for other counterparty type']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the logistics partner, carrier, or 3PL provider bound by this SLA.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA automatically renews at expiration unless terminated by either party.']
  bonus_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary bonus amount per measurement period when SLA performance exceeds upper threshold.']
  bonus_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for bonus amounts.']
  bonus_structure varchar [note: 'Description of financial or operational incentives awarded when SLA targets are exceeded.']
  breach_notification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated notifications are enabled when SLA thresholds are breached.']
  counterparty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service provider bound by this SLA. 3PL = Third-Party Logistics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA agreement record was first created in the system.']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the destination facility, port, or distribution center for lane-specific SLAs.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the SLA agreement becomes active and enforceable.']
  escalation_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the escalation contact for SLA performance issues.']
  escalation_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person for SLA breach escalations and dispute resolution.']
  escalation_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the escalation contact for urgent SLA breach notifications.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the SLA agreement expires or terminates. Null for open-ended agreements.']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which SLA performance is measured and reported.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Description of how the metric is calculated, including data sources and calculation logic.']
  metric_name varchar [note: 'Name of the performance metric being measured (e.g., On-Time Delivery Rate, Transit Time, Damage Rate).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal SLA review meeting or assessment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, special conditions, or exceptions related to this SLA agreement.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required to terminate or modify the SLA agreement.']
  origin_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the origin facility, port, or distribution center for lane-specific SLAs.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty amount per breach or per measurement period when SLA is not met.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for penalty amounts.']
  penalty_structure varchar [note: 'Description of financial or operational penalties applied when SLA targets are not met.']
  reporting_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system or platform used to track and report SLA performance metrics (e.g., Manhattan Associates WMS, EDI feed).']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the SLA terms and performance are formally reviewed by both parties.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the lanes, facilities, regions, or service types covered by this SLA.']
  scope_type varchar [note: 'Defines the operational scope to which this SLA applies (e.g., specific shipping lane, warehouse facility, geographic region).']
  service_mode varchar [note: 'Transportation mode covered by this SLA. LTL = Less Than Truckload, FTL = Full Truckload. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: air|ocean|ground|rail|intermodal|parcel|ltl|ftl — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SLA agreement.']
  sla_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the SLA agreement for easy identification.']
  sla_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the SLA agreement, used in contracts and communications.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Category of service level commitment being measured. OTIF = On Time In Full.']
  target_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target value (e.g., percentage for OTIF, days for transit time).']
  target_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric target or threshold value that the service provider commits to achieve.']
  threshold_lower_bound decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable performance level before penalties or escalations are triggered.']
  threshold_upper_bound decimal(10,4) [note: 'Performance level above which bonus or incentive structures may apply.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA agreement record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Defines the service level agreements governing logistics performance commitments between the apparel business and its 3PL partners, carriers, and distribution network nodes. Captures SLA type (transit time, OTIF, order accuracy, damage rate, customs clearance time), counterparty identity, applicable lane or facility scope, target metric value, measurement frequency, penalty structure, bonus structure, effective date range, and review cycle. Enables automated SLA breach detection and carrier scorecard generation.'
}

Table logistics.packing_list {
  packing_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the packing list record. Primary key.']
  customs_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.customs_entry. Business justification: A packing list is a primary supporting document for customs clearance. Each packing list is associated with a specific customs entry that declares the shipment contents to customs authorities. This FK']
  destination_facility_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center or store where goods will be received. Used for inbound planning and receiving workflow.']
  hs_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.hs_code. Business justification: Packing lists declare HS codes for the goods being shipped. N:1 relationship. Normalizes hs_code string to FK.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.lot. Business justification: Packing lists in apparel are generated at the production lot level — each lot of finished garments is packed and documented. Linking packing_list to lot enables AQL quality traceability, recall manage']
  origin_factory_supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: Packing lists show factory-of-origin for receiving verification, quality inspection routing by factory certification level, and factory performance tracking (packing accuracy, carton labeling complian']
  primary_packing_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing facility or warehouse where goods were packed. Used for sourcing analytics and quality traceability.']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.factory. Business justification: Packing lists originate from factories and require factory reference for customs country-of-origin documentation, certificate of origin validation, factory compliance audit trails, and GSP eligibility']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent shipment that this packing list documents. Links to the logistics shipment record for transportation and customs tracking.']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: Packing lists document what was shipped against sourcing PO; critical for receiving reconciliation, discrepancy management, and validating ordered vs. shipped quantities in apparel receiving operation']
  sourcing_purchase_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order_line. Business justification: Apparel packing lists are built at SKU/line level — each carton maps to specific PO lines. A direct FK to sourcing_purchase_order_line enables line-level ASN matching, DC receiving reconciliation, and']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or manufacturer who prepared this packing list. Used for vendor performance tracking and compliance auditing.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Packing lists document finished goods from specific work orders. Work order linkage enables production-to-shipment quantity reconciliation, quality traceability from production defects to shipped unit']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the packing list was approved for shipment. Used for workflow tracking and SLA monitoring.']
  carton_label_format varchar [note: 'Barcode or label standard used on cartons. GS1-128 for standard supply chain labels, SSCC for serial shipping container codes, UCC-128 for legacy format, custom for proprietary labels.']
  certificate_of_origin_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the certificate of origin document accompanying this shipment. Required for preferential trade agreement claims and customs clearance.']
  commercial_invoice_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the commercial invoice associated with this packing list. Used for customs valuation and financial reconciliation.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'Unique ISO 6346 container identification number for ocean freight shipments. Used for container tracking and customs clearance.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Type of shipping container or transport mode used. 20ft/40ft/40ft_hc for standard ocean containers, lcl for less-than-container-load, air_freight for air shipments, truck for ground transport.']
  country_of_origin_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the goods were manufactured or substantially transformed. Used for GSP eligibility and trade agreement compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this packing list record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any receiving discrepancies, damages, or quality issues identified. Used for supplier performance tracking and claims processing.']
  gsp_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the goods qualify for GSP duty-free treatment. True if eligible for preferential tariff rates under GSP program.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Incoterms 2020 rule defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and seller. Common values: FOB (Free on Board), CIF (Cost Insurance Freight), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the invoice value. Used for foreign exchange conversion and financial reporting.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this packing list record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'General free-text notes or comments about this packing list. Used for additional context, special instructions, or operational remarks.']
  packing_date date [note: 'Date when the goods were physically packed and the packing list was prepared. Used for lead time calculation and production schedule tracking.']
  packing_list_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the packing list document. Used for customs clearance, DC receiving, and cross-referencing with commercial invoice and bill of lading.']
  packing_list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the packing list document. Draft indicates in-progress, finalized means ready for shipment, submitted means sent to customs/receiver, received means acknowledged by DC, discrepancy indicates receiving variance, closed means reconciled.']
  packing_list_type varchar [note: 'Classification of packing list document. Master for container-level summary, detailed for SKU-level breakdown, consolidated for multi-PO shipments, sample for pre-production shipments.']
  packing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to pack the apparel or footwear goods. Flat pack for folded garments, hanging for garments on hangers, polybag for individual poly-bagged items, boxed for footwear in shoeboxes, mixed for combination methods.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether goods in this packing list require quality inspection upon receipt. True if inspection is mandatory before putaway.']
  quarantine_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether goods must be placed in quarantine hold upon receipt pending inspection or compliance verification. True if quarantine is required.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the packing list was acknowledged as received at the destination facility. Used for OTIF compliance and transit time calculation.']
  receiving_discrepancy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a discrepancy was identified during receiving (quantity variance, damage, quality issue). True if discrepancy exists and requires resolution.']
  rfid_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cartons in this packing list are tagged with RFID labels for automated scanning at DC receiving. True if RFID tags are applied.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number applied to the container or truck. Used for tamper detection and customs security verification.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions for special handling requirements such as fragile items, temperature control, hazmat, or security protocols. Used by warehouse and logistics teams.']
  total_carton_count varchar [note: 'Total number of cartons or packages included in this packing list. Used for receiving verification and freight planning.']
  total_cubic_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume of the shipment measured in cubic meters. Used for container utilization analysis and dimensional weight pricing.']
  total_gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment including packaging materials, measured in kilograms. Used for freight cost calculation and carrier capacity planning.']
  total_invoice_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total declared value of goods on the commercial invoice for this packing list. Used for customs valuation, duty calculation, and insurance coverage.']
  total_net_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total net weight of the goods excluding packaging materials, measured in kilograms. Used for customs valuation and duty calculation.']
  total_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets included in this packing list. Used for warehouse space planning and material handling resource allocation.']
  total_unit_quantity varchar [note: 'Total number of individual units (pieces) across all SKUs in this packing list. Used for inventory reconciliation and PO fulfillment verification.']

  Note: 'Shipping document record capturing the detailed contents of each shipment carton or container for apparel and footwear exports. Stores packing list number, associated shipment reference, carton count, total units, SKU-level breakdown (style, color, size, quantity per carton), gross weight, net weight, cubic measurement, carton dimensions, and packing method (flat pack, hanging, polybag). Required for customs clearance, DC receiving, and RFID-enabled carton scanning at Manhattan Associates WMS.'
}

Business justification: Lanes are assigned to cost centers for freight budget allocation and trade-lane-level variance analysis. Apparel supply chain finance teams budget freight by trade lane mapped to cost centers; this li']
  destination_location_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the destination location (warehouse, distribution center, store, or port) where shipments end.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Trade lanes are mapped to freight expense GL accounts for automated cost posting. Apparel logistics finance teams configure lane-to-GL mappings so that shipment costs on each lane are automatically co']
  lane_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the origin location (warehouse, distribution center, or port) where shipments begin.']
  origin_production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_factory. Business justification: Logistics lanes in apparel are defined between origin production factories and destination DCs. Factory-specific lane management enables accurate rate negotiation and transit time planning per factory']
  return_lane_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on lane (return_lane_id)']
  third_party_logistics_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the 3PL partner managing logistics operations for this lane.']
  base_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard base freight rate for this lane before surcharges and adjustments.']

  capacity_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum weight capacity available on this lane per shipment cycle, measured in kilograms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lane record was first created in the system.']

  distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total distance of the lane route measured in kilometers.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this lane configuration expires or is no longer available for shipment routing.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this lane configuration becomes active and available for shipment routing.']
  gsp_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lane qualifies for GSP duty-free treatment under trade preference programs.']
  hazmat_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lane is approved for transporting hazardous materials.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Standard Incoterm that defines responsibility and risk transfer for shipments on this lane.']
  landed_duty_paid boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lane rate includes all duties, taxes, and customs fees (LDP calculation).']
  lane_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the lane, typically combining origin and destination codes.']
  lane_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the shipping lane, typically origin to destination description.']
  lane_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the lane in the routing network.']
  lane_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lane based on geographic scope and routing characteristics.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lane record was last updated.']
  last_rate_update_date date [note: 'Date when the lane rate was last updated or renegotiated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, restrictions, or special handling instructions for this lane.']
  rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the lane rate.']
  rate_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure basis for the lane rate calculation.']
  requires_customs_clearance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether shipments on this lane require customs clearance procedures.']
  routing_guide_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking of this lane in the routing guide for shipment allocation decisions.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Speed and priority classification for shipments on this lane.']
  sla_on_time_delivery_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage for on-time delivery performance as defined in the carrier SLA for this lane.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lane provides temperature-controlled transport capabilities.']
  transit_time_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard transit time for shipments on this lane, measured in days.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation used for this lane.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for lane. Referenced by lane_id.'
}

Table logistics.contract {
  contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for contract']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: A logistics contract in the apparel supply chain is primarily a carrier contract — it governs the terms, rates, and service levels with a specific carrier. The contract table has no carrier_id FK curr']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Logistics contracts are budgeted and tracked against cost centers for freight and 3PL spend management. Apparel supply chain finance monitors contract spend vs. cost center budget; this link enables c']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Logistics contracts (carrier, 3PL) are linked to GL accounts for contract accrual and commitment accounting. Apparel finance teams post contract-level freight and warehousing accruals to specific GL a']
  master_contract_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on contract (master_contract_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the logistics service provider (carrier, 3PL, freight forwarder, customs broker) party to this contract.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the contract in the internal approval workflow.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contract was approved. Nullable if not yet approved.']
  auto_renewal_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews at the end of its term unless terminated.']
  base_rate_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Base rate or minimum charge amount per unit of service (per shipment, per container, per kilogram, etc.) as negotiated in the contract.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the contract, used in communications with carriers, 3PLs, and freight forwarders.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract indicating its operational state.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the logistics contract by service provider type: carrier (transportation), 3PL (third-party logistics), freight forwarder, customs broker, warehouse, or last-mile delivery.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this contract.']
  customs_clearance_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customs clearance services are included in the contract scope.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or terminates. Nullable for open-ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes legally binding and operational.']
  fuel_surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fuel surcharges are applicable under this contract.']
  fuel_surcharge_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of fuel surcharge applied to base rate when applicable. Nullable if not applicable.']
  generalized_system_preferences_utilization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes GSP utilization services to reduce or eliminate customs duties.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Geographic regions, countries, or trade lanes covered by this contract (e.g., USA-CHN, EU-APAC, North America).']
  harmonized_system_code_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract requires HS code compliance and classification services for customs clearance.']
  insurance_coverage_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cargo insurance coverage is included in the contract terms.']
  insurance_coverage_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum insurance coverage limit per shipment or per incident. Nullable if insurance is not included.']
  landed_duty_paid_calculation_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether LDP calculation and management services are included in the contract.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this contract record was last updated.']
  minimum_volume_commitment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum volume or quantity of shipments committed under this contract to qualify for negotiated rates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about the contract, including special terms, exceptions, or operational considerations.']
  on_time_delivery_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage for on-time delivery performance as specified in the SLA.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms negotiated in the contract (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, prepaid, cash on delivery).']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty amount per SLA breach incident. Nullable if penalty clause is not applicable.']
  penalty_clause_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether penalty clauses for SLA breaches are included in this contract.']
  rate_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the base rate (per shipment, per container, per kilogram, per cubic meter, per pallet, per mile).']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required for contract renewal or termination. Nullable if auto-renewal is not enabled.']
  routing_guide_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier must comply with the shipper\'s routing guide specifications.']
  service_level_agreement_target varchar [note: 'Service level agreement targets defined in the contract, including transit time commitments, on-time delivery percentages, and performance metrics.']
  service_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the logistics services covered under this contract, including transportation modes, geographic coverage, and service levels.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required to terminate the contract before its expiration date.']
  tracking_visibility_level varchar [note: 'Level of shipment tracking and visibility provided under this contract: basic (origin/destination only), milestone (key checkpoints), real-time (continuous GPS), or advanced (predictive analytics).']
  transit_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard transit time in days committed under this contract for the covered service scope.']
  transportation_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation covered by this contract.']
  volume_commitment_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum volume commitment (shipments, containers, kilograms, cubic meters, pallets).']
  wms_integration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether integration with Manhattan Associates WMS or other warehouse management systems is required under this contract.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for contract. Referenced by contract_id.'
}

  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing campaign. Primary key.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.audience_segment.
