  production_capacity_units_per_month decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monthly production capacity of the factory measured in units (garments, pieces, or pairs). Used for capacity planning, order allocation, and Open-to-Buy (OTB) calculations.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broader geographic region classification for sourcing strategy and logistics planning (e.g., South Asia, Southeast Asia, East Asia, Europe, Americas).']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall supplier risk classification based on audit results, compliance history, financial stability, geopolitical factors, and operational performance. High or Critical risk factories require enhanced monitoring and corrective action plans.']
  shift_structure varchar [note: 'Daily shift operation model of the factory. Indicates production hours and capacity flexibility. Triple shift operations provide maximum throughput but require strong labor compliance oversight.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the factory operates.']
  workforce_headcount varchar [note: 'Total number of employees working at the factory including production workers, supervisors, quality control staff, and administrative personnel. Required for labor compliance audits.']

  Note: 'Detailed operational profile of a manufacturing facility associated with a vendor, capturing factory name, physical address, country, region, factory type (Cut-Make-Trim, Full Package, Knitting, Weaving, Dyeing, Finishing, Embroidery), production capacity (units/month), number of production lines, workforce headcount, floor area (sqm), machinery types, product categories manufactured, gender specialization (menswear/womenswear/kidswear), minimum order quantity (MOQ), lead time (days), shift structure, and operational status. Distinct from the vendor legal entity — one vendor may operate multiple factories.'
}

Table supplier.vendor_contact {
  vendor_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor contact person. Primary key.']
  supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: Vendor contacts can be factory-specific (factory manager, production planner, QC supervisor) or vendor-level (corporate procurement, finance). Currently vendor_contact only has vendor_id. Adding facto']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or supplier organization this contact is associated with. Links to the vendor master record.']
  authorization_level varchar [note: 'Level of decision-making authority the contact has within the vendor organization (e.g., Operational, Managerial, Executive). Used to determine appropriate escalation paths and approval workflows.']
  certification_credentials varchar [note: 'Professional certifications or credentials held by the contact relevant to their role (e.g., WRAP Auditor, GOTS Certified, Six Sigma Black Belt, ISO 9001 Lead Auditor). Supports compliance verification and quality assurance.']
  contact_first_name varchar [note: 'First name or given name of the vendor contact person.']
  contact_full_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the vendor contact person, including first name, middle name, and last name.']
  contact_last_name varchar [note: 'Last name or family name of the vendor contact person.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor contact record. Indicates whether the contact is actively engaged, temporarily unavailable, or no longer with the vendor organization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor contact record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.']
  department varchar [note: 'Department or functional area within the vendor organization where the contact works (e.g., Sales, Quality Assurance, Compliance, Logistics, Finance, Production).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this contact person\'s role ended or they ceased to be the designated contact. Null if the contact is currently active. Used for contact history tracking and audit trails.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this contact person began their role with the vendor or became the designated contact for this business relationship. Used for contact history tracking and audit trails.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary business email address of the vendor contact person. Used for official communications, purchase orders, and compliance documentation.']
  emergency_contact_number varchar [note: 'Emergency contact phone number for urgent situations requiring immediate vendor response, such as production issues, quality failures, or compliance incidents.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this contact is currently active and should be used for communications. True if active, False if inactive or no longer with the vendor organization.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this contact is the primary point of contact for the vendor. True if primary, False otherwise. Only one primary contact should exist per vendor per role type.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title or position of the contact within the vendor organization (e.g., Account Manager, Quality Control Manager, Compliance Officer, Production Coordinator).']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Primary language(s) spoken by the contact for business communications. Supports multilingual vendor management and reduces miscommunication risk. Use ISO 639-1 two-letter codes (e.g., EN, ZH, ES, FR, IT, PT, DE, JA, KO, VI, BN, HI).']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent communication or interaction with this vendor contact. Used for relationship management and engagement tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor contact record was last updated or modified. Used for audit trails, change tracking, and data quality monitoring.']
  mobile_number varchar [note: 'Mobile phone number of the vendor contact person for urgent communications and field coordination.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the vendor contact. May include communication preferences, special instructions, relationship history, or other relevant information.']
  office_location varchar [note: 'Physical office location or site where the contact is based within the vendor organization (e.g., factory name, office branch, city). Used for site visit planning and logistics coordination.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary business phone number of the vendor contact person, including country code and extension if applicable.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'The contact\'s preferred method of communication for routine business interactions. Used to optimize communication effectiveness and response times.']
  reporting_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the contact\'s direct manager or supervisor within the vendor organization. Used for escalation paths and organizational context.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Standardized role classification indicating the primary function of the contact in the vendor relationship. Used for routing communications and workflow assignments.']
  specialization_area varchar [note: 'Specific area of expertise or specialization of the contact within their role (e.g., Fabric Sourcing, Garment Construction, Chemical Compliance, Export Documentation, Quality Testing). Used for routing specialized inquiries.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Time zone of the vendor contact\'s primary work location. Used for scheduling meetings and coordinating communications across global supply chains. Use IANA time zone database format (e.g., Asia/Shanghai, America/New_York, Europe/London).']
  wechat_handle varchar [note: 'WeChat contact identifier or username used for instant messaging communication with the vendor contact, commonly used for China-based suppliers.']
  whatsapp_handle varchar [note: 'WhatsApp contact identifier (typically phone number) used for instant messaging communication with the vendor contact, commonly used in international sourcing operations.']
  years_of_experience varchar [note: 'Number of years of professional experience the contact has in their field or role. Used for assessing vendor capability and relationship strength.']

  Note: 'Individual contact persons associated with a vendor or factory, capturing full name, job title, department, email, phone, WhatsApp/WeChat handle, preferred communication channel, language, role type (Account Manager, QC Liaison, Compliance Officer, Logistics Coordinator, Finance Contact), primary contact flag, and active status. Supports multi-contact management per vendor for sourcing, production, compliance, and logistics workflows.'
}

Table supplier.vendor_bank_account {
  vendor_bank_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor bank account record. Primary key.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier that owns this bank account. Links to the vendor master record.']
  account_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the bank account. Determines the settlement currency for payments and impacts foreign exchange requirements.']
  account_holder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the account holder as registered with the bank. Must match vendor legal name for payment processing and compliance verification.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number for the vendor. Format varies by country and banking system. Used for domestic and international payment processing.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor bank account. Active accounts are eligible for payment processing; inactive/suspended/closed accounts are blocked from new payments.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Type of bank account (checking, savings, current, business, escrow). Determines payment processing rules and settlement timing.']
  bank_address varchar [note: 'Full address of the bank branch or headquarters. Required for international payment routing and compliance documentation.']
  bank_branch_code varchar [note: 'Branch identifier or sort code used for domestic payment routing. Format varies by country (e.g., UK sort code, Indian IFSC code).']
  bank_branch_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific bank branch where the account is held. Used for domestic payment routing and vendor communication.']
  bank_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the bank\'s domicile. Critical for cross-border payment routing, foreign exchange, and compliance with international sanctions and trade regulations.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the financial institution holding the account. Used for payment routing and vendor master data management.']
  beneficiary_address varchar [note: 'Full address of the account beneficiary. Required for international wire transfers and compliance with anti-money laundering (AML) regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank account record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this bank account is no longer valid for payment processing. Null indicates an open-ended account. Used for account lifecycle management and historical tracking.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this bank account becomes effective for payment processing. Supports time-based account activation and vendor onboarding workflows.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this bank account in the source system or vendor portal. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data integration.']
  iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number for cross-border payments within SEPA and other regions. Standardized format for international payment routing.']
  intermediary_bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the intermediary or correspondent bank used for routing international payments when direct settlement is not available. Common in cross-border transactions.']
  intermediary_bank_swift_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC code of the intermediary bank. Required for routing payments through correspondent banking networks in international transactions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank account record was last updated. Used for change tracking, audit trails, and data synchronization.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of the most recent payment processed to this bank account in the account currency. Used for payment pattern analysis and fraud detection.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment processed to this bank account. Used for account activity monitoring and dormant account identification.']
  lc_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the current Letter of Credit arrangement for this vendor account. After this date, a new LC must be issued for continued payment processing.']
  lc_issuing_bank varchar [note: 'Name of the bank that issues Letters of Credit on behalf of the buyer (Apparel Fashion) for payments to this vendor. Required when LC payment method is used.']
  lc_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Letter of Credit is required for payments to this vendor account. True if LC is mandatory for trade finance compliance or vendor risk mitigation.']
  lc_terms varchar [note: 'Specific terms and conditions governing Letter of Credit issuance for this vendor, including sight/usance terms, document requirements, and negotiation conditions.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this bank account record. Used for audit trails and accountability in vendor master data management.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred payment method for this account: Wire Transfer, ACH (Automated Clearing House), SEPA (Single Euro Payments Area), TT (Telegraphic Transfer), LC (Letter of Credit), Check, or EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer). Determines payment processing workflow and timing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wire|ach|sepa|tt|lc|check|eft — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for this bank account when multiple accounts exist for a vendor. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by AP automation to select payment account.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Negotiated payment terms for this vendor account (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, Net 90, FOB, LDP, 2/10 Net 30). Defines when payment is due relative to invoice date or shipment.']
  primary_account_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary/default bank account for the vendor. True if this account should be used by default for payment processing when multiple accounts exist.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'ABA routing number for US domestic payments (ACH and wire transfers). Nine-digit code identifying the financial institution.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this bank account record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA FI, Oracle Financials, vendor portal). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for special payment instructions, reference requirements, or handling notes specific to this bank account. Used by AP processors for payment execution.']
  swift_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC code identifying the bank for international wire transfers. 8 or 11 character code used for cross-border payment routing.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number associated with this bank account (e.g., EIN, VAT number, TIN). Required for tax reporting (1099, withholding) and compliance with international tax regulations.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the bank account was successfully verified. Used for audit trails and periodic re-verification scheduling per compliance policies.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the bank account: micro-deposit test transactions, document review (bank statement/voided check), third-party verification service, manual verification, or bank letter confirmation.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of bank account verification process. Verified accounts have passed validation checks (micro-deposits, document review, third-party verification). Required for payment processing compliance.']

  Note: 'Banking and payment settlement details for vendors, including bank name, bank country, account holder name, IBAN/account number, SWIFT/BIC code, routing number, currency, payment method (Wire/ACH/LC/TT), Letter of Credit (LC) terms, LC issuing bank, LC expiry date, preferred payment terms (Net 30/60/90, FOB, LDP), and account verification status. Critical for AP processing, LC issuance, and cross-border payment compliance. Owned by the supplier domain as part of vendor master.'
}

Table supplier.factory_certification {
  factory_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the factory certification record. Primary key.']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Factory certifications in apparel are product-category-scoped (GOTS for organic cotton products, OEKO-TEX for specific categories). Linking to merchandise_hierarchy enables assortment planning validat']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Certification costs are tracked by brand profit center in apparel fashion for sustainability reporting and brand-level compliance cost allocation. Linking to profit_center enables brand P&L to reflect']
  supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: supplier_factory_certification tracks compliance certifications held by a manufacturing facility. The product currently references production.production_factory (cross-domain) but has NO in-domain FK ']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the national or international accreditation body that accredits the certifying body (e.g., ANAB, UKAS, DAkkS, CNAS). Provides additional assurance of the certifying body\'s competence.']
  audit_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent certification audit was conducted at the factory. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  audit_grade varchar [note: 'Grade or rating assigned during the certification audit. A indicates excellent compliance with no major findings. B indicates good compliance with minor findings. C indicates acceptable compliance with moderate findings requiring corrective action. D indicates poor compliance with major findings. Not Graded indicates the certification does not use a grading system.']
  audit_report_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the detailed audit report document. Contains findings, observations, and recommendations from the certification audit.']
  certificate_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the digital copy of the certification certificate document stored in the document management system or cloud storage. Used for verification and audit trail purposes.']
  certification_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for obtaining or renewing the certification, including audit fees, consulting fees, and administrative costs. Expressed in the company\'s reporting currency.']
  certification_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the certification cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY).']
  certification_level varchar [note: 'Level or tier of certification achieved, if the certification standard has multiple levels (e.g., \'Gold\', \'Silver\', \'Bronze\', \'Level 1\', \'Level 2\'). Null if the certification does not have levels.']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical compliance score assigned during the audit, typically expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00). Higher scores indicate better compliance with certification standards. Null if the certification does not use numerical scoring.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which corrective actions must be completed and verified. Format: yyyy-MM-dd. Null if no corrective actions are required.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether corrective actions are required to address audit findings. True if corrective action plan (CAP) is required. False if no corrective actions are needed.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action plan. Not required indicates no corrective actions are needed. Pending indicates corrective actions have been identified but not yet started. In progress indicates corrective actions are being implemented. Completed indicates corrective actions have been implemented and are awaiting verification. Verified indicates corrective actions have been verified by the certifying body. Overdue indicates corrective actions have not been completed by the due date.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certification record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  esg_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this certification is included in Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) reporting and sustainability disclosures. True if included in ESG reporting. False otherwise.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic coverage of the certification, indicating which factory locations or sites are covered (e.g., \'Main facility only\', \'All production sites\', \'Facility A and Facility B\'). Relevant for multi-site certifications.']
  major_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of major non-conformities or critical findings identified during the certification audit. Major findings typically require immediate corrective action and may impact certification status.']
  minor_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor non-conformities or observations identified during the certification audit. Minor findings require corrective action but do not immediately threaten certification status.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next surveillance or recertification audit. Format: yyyy-MM-dd. Null if not yet scheduled.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the certification, audit findings, or corrective actions. Used for internal documentation and knowledge sharing.']
  product_category_scope varchar [note: 'Specific product categories or types covered by the certification (e.g., \'Woven apparel\', \'Knit garments\', \'Footwear\', \'Accessories\'). Indicates which product lines are certified.']
  public_disclosure_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this certification is publicly disclosed on the company\'s website or in sustainability reports. True if publicly disclosed. False if confidential.']
  renewal_initiated_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification renewal process was formally initiated. Format: yyyy-MM-dd. Null if renewal has not been initiated.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Status of the certification renewal process. Not due indicates renewal is not yet required. Renewal initiated indicates renewal process has been started. Renewal in progress indicates renewal audit or documentation review is underway. Renewal completed indicates certification has been successfully renewed. Renewal failed indicates renewal was not successful and certification may expire or be revoked.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the factory based on certification status and audit findings. Low indicates minimal compliance risk. Medium indicates moderate risk requiring monitoring. High indicates significant risk requiring immediate attention. Critical indicates severe risk that may result in supplier disqualification.']
  scope_of_certification varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the scope and coverage of the certification, including specific product categories, processes, or facilities covered (e.g., \'Cut-Make-Trim operations for woven garments\', \'Dyeing and finishing of cotton fabrics\', \'Full manufacturing facility including warehousing\').']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source operational system from which this certification record originated (e.g., \'Infor PLM\', \'SAP S/4HANA MM\', \'Centric PLM\', \'Manual Entry\'). Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this certification record in the source operational system. Used for data reconciliation and traceability back to the source system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certification record was last updated in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Tracks all compliance certifications and ethical manufacturing accreditations held by a factory, including certification type (WRAP, FLA, GOTS, OEKO-TEX Standard 100, BCI, ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, SA8000, BSCI, Sedex/SMETA), certifying body, certificate number, issue date, expiry date, scope of certification, audit grade (A/B/C/D), corrective action required flag, certificate document URL, and renewal status. Enables compliance tracking against FTC, CPSC, and ESG reporting obligations.'
}

Table supplier.compliance_audit {
  compliance_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compliance audit record. Primary key for the compliance audit entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Audit costs (auditor fees, travel, certification) are allocated to responsible cost centers for departmental expense tracking and budget management. Standard practice in apparel compliance operations.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Audit expenses must be posted to specific GL accounts (professional fees, compliance costs) for financial reporting and P&L classification. Required for accurate COGS and SG&A allocation.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Compliance audit costs must be posted to the correct accounting ledger in multi-GAAP apparel companies (e.g., IFRS vs local statutory). Ledger assignment ensures audit expenditure is correctly capture']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_factory. Business justification: Compliance audits conducted at factories must be cross-referenced against internal production factory records for regulatory reporting and corrective action tracking. Mirrors the pattern already estab']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Compliance audit costs are attributed to profit centers for brand/channel P&L reporting in apparel fashion. Finance teams allocate audit expenditure by brand profit center to support ESG cost reportin']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Apparel compliance audits are scheduled relative to production seasons — factories must be audit-cleared before seasonal bulk production begins. Linking compliance_audit to season enables season-level']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or factory that was audited. Links to the supplier master data.']
  announced_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the audit was announced to the supplier in advance. True indicates the supplier was notified ahead of time; False indicates an unannounced surprise audit.']
  audit_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for conducting the audit, including auditor fees, travel expenses, and certification body charges. Expressed in the company\'s reporting currency.']
  audit_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the audit cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  audit_date date [note: 'The date when the on-site audit was conducted or completed. This is the principal business event date for the audit.']
  audit_duration_days decimal(4,1) [note: 'Total number of days spent conducting the on-site audit fieldwork. Includes partial days represented as decimals (e.g., 2.5 days).']
  audit_end_date date [note: 'The date when the audit fieldwork concluded. For multi-day audits, this represents the last day of on-site inspection.']
  audit_framework varchar [note: 'The compliance standard or framework against which the audit was conducted. Examples include FLA Workplace Code of Conduct, WRAP 12 Principles, BSCI Code of Conduct, Sedex SMETA, GOTS, OEKO-TEX Standard 100, ISO 14001, SA8000.']
  audit_grade varchar [note: 'Letter grade or categorical rating assigned to the factory based on audit performance. Grading systems vary by certification body and audit framework. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A|B|C|D|F|Pass|Conditional Pass|Fail|Gold|Silver|Bronze — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique audit reference number assigned by the auditing organization or internal compliance team.']
  audit_report_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the full audit report document stored in the document management system. Provides access to detailed findings, evidence, and recommendations.']
  audit_start_date date [note: 'The date when the audit fieldwork began. For multi-day audits, this represents the first day of on-site inspection.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit. Scheduled indicates the audit is planned but not yet started; In Progress indicates the audit is currently being conducted; Completed indicates the audit has been finalized and report issued; Cancelled indicates the audit was cancelled before completion; Pending Review indicates the audit is complete but awaiting final approval.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit based on its focus area. Social Compliance covers labor practices and working conditions; Environmental covers sustainability and environmental impact; Quality covers product quality systems; Fire Safety covers fire prevention and emergency preparedness; Chemical REACH covers chemical safety and REACH compliance; Structural Safety covers building and facility structural integrity.']
  auditing_firm varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party auditing organization or certification body that conducted the audit. Examples include Bureau Veritas, SGS, Intertek, UL, TUV. Null if the audit was conducted by internal compliance team.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead auditor who conducted the compliance audit. May be an internal compliance officer or external third-party auditor.']
  cap_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the submitted Corrective Action Plan has been reviewed and approved by the compliance team or auditing body. True indicates approval; False indicates rejection or pending review.']
  cap_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the supplier must submit a formal Corrective Action Plan addressing all identified non-conformances. Null if no CAP is required.']
  cap_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a formal Corrective Action Plan is required from the supplier to address identified non-conformances. True indicates CAP is mandatory; False indicates no CAP required (typically for audits with no significant findings).']
  cap_submitted_date date [note: 'The date when the supplier submitted their Corrective Action Plan in response to audit findings. Null if CAP has not yet been submitted or is not required.']
  certification_body varchar [note: 'Name of the accredited certification body issuing the compliance certificate or audit credential. Relevant for WRAP, FLA, GOTS, OEKO-TEX, BSCI, and Sedex audits.']
  certification_valid_from_date date [note: 'The effective start date of the compliance certification issued as a result of this audit. Applicable for certification audits (WRAP, FLA, GOTS, OEKO-TEX). Null for non-certification audits.']
  certification_valid_until_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the compliance certification issued as a result of this audit. Applicable for certification audits (WRAP, FLA, GOTS, OEKO-TEX). Null for non-certification audits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance audit record was first created in the system.']
  critical_ncr_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical non-conformances identified during the audit. Critical NCRs represent severe violations that pose immediate risk to worker safety, health, or fundamental rights (e.g., child labor, forced labor, unsafe structural conditions).']
  factory_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall numerical score assigned to the factory based on audit findings. Scoring scale varies by audit framework (e.g., 0-100 for some frameworks, A-F letter grades converted to numeric for others). Higher scores indicate better compliance.']
  final_audit_status varchar [note: 'The final outcome determination of the audit after all reviews and corrective actions. Pass indicates full compliance; Conditional Pass indicates compliance with minor issues requiring monitoring; Fail indicates non-compliance requiring immediate action; Pending Corrective Action indicates awaiting CAP implementation; Certification Granted/Denied applies to certification audits.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'High-level narrative summary of the key audit findings, including strengths, weaknesses, and areas of concern identified during the inspection. Provides context for the numerical scores and NCR counts.']
  major_ncr_count varchar [note: 'Number of major non-conformances identified during the audit. Major NCRs represent significant violations of compliance standards that require corrective action but do not pose immediate critical risk.']
  management_interviews_count varchar [note: 'Number of factory management personnel interviewed during the audit to assess management systems, policies, and commitment to compliance.']
  minor_ncr_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor non-conformances identified during the audit. Minor NCRs represent less severe violations or areas for improvement that do not significantly impact compliance status.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next periodic compliance audit of this supplier. Audit frequency is typically annual or biennial depending on risk profile and certification requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or context related to the audit that do not fit into structured fields. May include special circumstances, follow-up actions, or stakeholder communications.']
  reaudit_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the follow-up re-audit to verify corrective action implementation. Null if no re-audit is required.']
  reaudit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a follow-up re-audit is required to verify that corrective actions have been implemented and non-conformances have been resolved. True indicates re-audit is mandatory; False indicates no re-audit needed.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification assigned to the supplier based on audit findings. Used to determine monitoring frequency and sourcing decisions. Low indicates minimal compliance risk; Critical indicates severe risk requiring immediate action or potential delisting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance audit record was last modified in the system.']
  worker_interviews_count varchar [note: 'Number of factory workers interviewed during the audit as part of the social compliance assessment. Worker interviews are a key component of FLA, WRAP, and BSCI audits.']

  Note: 'Records of factory compliance and social responsibility audits conducted by internal teams, third-party auditors, or certification bodies. Captures audit type (Social Compliance, Environmental, Quality, Fire Safety, Chemical/REACH), audit date, auditor name, auditing firm, factory score, findings summary, number of critical/major/minor non-conformances (NCRs), corrective action plan (CAP) required flag, CAP due date, re-audit required flag, and final audit status (Pass/Conditional Pass/Fail). Supports FLA, WRAP, BSCI, and Sedex audit workflows.'
}

Table supplier.audit_finding {
  audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier audit finding record. Primary key for the supplier audit finding entity.']
  compliance_audit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent compliance or quality audit during which this finding was identified.']
  actual_closure_date date [note: 'Actual date when the corrective action was completed, verified, and the finding was formally closed. Null if the finding remains open.']
  auditor_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or context provided by the auditor regarding the finding, including mitigating factors, supplier cooperation, or special circumstances.']
  business_impact varchar [note: 'Description of the potential or actual business impact of the finding, including production delays, quality issues, regulatory penalties, reputational risk, or supply chain disruption.']
  closure_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the finding in the corrective action lifecycle. Open: newly identified, action not started. In Progress: corrective action underway. Pending Verification: action completed, awaiting verification. Closed: verified and accepted. Overdue: past target closure date without completion.']
  corrective_action_required varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action plan (CAP) required to remediate the finding, including specific steps, process changes, training requirements, or infrastructure improvements needed.']
  cost_to_remediate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual cost incurred by the supplier to implement the corrective action and remediate the finding, including infrastructure upgrades, training, process changes, or equipment purchases.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supplier audit finding record was first created in the system, following the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost to remediate amount, such as USD, EUR, CNY, or INR.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the finding was escalated to higher management levels or external stakeholders for intervention or decision-making.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) identifying whether this finding has been escalated to senior management, legal, or executive leadership due to severity, regulatory risk, or failure to remediate within agreed timelines.']
  evidence_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to supporting evidence documentation for the finding, including photographs, audit reports, corrective action plans, or verification records stored in the document management system.']
  finding_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the non-conformance finding based on the compliance domain. Categories include Labor (working conditions, child labor, forced labor), Health & Safety (workplace safety, PPE, ergonomics), Environmental (waste management, emissions, water usage), Chemical (restricted substances, OEKO-TEX compliance), Fire Safety (exits, extinguishers, evacuation plans), and Wages (minimum wage, overtime pay, payroll records).']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the non-conformance finding, including specific observations, conditions found, and evidence collected during the audit.']
  finding_identified_date date [note: 'Date when the non-conformance finding was first identified during the audit inspection or assessment.']
  finding_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique reference number assigned to this non-conformance finding for tracking and communication purposes.']
  finding_subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed subcategory or specific compliance area within the primary finding category, providing granular classification for analysis and reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supplier audit finding record was last updated or modified, following the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for tracking changes to corrective action status, closure dates, or verification results.']
  previous_finding_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the previous finding if this is identified as a repeat finding, enabling linkage and trend analysis of recurring non-conformances.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulatory requirement, standard clause, or compliance framework that was violated or not met, such as WRAP Principle 3, FLA Code Section 2.1, or OEKO-TEX Standard 100 Annex 6.']
  repeat_finding_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) identifying whether this finding is a repeat occurrence of a previously identified and closed non-conformance at the same supplier, indicating systemic compliance issues or ineffective corrective actions.']
  responsible_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or role at the supplier factory responsible for implementing the corrective action and ensuring closure of the finding.']
  responsible_party_title varchar [note: 'Job title or position of the responsible party at the supplier factory, such as Factory Manager, Compliance Officer, or Health & Safety Coordinator.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score assigned to the finding based on severity, likelihood of recurrence, and potential business impact, used for prioritization and supplier risk assessment.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Analysis of the underlying root cause of the non-conformance, identifying systemic issues, process gaps, or resource constraints that led to the finding.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the finding\'s severity based on risk and impact. Critical: immediate threat to worker safety or legal compliance, requires immediate action. Major: significant non-compliance requiring corrective action. Minor: low-risk non-compliance requiring improvement. Observation: opportunity for improvement without formal non-compliance.']
  target_closure_date date [note: 'Target date by which the corrective action must be completed and the finding closed, based on severity level and agreed-upon remediation timeline.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action was verified as complete and effective by the auditor or compliance team.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify completion and effectiveness of the corrective action, such as document review, on-site re-inspection, remote verification via photos/videos, or third-party audit.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name of the auditor, compliance officer, or third-party inspector who verified the corrective action completion and effectiveness.']

  Note: 'Individual non-conformance findings (NCRs) identified during a compliance or quality audit, capturing finding reference number, audit reference, finding category (Labor, Health & Safety, Environmental, Chemical, Fire Safety, Wages, Working Hours), severity level (Critical/Major/Minor/Observation), finding description, root cause, corrective action required, responsible party at factory, target closure date, actual closure date, evidence document URL, and closure status. Enables granular CAP tracking and repeat-finding analysis.'
}

Table supplier.capacity_profile {
  capacity_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the capacity profile record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Capacity reservations in apparel fashion are allocated against cost centers for production budgeting and capacity cost planning. Finance teams track reserved vs. available capacity costs by cost cente']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Factory capacity in apparel is product-category-specific (denim capacity differs from knit capacity). Linking capacity_profile to merchandise_hierarchy enables category-level capacity planning during ']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_factory. Business justification: Integrated capacity planning requires reconciling supplier-side capacity profiles against internal production factory records. When a supplier factory is also registered as a production_factory, plann']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Factories commit capacity by season; merchandising requires seasonal capacity visibility for buy plan feasibility assessment, vendor allocation strategy, and OTB budget validation in apparel productio']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or factory for which this capacity profile is defined.']
  allocated_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Number of units already allocated to confirmed purchase orders (PO) or production orders.']
  available_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Number of units still available for booking or allocation within the planning period.']
  capacity_confirmation_date date [note: 'Date when the factory formally confirmed or committed to this capacity allocation.']
  capacity_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk assessment level for capacity availability based on factory performance, compliance, and external factors (low, medium, high, critical).']
  capacity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capacity profile (active, inactive, suspended, expired, pending confirmation).']
  capacity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of capacity status: allocated (assigned to orders), reserved (held for future orders), available (open for booking), committed (contractually bound), or planned (forecasted).']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used to express capacity quantities (units, pieces, pairs, dozens, cases).']
  capacity_units_per_month varchar [note: 'Total production capacity expressed in units per month for the specified product category and season.']
  capacity_valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date of the period for which this capacity profile is valid and applicable.']
  capacity_valid_to_date date [note: 'End date of the period for which this capacity profile is valid and applicable.']
  compliance_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of factory compliance certifications (WRAP, FLA, GOTS, OEKO-TEX) relevant to this capacity profile.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity profile record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for pricing and payment transactions related to this capacity profile (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining delivery and risk transfer responsibilities (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity profile record was last modified or updated.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard production lead time in days from order placement to shipment readiness for this product category at this factory.']
  maximum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of units that can be produced in a single production run or order cycle for this product category.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units required per production run or purchase order (PO) for this product category at this factory.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding special conditions, constraints, or considerations for this capacity profile.']
  otif_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage for on-time and in-full delivery performance expected from this factory for this capacity profile.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the payment terms applicable to orders placed under this capacity profile (e.g., NET30, NET60, LC90).']
  peak_season_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator whether this capacity profile represents a peak production season with higher demand and tighter lead times.']
  planning_system_source varchar [note: 'Name of the source planning system or module from which this capacity profile was created or synchronized (e.g., Anaplan, SAP PP, Oracle RMS).']
  production_line_count varchar [note: 'Number of production lines or assembly lines dedicated to this product category during the capacity period.']
  quality_acceptance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical quality acceptance rate (percentage of units passing quality inspection) for this factory and product category.']
  reserved_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Number of units reserved or held for anticipated orders but not yet formally allocated.']
  shift_count varchar [note: 'Number of production shifts per day (e.g., 1, 2, or 3 shifts) operating during this capacity period.']
  utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total capacity currently utilized, calculated as (allocated + reserved) / total capacity * 100.']
  workforce_headcount varchar [note: 'Total number of workers or operators allocated to production for this product category during the capacity period.']

  Note: 'Seasonal and rolling production capacity commitments at the factory level, capturing season (SS25, FW25), product category, allocated/reserved/available capacity (units/month), utilization percentage, peak season flags, lead time by product type, minimum and maximum run sizes, and capacity confirmation date. Drives OTB (Open-to-Buy) planning, production scheduling, and factory loading decisions. One factory may have multiple capacity profiles across seasons and product categories.'
}

Table supplier.vendor_scorecard {
  vendor_scorecard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor scorecard evaluation record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Vendor management activities (evaluation, monitoring, remediation) are cost center responsibilities. Links scorecard ownership to departmental budgets for vendor management program costs.']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Apparel vendor scorecards are category-specific — a vendor may perform differently for denim vs. knitwear. Linking to merchandise_hierarchy enables category-level vendor performance KPI reporting, whi']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_factory. Business justification: Factory performance reporting in apparel requires combining supplier scorecard metrics with internal production factory data. Linking vendor_scorecard to production_factory enables integrated factory ']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Vendor performance is evaluated by season for tier classification, future buy plan vendor selection, and seasonal OTIF/quality trending; critical for merchandising vendor strategy and sourcing decisio']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or supplier being evaluated in this scorecard.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the scorecard evaluation was formally approved by management and published for use in sourcing decisions.']
  aql_defect_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of defective units found during quality inspections based on AQL sampling standards. Lower values indicate better quality performance.']
  capacity_utilization_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of vendor production capacity allocated to this business during the evaluation period. Indicates strategic partnership depth and allocation priority.']
  claim_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of orders resulting in quality claims, chargebacks, or Return to Vendor (RTV) actions during the evaluation period. Lower is better.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for evaluator notes, qualitative observations, context on performance issues, or strategic commentary on vendor relationship.']
  compliance_audit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score from social compliance and labor standards audits (WRAP, FLA, BSCI, Sedex). Typically scored 0-100 or letter grade converted to numeric.']
  cost_competitiveness_index decimal(5,2) [note: 'Index comparing vendor pricing against market benchmarks and peer vendors. Values above 100 indicate above-market pricing; below 100 indicates competitive pricing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this scorecard record was first created in the system.']
  evaluation_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this vendor scorecard evaluation is conducted (monthly, quarterly, seasonal, or annual).']
  evaluation_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the performance evaluation period for this scorecard.']
  evaluation_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the performance evaluation period for this scorecard.']
  evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the scorecard evaluation. Published scorecards are finalized and used for strategic decisions.']
  evaluator_role varchar [note: 'Job role or title of the evaluator (e.g., Senior Sourcing Manager, Quality Assurance Director, Procurement Lead).']
  innovation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Subjective score evaluating vendor contributions to product innovation, material development, process improvements, and value engineering initiatives.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this scorecard record was last updated or modified.']
  lead_time_performance_days varchar [note: 'Average lead time in days from purchase order issuance to delivery during the evaluation period. Shorter lead times improve supply chain agility.']
  on_time_sample_submission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of product samples (SMS, PP samples) submitted on or before the scheduled deadline per the Time and Action (TNA) calendar.']
  order_fill_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ordered quantity successfully fulfilled without shortages or cancellations. Measures vendor reliability and inventory management.']
  otif_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of purchase orders delivered on time and in full during the evaluation period. Key operational KPI for supply chain reliability.']
  overall_weighted_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite weighted score aggregating all KPI dimensions (OTIF, quality, compliance, cost, responsiveness, sustainability) per the scorecard weighting methodology. Primary ranking metric.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Vendor classification tier based on overall weighted score. Preferred vendors receive priority allocation; Probation/Delisted vendors face restrictions or exit.']
  pp_sample_approval_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of pre-production samples approved on first submission without requiring rework or resubmission. Indicates design and technical capability.']
  previous_tier varchar [note: 'Vendor performance tier from the prior evaluation period. Enables trend analysis and tier movement tracking.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Strategic sourcing recommendation based on scorecard results. Drives allocation decisions, remediation plans, or vendor exit strategies.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the vendor must complete remediation actions and demonstrate improvement. Null if no remediation is required.']
  remediation_plan_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal corrective action or remediation plan is required due to performance deficiencies (True) or not (False).']
  responsiveness_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Subjective rating (typically 1-5 or 1-10 scale) assessing vendor communication quality, issue resolution speed, and collaboration effectiveness.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score evaluating environmental and sustainability practices including certifications (GOTS, OEKO-TEX, BCI), energy efficiency, waste management, and chemical compliance.']
  tier_change_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor tier changed from the previous evaluation period (True) or remained the same (False).']
  total_po_count varchar [note: 'Total number of purchase orders issued to this vendor during the evaluation period. Indicates transaction volume and relationship scale.']
  total_po_value_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value in USD of all purchase orders issued to this vendor during the evaluation period. Provides spend context for performance assessment.']

  Note: 'Periodic performance evaluation of vendor and factory performance across operational KPIs, generated monthly/quarterly/seasonally. Captures OTIF (On-Time In-Full) rate, AQL defect rate, on-time sample submission, PP sample approval rate, compliance audit score, sustainability score, responsiveness rating, cost competitiveness index, overall weighted score, performance tier (Preferred/Approved/Conditional/Probation/Delisted), and evaluation period. Drives strategic sourcing decisions, vendor tiering, allocation preferences, and exit/remediation actions.'
}

Table supplier.risk_assessment {
  risk_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier risk assessment record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Risk assessment activities incur costs (consultant fees, mitigation expenses) allocated to responsible cost centers. Essential for risk management program cost tracking and budget accountability.']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Supplier risk assessments in apparel are scoped to product categories (e.g., chemical compliance risk for childrenswear, restricted substances for footwear). Linking to merchandise_hierarchy enables c']
  supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: risk_assessment currently has vendor_id → vendor and production_factory_id → production.production_factory (cross-domain), but no in-domain FK to supplier.supplier_factory. In apparel supply chain ris']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor being assessed. Links to the supplier master data entity.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which this risk assessment was conducted or recorded.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'The methodology or approach used to conduct the risk assessment. Self-Assessment relies on supplier-provided information. Third-Party Audit uses independent auditors. Site Visit involves on-site inspection. Document Review analyzes certifications and records. Questionnaire uses structured surveys. Scorecard applies a standardized rating framework. Hybrid combines multiple methods. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Self-Assessment|Third-Party Audit|Site Visit|Document Review|Questionnaire|Scorecard|Hybrid — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'The current workflow status of the risk assessment record. Draft indicates the assessment is being prepared. Under Review indicates it is awaiting approval. Approved indicates it has been validated and accepted. Closed indicates the risk has been resolved or is no longer relevant. Escalated indicates the risk requires senior management attention.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'The type or trigger of the risk assessment. Initial assessments occur during supplier onboarding, periodic assessments follow a schedule, triggered assessments respond to specific events or incidents.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'The name of the individual or organization that conducted the risk assessment or audit, if applicable. Supports traceability and accountability.']
  business_impact_area varchar [note: 'The primary business function or area that would be affected if the risk materializes. Supports prioritization and cross-functional risk management.']
  certification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of any relevant certification or compliance audit that informed this risk assessment, such as WRAP, FLA, GOTS, OEKO-TEX, or ISO certifications.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free-text comments, notes, or observations related to the risk assessment. Provides context and qualitative insights not captured in structured fields.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this risk assessment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'The date on which this risk was escalated to senior management or a risk committee, if applicable. Tracks critical risk visibility and governance.']
  esg_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk assessment is related to Environmental, Social, or Governance (ESG) criteria. True if the risk has ESG implications, False otherwise. Supports ESG reporting and sustainability initiatives.']
  geographic_risk_region varchar [note: 'The geographic region or country associated with this risk, if the risk is location-specific. Supports geopolitical and regional risk analysis.']
  impact_score varchar [note: 'A numeric score representing the severity of consequences if the risk materializes. Typically scored on a scale of 1 to 5, where 1 is Negligible and 5 is Catastrophic. Considers financial, operational, reputational, and compliance impacts.']
  inherent_risk_rating varchar [note: 'The overall risk level before any mitigation measures are applied. Typically derived from the combination of likelihood and impact scores. Low indicates minimal concern, Medium requires monitoring, High requires active management, and Critical demands immediate action and escalation.']
  likelihood_score varchar [note: 'A numeric score representing the probability that the risk will materialize. Typically scored on a scale of 1 to 5, where 1 is Very Unlikely and 5 is Very Likely. Used in conjunction with impact score to calculate inherent risk rating.']
  mitigation_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The estimated or actual cost of implementing the mitigation strategy, in the company\'s reporting currency. Supports cost-benefit analysis and risk management budgeting.']
  mitigation_status varchar [note: 'The current implementation status of the mitigation strategy. Tracks progress from planning through execution.']
  mitigation_strategy varchar [note: 'A detailed description of the actions, controls, or measures implemented or planned to reduce the likelihood or impact of the risk. May include supplier development programs, contract clauses, diversification strategies, audits, or contingency plans.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next review or reassessment of this risk. Ensures periodic monitoring and updates to risk status and mitigation effectiveness.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to any specific regulation, law, or compliance framework that this risk assessment addresses, such as FTC labeling requirements, CPSC safety standards, or GDPR data protection.']
  residual_risk_rating varchar [note: 'The risk level remaining after mitigation measures have been applied. Represents the accepted or tolerated risk level. Used to determine if additional mitigation is required or if the risk is within acceptable tolerance.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the risk was resolved, closed, or deemed no longer relevant. Supports risk lifecycle tracking and historical analysis.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'The standard interval at which this risk should be reviewed. High and Critical risks typically require more frequent review than Low risks.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'The primary category of risk being assessed. Geopolitical includes trade restrictions and political instability. Financial covers supplier solvency and payment risk. Compliance includes labor standards, environmental regulations, and certifications. Capacity addresses production volume and lead time risk. Quality covers defect rates and product safety. Environmental includes sustainability and carbon footprint. Labor covers working conditions and fair labor practices. Logistics includes transportation and delivery risk. Currency covers foreign exchange volatility. Reputational includes brand and ethical risk. Cybersecurity covers data protection and IT security. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Geopolitical|Financial|Compliance|Capacity|Quality|Environmental|Labor|Logistics|Currency|Reputational|Cybersecurity — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'A detailed narrative description of the identified risk, including context, potential triggers, and specific concerns. Provides qualitative detail for risk understanding and communication.']
  risk_subcategory varchar [note: 'A more granular classification of the risk within the primary category. For example, under Compliance, subcategories might include Child Labor, Forced Labor, Wage Violations, or Chemical Safety. Allows for detailed risk taxonomy.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the individual who last modified this risk assessment record. Supports accountability and audit trail.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this risk assessment record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the individual who created this risk assessment record. Supports accountability and audit trail.']

  Note: 'Structured risk assessment records for vendors and factories, capturing risk assessment date, risk category (Geopolitical, Financial, Compliance, Capacity, Quality, Environmental, Labor, Logistics, Currency), risk description, likelihood score (1-5), impact score (1-5), inherent risk rating (Low/Medium/High/Critical), mitigation strategy, residual risk rating, risk owner, next review date, and assessment status. Supports supply chain resilience planning and ESG risk reporting.'
}

Multi-brand apparel companies require this link for legal entity reporting, contract liability recognition, an']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Contract management is a procurement cost center function. Links agreement ownership to departmental budgets for legal review, negotiation, and contract administration costs.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Vendor agreements carry contract values and financial commitments requiring GL account assignment for accrual accounting, commitment tracking, and contract liability postings. Apparel fashion finance ']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Vendor agreements in apparel are scoped to product categories (e.g., a vendor approved only for knitwear). Linking to merchandise_hierarchy enables compliance validation — ensuring POs are only placed']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Vendor agreements in apparel fashion are brand-specific, with contract values and exclusivity terms tied to individual brand profit centers. Associating agreements with profit centers enables brand-le']
  supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: vendor_agreement currently only links to vendor via vendor_id. In apparel manufacturing, agreements are often factory-specific — CMT agreements, capacity reservation agreements, and OEM/ODM contracts ']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor party covered by this agreement.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the vendor agreement, typically formatted with prefix and numeric sequence.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the vendor agreement indicating its operational validity and enforceability.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor agreement based on the nature of the commercial relationship and scope of services or goods covered.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Number of formal amendments or addendums executed against this agreement since its original signing.']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the vendor is subject to compliance audits (social, environmental, quality) as stipulated in the agreement.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement automatically renews at expiry unless either party provides termination notice.']
  compliance_certifications_required varchar [note: 'List of mandatory compliance certifications the vendor must maintain (e.g., WRAP, FLA, BCI, GOTS, ISO 14001, ISO 45001).']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor agreement record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding payable amount allowed under this agreement before additional approvals or holds are required.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which financial transactions under this agreement are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  default_incoterm varchar [note: 'The default Incoterm rule applied to shipments under this agreement unless otherwise specified on the purchase order (PO). Common values include FOB (Free on Board), CIF (Cost Insurance Freight), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dispute_resolution_method varchar [note: 'The agreed method for resolving disputes arising under this agreement.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the vendor agreement becomes legally binding and operational.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes an exclusivity clause restricting the vendor from supplying similar goods or services to competitors.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor agreement terminates unless renewed. Nullable for open-ended agreements.']
  force_majeure_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes a force majeure clause excusing performance due to extraordinary events beyond the parties\' control.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The legal jurisdiction and governing law under which the agreement is interpreted and enforced (e.g., State of New York, England and Wales).']
  incoterms_version varchar [note: 'The version of International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) governing the delivery, risk transfer, and cost allocation for goods under this agreement.']
  insurance_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of insurance coverage the vendor is required to maintain (e.g., general liability, product liability, workers compensation).']
  ip_ownership_clause varchar [note: 'Summary of the intellectual property ownership terms, specifying whether designs, patterns, tech packs, or product innovations remain with the buyer, vendor, or are jointly owned.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment or addendum to the agreement. Nullable if no amendments have been made.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor agreement record was last updated in the system.']
  liability_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum liability amount either party is exposed to under the agreement, excluding cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'The minimum order quantity (MOQ) required per purchase order (PO) or per style/SKU as stipulated in the agreement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, or internal comments related to the vendor agreement.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The primary method of payment authorized under this agreement.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the vendor, expressed as net days or other conditions (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30, Letter of Credit at sight).']
  performance_bond_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is required to provide a performance bond or bank guarantee to secure contractual obligations.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required to prevent auto-renewal or to initiate renewal discussions.']
  signatory_name_buyer varchar [note: 'Name of the authorized signatory representing the buyer (Apparel Fashion) on the agreement.']
  signatory_name_vendor varchar [note: 'Name of the authorized signatory representing the vendor on the agreement.']
  signed_date date [note: 'The date on which the agreement was signed by authorized representatives of both parties.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance written notice required by either party to terminate the agreement.']

  Note: 'Master vendor agreements and framework contracts governing the commercial relationship with a supplier, including agreement type (Master Supply Agreement, CMT Contract, FOB Contract, Fabric Supply Agreement, 3PL SLA), agreement reference number, effective date, expiry date, auto-renewal flag, governing law jurisdiction, incoterms version, payment terms, currency, credit limit, exclusivity clause flag, IP ownership clause, termination notice period, and agreement status (Draft/Active/Expired/Terminated). SSOT for vendor contractual terms within the supplier domain.'
}

Table supplier.material_supplier {
  material_supplier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the material-supplier association record in the Approved Vendor List (AVL). Primary key for this data product.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material entity being supplied. Links to the material master data product.']
  supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: supplier_material_supplier is the qualified supplier-material association linking approved raw materials, fabrics, and trims to their vendors. Currently it has vendor_id → vendor but no factory-level ']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier entity providing this material. Links to the supplier master data product.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of this supplier-material association in the AVL (Approved Vendor List). Controls whether this supplier can be used for procurement.']
  approved_colorways varchar [note: 'List of approved color options or colorways that this supplier is qualified to provide for this material. Supports collection planning and merchandising.']
  capacity_per_month decimal(15,3) [note: 'Supplier\'s monthly production capacity for this material in the specified unit of measure. Used for capacity planning and allocation decisions.']
  composition_percentage varchar [note: 'Detailed percentage breakdown of material composition by component. Supports multi-component materials and blends.']
