  inland_freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost of domestic transportation from the port of discharge to the final warehouse or distribution center.']
  insurance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost of cargo insurance covering the goods during international transit. Component of landed cost.']
  landed_cost_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the landed cost calculation. Human-readable reference number used in financial reporting and audit trails.']
  landed_cost_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the landed cost record in the financial workflow. Values: draft (initial calculation), calculated (computation complete), approved (reviewed and approved), posted (recorded to general ledger), reversed (corrected or cancelled).']
  letter_of_credit_number varchar [note: 'The letter of credit number used to secure payment for this shipment. Links to trade finance instruments.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this landed cost record was last modified. Audit trail for record updates and corrections.']
  ocean_freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost of ocean freight transportation from the port of loading to the port of discharge. Component of landed cost.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country of manufacture or origin for the goods. Used for customs duty calculation and trade compliance.']
  port_handling_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost of port handling services including unloading, storage, and terminal fees at the port of discharge.']
  port_of_discharge varchar [note: 'The seaport or airport where the goods were unloaded upon arrival. Used for customs clearance and port handling cost allocation.']
  port_of_loading varchar [note: 'The seaport or airport from which the goods were shipped. Used for freight cost allocation and logistics tracking.']
  quantity_received decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of goods received in this shipment. Used to calculate unit landed cost and allocate costs across units.']
  tariff_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The customs duty rate percentage applied to the goods based on the HS code and origin country. Used to calculate the customs duty amount.']
  total_landed_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total landed duty paid cost per unit or shipment. Sum of FOB price, freight, insurance, customs duty, and all other charges. Authoritative cost basis for inventory valuation and COGS.']
  unit_landed_cost_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The landed cost per individual unit of the SKU. Calculated by dividing total landed cost by quantity received. Used for per-unit inventory valuation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the quantity received. Standard units include EA (each), PC (piece), KG (kilogram), LB (pound), M (meter), YD (yard), PAIR, SET, BOX, CASE. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|LB|M|YD|PAIR|SET|BOX|CASE — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Captures the full landed duty paid (LDP) cost breakdown for imported apparel goods, including FOB price, ocean/air freight, insurance, customs duty, HS code tariff rate, GSP preference utilization, port handling, and inland freight. Linked to purchase orders and goods receipts in SAP S/4HANA MM. Provides the authoritative LDP cost basis for inventory valuation, COGS recognition, and margin analysis by sourcing origin.'
}

Table finance.company_code {
  company_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the company code record. Primary key. Inferred role: MASTER_RESOURCE (legal entity as a managed organizational resource).']
  accounting_principle varchar [note: 'Primary accounting standard applied for statutory financial reporting. Options include IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards), US GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), local GAAP, or hybrid approaches for dual reporting.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date on which the company code was activated in the SAP S/4HANA FI system and began posting financial transactions.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the registered legal address for the company code. Includes street number and street name.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the registered legal address for the company code. Includes building name, suite, or floor information.']
  business_segment varchar [note: 'Primary business segment classification for this company code within the apparel fashion group. Used for segment reporting, profitability analysis, and strategic planning.']
  chart_of_accounts varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the chart of accounts assigned to this company code. The COA defines the structure of General Ledger (GL) accounts used for recording financial transactions.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the registered legal address for the company code.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Four-character alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the legal entity within the SAP S/4HANA FI system. This is the business identifier used across all financial transactions and statutory reporting.']
  company_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the company as registered with statutory authorities. Used for financial statements, tax filings, and legal documentation.']
  company_name_short varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or trading name of the company used for internal reporting and operational displays.']
  company_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the company code. Active indicates the entity is operational and posting transactions. Inactive, dormant, liquidation, or merged indicate non-operational states.']
  consolidation_entity_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator specifying whether this company code is included in group-level financial consolidation. True indicates the entity participates in consolidated financial statements.']
  consolidation_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the consolidation group or reporting entity to which this company code belongs. Used for multi-level consolidation hierarchies and segment reporting.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the Controlling (CO) area to which this company code is assigned. The controlling area is the organizational unit for cost accounting, profitability analysis, and internal management reporting.']
  cost_of_sales_accounting_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator specifying whether this company code uses cost-of-sales accounting (income statement by function) or period accounting (income statement by nature). True indicates cost-of-sales method.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country of domicile for the legal entity. Determines tax jurisdiction, statutory reporting requirements, and local GAAP applicability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this company code record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_control_area varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the credit control area responsible for managing customer credit limits and credit exposure for this company code.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the local currency of the company code. All financial transactions for this entity are recorded in this currency before consolidation.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date on which the company code was deactivated or ceased operations. Null if the entity is still active.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for official correspondence with the company code.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the company code, if applicable. Used for formal legal and regulatory correspondence.']
  field_status_variant varchar [note: 'Four-character code defining the field status variant that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed during financial document posting for this company code.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'Two-character code defining the fiscal year structure for this company code, including the number of posting periods, special periods for year-end adjustments, and fiscal year start/end dates.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region classification for this company code. Used for regional performance analysis, geographic segment reporting, and market-based strategic decisions.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date on which the legal entity was incorporated or registered with the statutory authority. Used for legal compliance and entity lifecycle tracking.']
  intercompany_clearing_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator specifying whether this company code participates in automated intercompany clearing and reconciliation processes. True indicates intercompany transactions are tracked and cleared.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the primary language used for financial reporting and communication for this company code.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person who last modified this company code record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this company code record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the company code headquarters or registered office.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the registered legal address for the company code.']
  posting_period_variant varchar [note: 'Four-character code defining the posting period variant that controls which posting periods are open or closed for transaction posting in this company code.']
  profit_center_standard_hierarchy varchar [note: 'Code identifying the standard profit center hierarchy used for profitability analysis and internal management reporting for this company code.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the registered legal address for the company code.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary tax jurisdiction applicable to this company code. Determines tax calculation rules, tax reporting requirements, and compliance with local tax authorities.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the company code location. Used for timestamp normalization and cross-entity transaction reconciliation.']
  vat_registration_number varchar [note: 'VAT or sales tax registration number assigned by the local tax authority. Required for VAT reporting and cross-border transactions within VAT jurisdictions.']

  Note: 'Master record for each legal entity (company code) within the apparel fashion group as defined in SAP S/4HANA FI. Captures company code, company name, country, currency, chart of accounts, fiscal year variant, controlling area, tax jurisdiction, and IFRS/local GAAP accounting principle. Serves as the organizational anchor for statutory reporting, financial consolidation, and intercompany accounting.'
}

Table finance.intercompany_transaction {
  intercompany_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the intercompany transaction record. Primary key for the intercompany transaction entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Intercompany transactions can be allocated to cost centers for cost tracking. Normalizing STRING cost_center_code to FK cost_center_id → cost_center.cost_center_id. Remove redundant cost_center_code S']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Intercompany transactions post to GL accounts for financial reporting. Normalizing STRING gl_account_code to FK gl_account_id → gl_account.gl_account_id. Remove redundant gl_account_code STRING column']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Intercompany transactions generate journal entries in SAP S/4HANA FI for both the sending and receiving entities. intercompany_transaction has reference_document_number as a STRING reference but no st']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Intercompany transactions are posted to specific accounting ledgers in SAP S/4HANA parallel accounting. intercompany_transaction has fiscal_period and fiscal_year but no ledger_id FK. Adding ledger_id']
  sending_company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Intercompany transactions track sending legal entity for intercompany reconciliation. Normalizing STRING sending_company_code to FK sending_company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id (first FK to ']
  sending_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Intercompany transactions track sending profit center for segment reporting. Normalizing STRING sending_profit_center_code to FK sending_profit_center_id → profit_center.profit_center_id (first FK to ']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'The brand code associated with the intercompany transaction, particularly relevant for royalty and license fee transactions. Used for brand-level financial analysis.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'The sales or distribution channel associated with the intercompany transaction. Wholesale for B2B distribution; retail for owned stores; ecommerce for digital; franchise for licensed operations; outlet for clearance channels.']
  consolidation_unit_code varchar [note: 'The consolidation unit or reporting entity code used in the financial consolidation system. Identifies the level at which this transaction is eliminated in the consolidation hierarchy.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who created the intercompany transaction record in the system. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the intercompany transaction record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  elimination_date date [note: 'The date when the intercompany transaction was eliminated during the financial consolidation process. Null if elimination is still pending.']
  elimination_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the intercompany transaction in the consolidation elimination process. Pending indicates awaiting elimination; eliminated means fully removed from consolidated statements; partially eliminated for complex multi-step transactions; exception for transactions requiring manual review.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate used to convert the transaction amount from transaction currency to group currency. Typically the rate effective on the transaction date.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the intercompany transaction is recorded, typically 1-12 for monthly periods or 1-13 including adjustments.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the intercompany transaction is recorded, used for financial reporting and consolidation purposes.']
  geographic_region_code varchar [note: 'The geographic region code associated with the intercompany transaction, used for regional segment reporting and transfer pricing documentation by jurisdiction.']
  group_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the intercompany transaction translated into the group reporting currency using the applicable exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting.']
  group_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the corporate group reporting currency. All intercompany transactions are translated to this currency for consolidation purposes.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who last modified the intercompany transaction record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the intercompany transaction record was last modified or updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  netting_position varchar [note: 'Indicates the settlement position of the intercompany transaction from the group perspective. Payable means the sending entity owes the receiving entity; receivable means the opposite; settled indicates payment completed; offset means netted against other transactions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments providing additional context about the intercompany transaction, including special circumstances, adjustments, or audit findings.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date when the intercompany transaction was posted to the general ledger in the financial system. May differ from transaction date due to period-end processing or adjustments.']
  product_category_code varchar [note: 'The product category or merchandise hierarchy code associated with the intercompany transaction, particularly relevant for goods transfers. Used for category-level profitability analysis.']
  receiving_company_code varchar [note: 'The company code of the legal entity that is the receiver or consumer in the intercompany transaction. Represents the entity being charged or receiving goods/services. Typically a 4-character alphanumeric code per SAP standard.']
  receiving_profit_center_code varchar [note: 'The profit center code within the receiving company that is charged for the intercompany transaction. Used for internal profitability analysis and segment reporting.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date when the intercompany transaction was reconciled and confirmed between the sending and receiving entities. Null if reconciliation is pending or disputed.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'The current reconciliation status between the sending and receiving entities. Matched indicates both sides agree; unmatched for discrepancies; disputed for contested amounts; resolved for cleared disputes.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'The reference number of the source document that triggered the intercompany transaction, such as a purchase order, invoice, or service agreement number. Used for audit trail and reconciliation.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date when the intercompany transaction was settled through cash payment, netting, or other settlement mechanism between the legal entities. Null if settlement is pending.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'The mechanism used to settle the intercompany transaction. Cash payment for direct fund transfer; netting for multilateral settlement; offset against other balances; loan conversion for capitalization; waiver for forgiveness.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'The tax jurisdiction code applicable to the intercompany transaction, used for transfer pricing compliance and tax reporting. Critical for OECD BEPS documentation.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the intercompany transaction in the transaction currency. Represents the gross amount charged or transferred before any adjustments.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the intercompany transaction was originally denominated and executed between the two legal entities.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'The business date when the intercompany transaction occurred or was recognized. This is the accounting effective date used for financial period assignment and consolidation.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique transaction number for the intercompany transaction, typically following format IC followed by 10 digits. Used for external reference and audit trails.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the intercompany transaction indicating the nature of the business activity between legal entities. Goods transfer represents finished goods or materials moved between entities; service charge for shared services; royalty and license fees for intellectual property usage; management fees for corporate overhead; loan and interest for financing arrangements; dividend for profit distribution; allocation for cost sharing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: goods_transfer|service_charge|royalty|license_fee|management_fee|loan|interest|dividend|allocation — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transfer_price_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any adjustment amount applied to the intercompany transaction to align with transfer pricing policy and arms-length principles. Positive values indicate upward adjustments; negative values indicate downward adjustments.']
  transfer_pricing_method varchar [note: 'The OECD-compliant transfer pricing methodology applied to determine the arms-length price for this intercompany transaction. Comparable uncontrolled price uses market benchmarks; resale price based on resale margin; cost plus adds markup to cost; transactional net margin compares net margins; profit split allocates combined profits.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of withholding tax applied to the intercompany transaction, particularly relevant for cross-border royalty, interest, and service fee payments. Recorded in transaction currency.']
  withholding_tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The withholding tax rate percentage applied to the intercompany transaction based on applicable tax treaties and local regulations. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5%).']

  Note: 'Records intercompany financial transactions between legal entities within the apparel fashion group, including intercompany sales of finished goods (e.g., sourcing entity to retail entity), royalty and license fee charges, shared service allocations, management fees, and intercompany loans. Captures sending and receiving company codes, transaction type, amount in transaction and group currency, elimination status, netting position, transfer pricing method, and IFRS 10 consolidation treatment. Essential for financial consolidation, transfer pricing documentation (OECD guidelines), and group statutory reporting.'
}

Table finance.tax_item {
  tax_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tax line item record in the financial system.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Tax line items (VAT, withholding tax, import duties) are generated from AP invoice postings. tax_item currently stores document_number as a STRING reference to the source document, but lacks a structu']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Tax line items (output VAT, sales tax) are generated from AR invoice postings for customer billing. tax_item currently uses document_number as a generic STRING reference. Adding ar_invoice_id enables ']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Tax items are posted to a legal entity (company code). Normalizing STRING company_code to FK company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id. Remove redundant company_code STRING column.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Tax items can be allocated to cost centers for cost tracking. Normalizing STRING cost_center_number to FK cost_center_id → cost_center.cost_center_id. Remove redundant cost_center_number STRING column']
  finance_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_payment. Business justification: Withholding tax items are directly associated with payment transactions in SAP FI. tax_item has withholding_tax_indicator=true for such items. Adding finance_payment_id links withholding tax line item']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Tax items post to GL accounts for financial reporting. Normalizing STRING gl_account_code to FK gl_account_id → gl_account.gl_account_id. Remove redundant gl_account_code STRING column.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Tax line items are posted via journal entries in SAP S/4HANA FI. tax_item has document_number as a STRING reference but no structured FK to journal_entry. Adding journal_entry_id links tax items to th']
  landed_cost_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.landed_cost. Business justification: Customs duties, import VAT, and tariff-related tax items are directly associated with landed cost records for imported apparel goods. tax_item has customs_entry_number, hs_code, and landed_cost_compon']
  original_tax_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original tax item record if this entry is a reversal or adjustment, enabling audit trail and reconciliation.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer associated with this tax item, relevant for sales tax and output tax scenarios.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Tax items can be allocated to profit centers for profitability analysis. Normalizing STRING profit_center_code to FK profit_center_id → profit_center.profit_center_id. Remove redundant profit_center_c']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier associated with this tax item, relevant for withholding tax and input tax scenarios.']
  wholesale_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.wholesale_account. Business justification: Tax items for B2B wholesale transactions need wholesale account reference for VAT exemption certificate validation, resale certificate tracking, cross-border tax compliance (EU intra-community supply,']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the business area or segment (e.g., retail, wholesale, e-commerce) to which this tax item is allocated for internal reporting.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the tax obligation arises (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person or system process that created this tax item record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax item record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the tax amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customs_entry_number varchar [note: 'The customs declaration or entry number associated with import duty tax items, used for customs clearance tracking.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date on the source document (invoice date, receipt date) that triggered this tax obligation.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known financial document number (invoice number, receipt number, or accounting document number) associated with this tax item.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this tax item in the filing and payment lifecycle: pending (not yet filed), filed (submitted to authorities), paid (payment completed), reconciled (confirmed by authorities), or disputed (under review or appeal).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period number within the fiscal year (1-12 for monthly, 1-4 for quarterly) when this tax item is recorded.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this tax item is recorded for financial and statutory reporting purposes.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'The Harmonized System code used for customs classification of goods, relevant for import duty and customs tax calculations.']
  landed_cost_component varchar [note: 'Identifies which component of landed cost this tax item represents: duty (import tax), freight (shipping tax), insurance (insurance premium tax), or handling (processing fees).']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person or system process that last modified this tax item record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax item record was last updated or modified.']
  line_item_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the parent financial document, used for ordering and referencing individual tax entries.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, explanations, or special instructions related to this tax item.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date when the tax amount was paid to the tax authority, if applicable.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date when this tax item was posted to the general ledger for accounting purposes.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this tax item is a reversal or correction of a previously posted tax entry.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated tax amount for this line item, derived from taxable base amount multiplied by tax rate.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax code identifier that determines the tax type, rate, and calculation method applied to this transaction line (e.g., V1 for standard VAT, I0 for import duty).']
  tax_determination_date date [note: 'The date used to determine the applicable tax rate and rules, typically the transaction date or delivery date.']
  tax_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this is input tax (tax paid on purchases, recoverable) or output tax (tax collected on sales, payable to authorities).']
  tax_exemption_certificate_number varchar [note: 'The certificate or authorization number supporting the tax exemption claim, required for audit and compliance verification.']
  tax_exemption_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for tax exemption or reduced rate, if applicable (e.g., export exemption, diplomatic exemption, zero-rated goods).']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the tax authority or jurisdiction where this tax is applicable (e.g., country code, state code, or customs territory).']
  tax_jurisdiction_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the tax jurisdiction or authority (e.g., United States - California, European Union, HM Revenue & Customs UK).']
  tax_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage rate applied to calculate the tax amount (e.g., 20.00 for 20% VAT, 5.50 for 5.5% import duty).']
  tax_reporting_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period or reporting period to which this tax item belongs (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-03, 2024-FY).']
  tax_return_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the tax return filing in which this tax item is included for statutory reporting to tax authorities.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'The category of tax applied: VAT (Value Added Tax), sales tax, import duty, excise duty, withholding tax, or GST (Goods and Services Tax).']
  taxable_base_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The base amount on which the tax is calculated, before tax is applied. Represents the net value subject to taxation.']
  withholding_tax_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this tax item represents withholding tax deducted at source from vendor payments.']

  Note: 'Captures tax line items associated with financial transactions in SAP S/4HANA FI, including VAT, sales tax, import duty, withholding tax, and GST. Stores tax code, tax type, taxable base amount, tax amount, tax jurisdiction, reporting period, tax return reference, and filing status. Supports indirect tax compliance, statutory tax reporting, and customs duty reconciliation for apparel fashion global operations.'
}

Table finance.bank_account {
  bank_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bank account record. Primary key for the bank account master data entity.']
  bank_account_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Internal account identifier within the house bank structure. Used in combination with house_bank_id to uniquely identify the account in SAP.']
  bank_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Bank accounts are linked to GL accounts for reconciliation and financial reporting. Normalizing STRING gl_account_code to FK gl_account_id → gl_account.gl_account_id. Remove redundant gl_account_code ']
  cash_pool_header_bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on bank_account (cash_pool_header_bank_account_id)']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Bank accounts belong to a legal entity (company code). Normalizing STRING company_code to FK company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id. Remove redundant company_code STRING column.']
  account_closing_date date [note: 'Date when the bank account was officially closed. Null for active accounts.']
  account_holder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the account holder as registered with the bank. Typically matches the company legal entity name.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The actual bank account number as assigned by the financial institution. Format varies by country and banking system.']
  account_opening_date date [note: 'Date when the bank account was officially opened and activated with the financial institution.']
  account_purpose_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the business purpose and usage of this bank account. Provides context for treasury management and audit purposes.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the bank account. Active accounts are in use, inactive are temporarily suspended, closed are permanently terminated, blocked are restricted by bank or company, dormant have no recent activity.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bank account based on its business purpose. Operating accounts handle day-to-day transactions, payroll accounts for employee payments, Letter of Credit (LC) accounts for trade finance, cash pooling for treasury optimization, escrow for held funds, and transit for in-process payments.']
  available_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Available balance after accounting for holds, pending transactions, and reserved funds. Represents actual spendable cash.']
  bank_address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the bank branch physical address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  bank_address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the bank branch physical address (suite, floor, building). Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  bank_branch_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific bank branch where the account is maintained.']
  bank_city varchar [note: 'City where the bank branch is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  bank_key varchar [note: 'Country-specific bank identifier code (e.g., routing number in USA, sort code in UK, bank code in other countries). Used for domestic payment processing.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the financial institution where the account is held.']
  bank_phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the bank branch. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  bank_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the bank branch location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  bank_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the bank branch is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  cash_pooling_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this account participates in a cash pooling arrangement for treasury optimization across multiple entities or accounts.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the bank account is domiciled. Important for regulatory reporting and tax compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank account master record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code representing the primary currency denomination of the bank account.']
  current_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current available balance in the bank account as of the last reconciliation or statement date. Used for cash flow management and liquidity analysis.']
  house_bank_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the house bank in SAP S/4HANA FI. Represents the company\'s banking partner where the account is maintained.']
  iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number used for cross-border payments and international transactions. Standardized format for global banking.']
  interest_rate_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual interest rate percentage applied to the account balance. May be positive for interest-bearing accounts or negative for certain treasury accounts.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank account master record was last updated in the system.']
  last_reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date when the last bank reconciliation was completed for this account. Critical for End of Month (EOM) close and financial reporting accuracy.']
  last_statement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent bank statement received and processed for this account. Used for reconciliation tracking.']
  lc_facility_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this account is designated for Letter of Credit (LC) issuance and settlement. Critical for apparel fashion global sourcing and Free on Board (FOB) payment terms.']
  lc_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum Letter of Credit facility limit available on this account. Used for managing trade finance capacity for international supplier payments.']
  minimum_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum balance requirement set by the bank or company policy to avoid fees or maintain account status.']
  overdraft_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum overdraft or credit facility limit extended by the bank for this account. Zero if no overdraft facility exists.']
  payment_method_supported varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of payment methods supported by this account (e.g., wire transfer, ACH, check, EDI, SWIFT). Used for payment run configuration.']
  relationship_manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the bank relationship manager. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  relationship_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the bank relationship manager or account officer responsible for this account. Key contact for banking services and issue resolution.']
  relationship_manager_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the bank relationship manager. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  swift_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC code identifying the bank and branch for international wire transfers and Letter of Credit (LC) transactions.']

  Note: 'Master record for bank accounts maintained in SAP S/4HANA FI (house banks and account IDs) used for payment processing, cash pooling, and treasury management. Captures bank key, account number, IBAN, SWIFT/BIC, currency, GL reconciliation account, account type (operating, payroll, LC), daily balance, and bank relationship details. Essential for cash flow management, payment run configuration, and Letter of Credit settlement in apparel fashion global operations.'
}

Table finance.letter_of_credit {
  letter_of_credit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the letter of credit record. Primary key.']
  amended_letter_of_credit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on letter_of_credit (amended_letter_of_credit_id)']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Letters of credit are issued by a legal entity (company code). Normalizing STRING company_code to FK company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id. Remove redundant company_code STRING column.']
  issuing_bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Letters of credit are issued through a specific bank account maintained in SAP S/4HANA FI. Currently the issuing bank is captured as denormalized STRING fields (issuing_bank_name, issuing_bank_swift_c']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: In apparel fashion, Letters of Credit are issued specifically against sourcing POs as the primary vendor payment instrument for overseas factory orders. Finance and sourcing teams must reconcile LC ut']
  vendor_bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor_bank_account. Business justification: LC settlement requires identifying the exact vendor bank account designated as beneficiary. In apparel fashion, LC payment processing and bank document preparation depend on linking the LC to the spec']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the supplier master data. Links the LC beneficiary to the internal supplier record for procurement and payment tracking.']
  advising_bank_name varchar [note: 'The name of the bank that advises the letter of credit to the beneficiary, typically located in the beneficiary\'s country.']
  advising_bank_swift_code varchar [note: 'The SWIFT/BIC code of the advising bank for international communication and document handling.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent amendment to the letter of credit terms.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number tracking amendments made to the original letter of credit. Zero indicates the original LC with no amendments.']
  applicant_address varchar [note: 'The full business address of the applicant party requesting the letter of credit.']
  applicant_name varchar [note: 'The name of the party (buyer/importer) who requests the issuing bank to open the letter of credit.']
  available_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining credit amount available for utilization under the letter of credit.']
  beneficiary_address varchar [note: 'The full business address of the beneficiary party entitled to receive payment under the letter of credit.']
  beneficiary_name varchar [note: 'The name of the party (seller/exporter/supplier) in whose favor the letter of credit is issued and who is entitled to draw under it.']
  charges_account varchar [note: 'Indicates which party (applicant or beneficiary) is responsible for paying the letter of credit charges and fees.']
  confirming_bank_name varchar [note: 'The name of the bank that adds its confirmation to the letter of credit, providing additional payment guarantee to the beneficiary.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who created this letter of credit record in the system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this letter of credit record was first created in the system. Record audit trail for data governance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the letter of credit amount.']
  document_requirements varchar [note: 'Detailed description of all documents required to be presented for negotiation under the letter of credit (e.g., commercial invoice, packing list, bill of lading, certificate of origin, inspection certificate).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date when the letter of credit expires and is no longer valid for presentation of documents. Represents the effective-until date of the agreement.']
  goods_description varchar [note: 'Description of the goods or merchandise covered by the letter of credit, including product details, quantities, and specifications.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'The International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery terms, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between buyer and seller. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date when the letter of credit was officially issued by the issuing bank. This is the principal business event timestamp marking when the LC becomes effective.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who last modified this letter of credit record in the system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this letter of credit record was last updated in the system. Record audit trail for data governance.']
  latest_shipment_date date [note: 'The last permissible date for shipment of goods under the terms of the letter of credit.']
  lc_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum monetary value of the letter of credit. This is the principal quantitative fact representing the credit limit available to the beneficiary.']
  lc_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known documentary credit number issued by the bank. This is the business identifier used in trade finance communications and documentation.']
  lc_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the letter of credit in the trade finance workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|advised|confirmed|amended|utilized|expired|closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lc_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the letter of credit based on its terms and conditions. Determines the level of commitment and transferability.']
  partial_shipment_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether partial shipments of goods are permitted under the letter of credit terms.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The payment terms specified in the letter of credit indicating when payment will be made (at sight, deferred, or upon acceptance).']
  port_of_discharge varchar [note: 'The port or place where goods are unloaded at destination under the letter of credit terms.']
  port_of_loading varchar [note: 'The port or place where goods are loaded for shipment under the letter of credit terms.']
  presentation_period_days varchar [note: 'The maximum number of days after shipment date within which documents must be presented to the bank for negotiation.']
  revolving_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the letter of credit automatically renews for additional shipments after each utilization.']
  special_conditions varchar [note: 'Any additional special terms, conditions, or instructions specific to this letter of credit that are not covered by standard fields.']
  tenor_days varchar [note: 'The number of days after sight or shipment date when payment is due under deferred payment or usance terms.']
  tolerance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The permissible percentage variance (plus or minus) allowed in the LC amount and quantity as per UCP 600 Article 30.']
  transferable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the letter of credit can be transferred in whole or in part to one or more second beneficiaries.']
  transshipment_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transshipment (transfer of goods from one vessel to another during transit) is permitted under the letter of credit terms.']
  utilized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative amount that has been drawn or utilized against the letter of credit to date.']

  Note: 'Letter of Credit documentary credit record managing trade finance for international supplier payments including LC type, amount, beneficiary, and document requirements'
}

Ref: finance.bank_account.bank_gl_account_id > finance.gl_account.gl_account_id
Ref: finance.bank_account.cash_pool_header_bank_account_id > finance.bank_account.bank_account_id
Ref: finance.bank_account.company_code_id > finance.company_code.company_code_id
Ref: finance.letter_of_credit.amended_letter_of_credit_id > finance.letter_of_credit.letter_of_credit_id
Ref: finance.letter_of_credit.company_code_id > finance.company_code.company_code_id
Ref: finance.letter_of_credit.issuing_bank_account_id > finance.bank_account.bank_account_id
Ref: finance.letter_of_credit.sourcing_purchase_order_id > sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order.sourcing_purchase_order_id
Ref: finance.letter_of_credit.vendor_bank_account_id > supplier.vendor_bank_account.vendor_bank_account_id
Ref: finance.letter_of_credit.vendor_id > supplier.vendor.vendor_id
